| According to Torah sources, the Hebrew year 6000 marks the latest time for the initiation of the Messianic Age.<br/><br/>משיח=קוראנא =358<br/><br/>There is a 164 year chronological discrepancy between the rabbinic dating for the destruction of the First Temple in and the academic dating<br/><br/>The discrepancy between the traditional and academic dating arises over the date of the destruction of the First Temple arising as a result of Jewish sages missing out the reign lengths of several Persian kings during the Persian Empire's rule over Israel.<br/><br/>Modern scholars tally ten Persian kings whose combined reigns total 208 years. By contrast, the ancient Jewish sages only mention four Persian kings totaling 52 years.<br/><br/>You should know, that the kingdom of Persia ruled 200 years during the time of the Temple and not 52 as Chazal have it. The names of the kings are as follows: Cyrus, his son Cambyses, Darius I, then Xerxes, the son of Darius of whom it is said that he is Achashverus, Artaxerxes I who ruled until 424 BCE, and so on for the kings of Persia until the year 334 BCE when Alexander of Macedon conquered them.]. Ezra's emigrated to the land of Israel in the time of King Artaxerxes which was some 80 years after King Cyrus of Persia's declaration.<br/><br/>This is even more bizarre, the sages say in Tractate Rosh Hashanah 3b that "Cyrus was Darius was Artaxerxes. Cyrus [Koresh] for he was a kosher king, Artaxerxes for the name of his kingdom, and his name was Darius." With this they turned three kings, who reigned over a period of more than 100 years, into one king! Don't wonder about it; in many places Chazal made several people into one. We will bring two examples. "Sheshbatzar is Danniel, and why was he called Sheshbatzar? For he withstood six (shesh) troubles (tzarot)" (Rashi on Ezra 1:8). About this did Ibn Ezra say (Daniel 1:4), "Daniel is not Sheshbatzar, with absolute proof." Similarly, "Keturah is Hagar," and the Rashbam wrote, "According to the plain meaning this is not Hagar" (Genesis 25:1).<br/><br/>The reigns of several Persian kings appear to be missing from the traditional calculations. !<br/><br/>It seems that for some reason 156 year of Persian rule over the Holyland the Sages did not include in the calander ?<br/><br/>The words<br/><br/>"seal the words and close the book"<br/><br/>In the book of Daniel as a positive commandment to obscure the calculations for the Messiah mentioned within, so that the true date of the Messiah's arrival would not be known<br/><br/>Ketubot 111a-b: Oaths That Bind<br/><br/>That the Jews should not return to the Land of Israel be-homah – "like a wall";<br/><br/>That the Jews should not rebel against the nations of the world;<br/><br/>That the nations of the world should not oppress the Jewish people overmuch.<br/><br/>Rashi interprets the first oath to mean that the Jewish people should not return to Israel by force, all together (in fact, some manuscripts have ka-homah, which would indicate "all together"). The Maharsha argues that it refers to the actual building of a wall – which would indicate a rebellion against the ruling nation. Such building could not be done without the permission of the nations that ruled over the land, as took place with the return of Ezra and Nehemia at the beginning of the Second Temple era.<br/><br/>Based on his reading of the pesukim in Shir HaShirim, Rabbi Levi believed that each passage implied a double oath. Aside from the three mentioned above, he adds three more:<br/><br/>That the end (ketz) should not be revealed;<br/><br/>That the end should not be pushed off;<br/><br/>That the secret should not be shared with the non-Jews.<br/><br/>Rashi understands that the fifth oath is a warning that sins and transgressions will delay the Messianic end of days, while the Maharsha suggests that it means that the people should not push off the ends of days in their hearts, but that they should believe that the Messiah will come swiftly.<br/><br/>There are also a wide variety of opinions regarding the secret that cannot be shared with non-Jews. While Rabbeinu Tam believes that it is the secret of the Jewish calendar and Rabbi Avraham Ya'akov Neimark in his Eshel Avraham suggests that it refers to the oaths that are under discussion, it may not be connected with a religious issue at all. In a mosaic floor that was found in Ein Gedi, a curse is placed on the person who reveals the secret of the city to non-Jews. It is an apparent reference to a security secret; a way to enter the city.<br/><br/>"They shall be taken to Babylonia and there they shall remain until the day that I recall them, said the Lord" (Jeremiah 27:22). Based on that verse, Rav Yehuda held that since the Babylonian exile was imposed by divine decree, permission to leave Babylonia for Eretz Yisrael could be granted only by God. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira interpret that verse? The Gemara answers that Rabbi Zeira maintains that that verse is written about the Temple service vessels, and it does not refer to the Jewish people, as the previous verse states: "Thus says the Lord of Hosts, the God of Israel, concerning the vessels that remain in the house of the Lord" (Jeremiah 27:21). Consequently, Rabbi Zeira sought to ascend to Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And how does Rav Yehuda respond to this argument? The verse is clearly referring to the Temple vessels, not to the people. The Gemara answers that another verse is written: "I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the hinds of the field, that you not awaken or stir up love, until it please" (Song of Songs 2:7). Rabbi Yehuda derived from here that no act of redemption should be performed until a time arrives when it pleases God to bring about the redemption.<br/><br/>And Rabbi Zeira maintains that the oath mentioned in that verse means that the Jews should not ascend to Eretz Yisrael as a wall, i.e., en masse, whereas individuals may immigrate as they wish. The Gemara asks: And what does Rav Yehuda reply to this? The Gemara answers that this command is derived from another verse in which "I adjure you" (Song of Songs 3:5) is written. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira explain the repetition of this oath in these verses?<br/><br/>The Gemara explains: That verse is necessary for that which was taught by Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, who said: Why are these three oaths (Song of Songs 2:7, 3:5, 8:4) needed? One, so that the Jews should not ascend to Eretz Yisrael as a wall, but little by little. And another one, that the Holy One, Blessed be He, adjured the Jews that they should not rebel against the rule of the nations of the world. And the last one is that the Holy One, Blessed be He, adjured the nations of the world that they should not subjugate the Jews excessively.<br/><br/>And how does Rav Yehuda respond? It is written: "That you not awaken or stir up love" (Song of Songs 2:7), which serves to amplify and include a prohibition against Jews immigrating to Eretz Yisrael. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira explain the extra emphasis of this phrase?<br/><br/>The Gemara explains: He needs this phrase for that which was taught by Rabbi Levi, who said: These six oaths, i.e., the aforementioned three verses containing oaths, each of which contains the phrase "That you not awaken or stir up," why are they necessary? Three are those that we said and explained above. The other three oaths are as follows: That those who know should not reveal the end of days; and that they should not distance the end of days by saying that it is still distant; and that they should not reveal the secret of the Jews to the nations.<br/><br/>The Gemara discusses a phrase in the verse cited above. "I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the hinds of the field" (Song of Songs 2:7). Rabbi Elazar said: The Holy One, Blessed be He, said to the Jewish people: If you fulfill the oath, it is good, and if not, I will abandon your flesh and all will devour you like the gazelles and like the hinds of the field.<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said: Anyone who resides in Eretz Yisrael dwells without transgression, as it is stated: "And the inhabitant shall not say: I am sick; the people that dwell there shall be forgiven their iniquity" (Isaiah 33:24). Rava said to Rav Ashi: We learned this promise with regard to those who suffer from sickness. The phrase "I am sick" indicates that they are the ones who are forgiven their sins.<br/><br/>Rav Anan said: Anyone who is buried in Eretz Yisrael is considered as though he is buried beneath the altar. It is stated here: "An altar of earth [adama] you shall make for Me" (Exodus 20:21), and it is stated there: "For He does avenge the blood of His servants, and renders vengeance to His adversaries, and atones for the land of [admato] His people" (Deuteronomy 32:43). This teaches that one who is buried in the earth of Eretz Yisrael is considered as though he is buried beneath the altar in the Temple.<br/><br/>The Gemara relates: Ulla was accustomed to ascend to Eretz Yisrael from time to time. However, he died outside of Eretz Yisrael. They came and said to Rabbi Elazar that Ulla had passed away. He said: Woe for you Ulla, as through you a verse was fulfilled: "You shall die in an unclean land" (Amos 7:17). They said to him: But his coffin is coming for burial in Eretz Yisrael. He said to them: Even so, one who was absorbed by the soil of Eretz Yisrael while he was yet alive is not similar to one who was absorbed only after death.<br/><br/>The Gemara relates with regard to a certain man from Eretz Yisrael that a yevama, i.e., a woman whose childless husband died and left a surviving brother, happened before him, the surviving brother, for levirate marriage. This yevama was living in the district of Bei Ḥoza'a, far away in southeast Babylonia. The man came before Rabbi Ḥanina and said to him: What is the halakha as to whether I may descend to Babylonia to enter into levirate marriage with this woman?<br/><br/>א"ל אחיו נשא כותית ומת ברוך המקום שהרגו והוא ירד אחריו<br/><br/>Rabbi Ḥanina said to him: His brother married a Samaritan woman [kutit] and died. Rabbi Ḥanina described the man's late brother in these terms because he had left Eretz Yisrael to marry, and for the same reason he called his wife a Samaritan. Blessed be the Omnipresent who killed him. And yet the brother wishes to follow in his footsteps and descend after him? Better that he stay in Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>Rav Yehuda said that Shmuel said: Just as it is prohibited to leave Eretz Yisrael and go to Babylonia, so too, is it prohibited to leave Babylonia for any of the other lands. Rabba and Rav Yosef both say: Even to go from Pumbedita to Bei Kuvei, which is located beyond the border of Babylonia proper, is not permitted. The Gemara relates: A certain man left Pumbedita to live in Bei Kuvei, and Rav Yosef excommunicated him. A certain man left Pumbedita to live in Astonia, which also lay beyond the borders of Babylonia proper, and he died. Abaye said: Had this Torah scholar wanted, he would still be alive, as he could have stayed in Babylonia.<br/><br/>Rabba and Rav Yosef both say: With regard to the worthy of Babylonia, Eretz Yisrael absorbs them; with regard to the worthy of other lands, Babylonia absorbs them. The Gemara asks: With regard to what matter did they issue this statement? If we say that they were referring to matters of lineage, didn't the Master say: Lineage of residents of all lands are muddled compared to that of Eretz Yisrael, and lineage of residents of Eretz Yisrael is muddled compared to that of Babylonia. This means that the lineage of Babylonians was purer than that of the residents of Eretz Yisrael. Rather, they taught this with regard to matters of burial, i.e., the worthy of Babylonia are buried in Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>Rav Yehuda said: With regard to anyone who resides in Babylon, it is as though he is residing in Eretz Yisrael, as it is stated: "Ho Zion, escape, you who dwells with the daughter of Babylon" (Zechariah 2:11). This verse equates the two countries. Abaye said: We have a tradition that Babylonia will not see the pangs of the Messiah, i.e., it will be spared the suffering that will be prevalent at the time of his arrival. Abaye interpreted this statement in reference to the city of Hutzal deVinyamin in Babylonia, and as a result people call it Karna deShizavta, Horn of Salvation, as its residents will not endure the travails of the time of the Messiah.<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said: The dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael will not come alive and be resurrected in the future, as it is stated: "And I will set glory [tzvi] in the land of the living" (Ezekiel 26:20). This teaches that with regard to a land which contains My desire [tzivyoni], its dead will come alive; however, with regard to a land which does not contain My desire, i.e., outside of Eretz Yisrael, its dead will not come alive.<br/><br/>Rabbi Abba bar Memel raised an objection from a different verse: "Your dead shall live; my dead bodies shall arise" (Isaiah 26:19). What, is it not the case that the phrase "Your dead shall live" is referring to the dead of Eretz Yisrael, whereas the subsequent phrase "My dead bodies shall arise" is referring to the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael? And if so, what is the meaning of the verse "And I will set glory [tzvi] in the land of the living"? This verse is written with regard to Nebuchadnezzar, as the Merciful One states: I will bring upon you a king who is as swift as a deer [tzvi].<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said to Rabbi Abba bar Memel: My teacher, I teach it from a different verse, as it is stated: "He gives breath to the people upon it, and spirit to they who walk there" (Isaiah 42:5). This indicates that the future resurrection is specifically for those who dwell in Eretz Yisrael. Rabbi Abba retorted: But isn't it written: "My dead bodies shall arise"? How do you interpret this verse? Rabbi Elazar replied: That verse is not referring to those living outside Eretz Yisrael; rather, it is written with regard to stillborns, as they too will merit resurrection.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And what does Rabbi Abba bar Memel do with this verse "He gives breath to the people upon it"? The Gemara answers: He requires that verse for that which was taught by Rabbi Abbahu. As Rabbi Abbahu said: Even a Canaanite maidservant in Eretz Yisrael is assured a place in the World-to-Come. It is written here: "To the people [la'am] upon it," and it is written there: "Abide you here with [im] the donkey" (Genesis 22:5). This verse in Genesis is traditionally interpreted to mean: A people [am] that is similar to a donkey, from which it may be inferred that even the members of this people merit a share in the world to come.<br/><br/>With regard to the aforementioned verse "And spirit to they who walk there" (Isaiah 42:5), Rabbi Yirmeya bar Abba said that Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Anyone who walks four cubits in Eretz Yisrael is assured of a place in the World-to-Come.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And according to the opinion of Rabbi Elazar, will the righteous outside of Eretz Yisrael not come alive at the time of the resurrection of the dead? Rabbi Ile'a said: They will be resurrected by means of rolling, i.e., they will roll until they reach Eretz Yisrael, where they will be brought back to life. Rabbi Abba Salla Rava strongly objects to this: Rolling is an ordeal that entails suffering for the righteous. Abaye said: Tunnels are prepared for them in the ground, through which they pass to Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>The verse states that Jacob commanded Joseph: "You shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burying-place" (Genesis 47:30). Karna said: There are inner matters here, i.e., a secret meaning: Our Patriarch Jacob knew that he was completely righteous, and if the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael come alive, why did he trouble his sons to bring him to Eretz Yisrael? The reason is that he was concerned lest he not merit the tunnels.<br/><br/>On a similar note, you say: "And Joseph took an oath of the children of Israel, saying: God will surely remember you, and you shall carry up my bones from here" (Genesis 50:25). Rabbi Ḥanina said: There are inner matters here. Joseph knew concerning himself that he was completely righteous, and if the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael come alive, why did he trouble his brothers to carry his coffin four hundreds parasangs to Eretz Yisrael? The reason is that he was concerned lest he not merit the tunnels.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers sent him a letter to Babylonia from Eretz Yisrael, in which they mentioned this idea that Jacob knew that he was completely righteous, as detailed above. They continued by writing that Ilfa adds matters to this statement: An incident occurred involving one who was suffering through his love for a certain woman he desired to marry, and he sought to descend from Eretz Yisrael. When he heard this idea concerning the tremendous significance of living in Eretz Yisrael, he suffered without leaving the country until the day he died.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers further wrote in their letter: And although you are a great Sage, one who studies by himself is not similar to one who studies from his teacher, and therefore you should come to Eretz Yisrael. And if you say that you do not have a teacher in Eretz Yisrael, in fact you do have a teacher. And who is he? He is Rabbi Yoḥanan.<br/><br/>And if you do not ascend to Eretz Yisrael, be careful in three matters: Do not sit excessively, as sitting is harmful with regard to hemorrhoids; do not stand excessively, as standing is harmful with regard to heart trouble; and do not walk excessively, as walking is harmful with regard to eye problems. Rather, divide your time: One-third for sitting, one-third for standing, and one-third for walking.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers offered him more advice in their letter: With regard to any sitting that is without support, i.e., an object on which to lean, standing is more comfortable than that position. The Gemara asks: Can it enter your mind that standing is better than sitting? Didn't you say that standing is harmful with regard to heart trouble? They remained for eight jubilees (8 x 50 = 400 years), that is to say they were in Jerusalem for 400 years. Exactly 400 years later, in 1917, the Ottoman Turks were conquered by the British. The League of Nations conferred the Mandate for the Holy Land and Jerusalem to the British. Thus, from 1917, under international law, Jerusalem was no-man's land.<br/><br/>Then, when Israel captured Jerusalem in the Six Day War of 1967, exactly one jubilee (50 years) after 1917, Jerusalem reverted to Jewish-Israeli ownership once again. Thereby, according to the prophecies of Judah Ben Samuel, the Messianic End Times began.<br/><br/>Ben Samuel was often called "Light of Israel." Even bishops came to him for advice. If anyone asked him where his wisdom came from he would answer, "The prophet Elijah, who will precede the Messiah, appeared to me and revealed many things to me and emphasized that the precondition for answered prayer is that it is fueled by enthusiasm and joy for the greatness and holiness of God."<br/><br/>The rabbi also prophesied that during the ninth Jubilee Jerusalem would be a "no-man's land." This is exactly what happened from 1917 to 1967, due to the fact that the Holy Land was placed under British Mandate in 1917 by the League of Nations and literally "belonged" to no nation.<br/><br/>Even after Israel's war of independence in 1948-49, Jerusalem was still divided by a strip of land running right through the heart of the city, with Jordan controlling the eastern part of the city and Israel controlling the western part of the city. That strip of land was considered and even called "no-man's land" by both the Israelis and the Jordanians.<br/><br/>It was not until the Six Day War in 1967 when the entire West Bank of the Holy Land was conquered by the Israeli army that the whole city of Jerusalem passed back into the possession of Israel. So once again the prophecy made by the rabbi 750 years previously was fulfilled to the letter.<br/><br/>It certainly would be significant if both 1917 and 1967 were Jubilee years, considering the significance of what happened in Jerusalem on those years. But it gets even more interesting, because Judah Ben Samuel also prophesied that during the 10th Jubilee Jerusalem would be under the control of the Jews and the Messianic "end times" would begin. Ná Wonder air; i go leor áiteanna rinne Chalal roinnt daoine i gceann amháin. Is "Sheshbazar" Daniel, agus cén fáth ar tugadh Sheshbazar air? Chun sé trioblóide (tzarot) a sheasamh (Rashi ar Ezra 1:8). Maidir leis seo rinne Ibn Ezra a rá (Dainéil 1:4), "Ní hamháin gur bréag bréagpha" é, le cruthúnas iomlán. Ar an gcaoi chéanna, scríobh Keturah Hagara, agus an Rashbam, "De réir an léir a chiallaíonn nach bhfuil sé seo Hagar" (Geineasas 25:1).<br/><br/>Is cosúil go bhfuil réimis roinnt ríthe Peirsis in easnamh ó na ríomhanna traidisiúnta.!<br/><br/>Dealraíonn sé, ar chúis éigin 156 bhliain de riail na Peirsis thar an Hollywood Ní raibh na Sácsaí san áireamh sa chrannchur?<br/><br/>Na focail<br/><br/>" Séala na focail agus a dhúnadh an leabhar"<br/><br/>I leabhar Daniel mar aithne dhearfach chun na ríomhanna a luaitear laistigh den Mhisean a cheilt, ionas nach mbeadh an dáta fíor theacht an Meisias ar eolas<br/><br/>Keto 111a-b: Minth sin<br/><br/>Níor chóir do na Giúdaigh filleadh ar an Talamh Iosrael a bheith ina n-OH — "cosúil le balla";<br/><br/>Nach bhfuil na Giúdaigh chóir a rebel i gcoinne na náisiúin an domhain;<br/><br/>Nach bhfuil na náisiúin an domhain ba chóir a chur i gcoinne na daoine Giúdach i bhfad ró.<br/><br/>Déanann Rashi an chéad mhionn a thuiscint nár chóir do na daoine Giúdacha filleadh ar Iosrael trí fhórsa, gach duine le chéile (i ndáiríre, tá ka-OH, a léiríonn "gach duine le chéile"). Áitíonn an Maharasha go dtagraíonn sé d’fhoirgneamh iarbhír bhalla – rud a thabharfadh le fios gur éirí amach in aghaidh an náisiúin rialaithe. Ní fhéadfaí foirgneamh den sórt sin a dhéanamh gan cead ó na náisiúin a bhí i gceannas ar an talamh, mar a tharla le filleadh Ezra agus Nehemia ag tús an Dara Teampall.<br/><br/>Bunaithe ar a léamh ar an lussim ihirhir, chreid Rabbei Levi go intuigthe gach sliocht ina mhionn dúbailte. Seachas na trí cinn a luaitear thuas, cuireann sé trí níos mó:<br/><br/>Níor cheart an deireadh (kez) a nochtadh;<br/><br/>Níor chóir an deireadh a chur leis;<br/><br/>Níor chóir an rún a roinnt leis na Giúdaigh neamh-Ghiúdacha.<br/><br/>Tuigeann Rashi gur rabhadh é an cúigiú mionn go mbeidh peacaí agus transgression moill ar an deireadh na laethanta, agus tugann an Marsha le fios go gciallaíonn sé nár chóir na daoine a bhrú amach ar an foircinn na laethanta ina gcroí, ach gur chóir dóibh a chreidiúint go mbeidh an Meisias teacht go tapa.<br/><br/>Tá réimse leathan tuairimí ann freisin maidir leis an rún nach féidir a roinnt le Giúdaigh neamh-Ghiúdacha. Cé go gcreideann Raibeinu Tam go bhfuil sé mar rún ag an bhféilire Giúdach agus Rabi Avram Ya'akov Neimark ina Úsel Avaram go dtagrÚtagraíonn sé do na daoine atá faoi chaibidil, b'fhéidir nach mbeadh baint aige le saincheist reiligiúnach ar chor ar bith. I urlár mósáic a aimsíodh in Ein Gerdi, cuirtear mallóg ar an duine a nochtann an rún na cathrach le neamh-Giúdach. Is tagairt dealraitheach do rún slándála; bealach chun dul isteach sa chathair.<br/><br/>"Ba Dhearg" agus fanfaidh siad go dtí an lá a thugaim chun cuimhne iad, a dúirt an Tiarna" (Irimia 27:22). Bunaithe ar an véarsa sin, chinn Rav Yehuda nach bhféadfadh an Ghailís bac ar deoraíocht Babylon ach amháin ag Dia a iarraidh: Agus conas a dhéanann Rabbia Zeira léirmhíniú go véarsa? Freagraíonn an Ghairm Ghearma go bhfuil an véarsa scríofa faoi na soithí seirbhíse teampall, agus ní thagraíonn sé do na daoine Giúdachas, mar a deir na véarsaí roimhe seo: " Dá bhrí sin, a bhaineann leis na soithí a fhanann i dteach an Tiarna" (Irimia 27:21). Dá bhrí sin, d'iarr Rabbia Zira a ascend to Erez Yisarel.<br/><br/>Iarrann an Ghairmeoir: Agus conas a thugann Rav Yehuda freagra ar an argóint seo?Tá an véarsa soiléir ag tagairt do na soithí Teampaill, ní do na daoine. Freagraíonn an Ghairm go bhfuil véarsa eile scríofa: "I a leithscéal, a iníonacha O Iarúsailéim, ag na galla agus ag na leideanna den réimse, nach bhfuil tú ag múscailt nó a stir suas grá, go dtí go bhfuil sé d'éirigh as duit" (Sing of Songs 2: 7). Coinín Yehuda tháinig as anseo nach bhfuil aon ghníomh fuascailte a dhéanamh go dtí go dtagann am nuair a thoilíonn sé Dia a thabhairt mar gheall ar an fhuascailt.<br/><br/>Agus tá sé ráite i Rubar i Nairobi gur cheart don mhionn a luaitear sa véarsa sin a bheith ann go dtí Erez Ysal mar bhalla, ie, en masse, ach daoine aonair a inimirce mar is mian leo. Freagraíonn an Ghairm go bhfuil an t-ordú seo bunaithe ar véarsa eile ina bhfuil "I dtreo tú" (Song of Songs 3:5) scríofa: An ordú a dhéanann an leabhar seo á mhíniú: Agus conas a mhíníonn an leabhar coinín Zeira an repertweth de na véarsaí?<br/><br/>Mínítear sa Ghaiméar: Tá an véarsa sin riachtanach don mhéid a mhúin Rabboi, mac Rabbi Ụraina, a dúirt: Cén fáth go bhfuil na trí mhionn seo (Song of Songs 2:7, 3:5, 8: 4) ag teastáil? Ceann amháin, ionas nach mbeidh na Giúdaigh ascend go Erelze mar bhalla, ach beag faoi bheagán. Agus ceann eile, gur é an Ceann Naofa, Beannaithe mar Sé, d’aontaigh na Giúdaigh nár chóir dóibh a rebel a bhaint amach i gcoinne riail na náisiún ar fud an domhain. Agus is é an ceann deireanach ná gur ndearna an t-aon Naofa, Blessed a mheas, ar náisiúin an domhain nár chóir dóibh na Giúdaigh a subjugáil go iomarcach.<br/><br/>Agus conas a freagraíonn Rav Yehuda? Tá sé scríofa: " Nach músclaíonn tú nó a corraigh suas grá" (Song of Songs 2: 7), a fheidhmíonn a amplify agus áirítear toirmeasc i gcoinne Giúdaigh ar inimirce go Ertz Yisrael. Iarrann an Ghairmeoir: Agus conas a mhíníonn an brabi Zeira an bhéim breise ar an abairt seo?<br/><br/>Deir an Ghairm Ghearma: Tá an frása seo ag teastáil uaidh a mhúin Cabili Levi, a dúirt: na sé osa, i.e., na trí véarsa thuasluaite ina bhfuil na daoine a bhfuil an abairt “ Nach dhúisíonn tú, nó a corraigh suas,” cén fáth a bhfuil siad riachtanach? Is iad trí cinn a dúirt muid agus a mhínigh muid thuas. Is iad seo a leanas na trí mhionn eile: Nach chóir go mbeadh daoine a bhfuil aithne acu a nochtadh deireadh na laethanta; agus nach chóir go mbeadh siad ag teacht le deireadh na laethanta ag rá go bhfuil sé i bhfad i gcéin; agus nach chóir dóibh a nochtadh ar an rún na Giúdaigh ar na náisiúin.<br/><br/>Pléann an Ghairm Ghearma frása sa véarsa atá luaite thuas "Déanaim breithniú ar, iníonacha Iarúsailéim, ar a gcuid gnaíle agus ar na rudaí a bhí sa réimse" (Song of Songs 2: 7). Coinín Elsa dúirt: An Ceann Naofa, Beannaithe a bheith air, dúirt sé, leis na daoine Giúdach: Má chomhlíonann tú an mionn, tá sé go maith, agus más rud é nach bhfuil, beidh mé a thréigean do flesh agus beidh gach devour tú cosúil leis na gezzelles agus is maith leis na leideanna an réimse.<br/><br/>Dúirt Rabbiza: duine ar bith a chónaíonn iÉisteachtaí Erretz Yisal gan transgression, mar a luaitear é: "Agus ní bheidh an áitritheoirí a rá: Tá mé tinn; na daoine a dwell ann maithiúnas a n-inquity" (Íseáia 33:24). Dúirt Rava le Rav Ashi: D'fhoghlaim muid an gealltanas seo maidir leo siúd atá ag fulaingt ó bhreoiteacht. Léiríonn an abairt "Tá mé tinn" gurb iad na daoine a bhfuil maithiúnas acu dá bpeacaí.<br/><br/>Dúirt Rav Anan: Meastar go bhfuil duine ar bith a bhfuil curtha in Erez Yisreal mar go bhfuil sé curtha faoi thalamh an altóir. Deirtear anseo: "A altóir an domhain [adama] a dhéanfaidh tú dom" (Eaxodus 20:21, agus tá sé luaite ann: "Do ndéanann sé díoltas a fháil ar an fhuil a chuid seirbhíseach, agus a thugann bhfeice ar a chuid a chuid a chuid adversaries, agus a dhéanann an lua ar an talamh [admato] a mhuintir" (Deotranaimí 32:43). Múineann sé seo go meastar go bhfuil duine a bhfuil sé curtha i saol Ertz Yisreal mar go bhfuil sé curtha faoi bhun an altóir sa Teampaill.<br/><br/>D’éirigh sé go maith leis an Ghemara, i gcomhar le Erelz Dire ó am go chéile. Mar sin féin, fuair sé bás taobh amuigh de Erez Yisareal. Tháinig sé chun bás taobh amuigh de Eritz Yisa ar dtús agus dúirt sé go dtí an Fuadla a bhí imithe ar shiúl. Dúirt sé: Woe for you Ulla, mar trí tú bhí véarsa comhlíonta: "Bialóidh tú bás i dtalamh neamhghlan" (Amos 7:17) Dúirt siad leis: Ach tá a chóifin ag teacht chun adhlactha i Ertz Ysrael. Dúirt sé leo: Ina ainneoin sin, duine a ghlac ithir Ertz Yisrael leis agus nach raibh sé beo fós cosúil le duine a bhí absorbed ach amháin tar éis an bháis.<br/><br/>Baineann an Ghairm le fear áirithe ó Ertz Ysrael gur bhásaigh Yyma, ie, bean a bhfuil a fear gan leanbh gan dídean agus a d’fhág deartháir marthanach, a tharla roimhe sin, don dheartháir atá fós ann, don phósadh is beo. Bhí an Yyma seo ina chónaí i gceantar Bei Ḥza'a, i bhfad ar shiúl i ndeisceart na Bablóine. Tháinig an fear os comhair Rabbi Ḥnaina agus dúirt sé leis: Cad é an halkaha an shliocht Bhalóí mé chun pósadh leis an bhean seo a athbheochan?<br/><br/>ATHSTRUCHTÚRÚ ATHSTRUCHTÚRÚNACH ATHSTRUCHTÚRÚ ATHRIARIALACHAÚNACH URINNE n-imbhríochaNITHE ATHSTRUCHTÚRACH ATHSTRUCHTÚRÚNÚ ATHRIALACHA<br/><br/>Dúirt Rabbäina leis: Phós a dheartháir bean Samarian [kut] agus fuair sé bás. Chuir Rabbi Ụnaina síos deartháir déanach an duine sna téarmaí seo toisc gur fhág sé Ertz Ysrael chun pósadh, agus ar an gcúis chéanna ar a dtugtar sé a bhean chéile Samartan. Beannaigh a bheith ar an Omnitrix a maraíodh é. Agus fós an deartháir mian leis a leanúint ina chuid foots agus shliocht tar éis é? níos fearr go bhfuil sé fanacht i Erez Ysrael.<br/><br/>Dúirt Rav Yehuda go bhfuil Shmuel: Díreach mar tá sé toirmiscthe Erretz Yisreal a fhágáil agus dul go dtí an mBablbia, mar sin freisin, tá cosc air an Bhablúis a fhágáil ar cheann ar bith de na tailte eile. Deir an méid seo: Fiú dul ó Pumbedita go Bei Kuvei, atá suite lasmuigh de theorainn na Bablóilí, nach bhfuil cead ag an Gearmara a bheith ina chónaí i Bei Kuvei, agus chuir Rav Yosef é in iúl. D’fhág fear áirithe Pumbedita chun cónaí san Astonia, a leag amach thar theorainneacha na Bablóine, agus a fuair bás, dúirt Abay: Dá mbeadh an scoláire Torah seo ag iarraidh, bheadh sé beo fós, mar a d’fhéadfadh sé fanacht sa Bhlóineia.<br/><br/>Deir an méid seo: Maidir le saibhreas na Bablóine, glacann Erretz Yisrael leo; maidir le fiú tailte eile, Abshualann an Bhablóin iad. Iarrann an Ghairmchlár na cúiseanna seo: Má deirimid go raibh siad ag tagairt do chúrsaí lineage, níor dúirt an Máistir: Déantar lineage cónaitheoirí na dtailte go léir a mhúdailt i gcomparáid le Ertz Yirasel, agus déantar lineage cónaitheoirí Ertz Yisreal a mhú i gcomparáid le saol na Bablóine. Ciallaíonn sé seo gur ghnách le muintir Ertz Yisrael, mar aon le himeallaíocht na mBablóin, gur mar gheall ar an áit sin a tharla an áit, i.e. gur in Ertz Ysrael a bhí an tuathánach.<br/><br/>Dúirt Rav Yehuda: Maidir le duine ar bith a chónaíonn i mBablóin, tá sé mar a bhfuil cónaí air i Erez Yaisreal, mar a luaitear é: "Ho Zion, éalú, tú a dwells le iníon Babylon" (Zechiah 2:11).Is ionann an véarsa seo agus an dá thír. Dúirt Abaye: Tá traidisiún againn nach bhfeicfidh an Bhablúis pannaí an Mhaois, i.e., beidh sé ag fulaingt a bheidh forleithne tráth a thíola. Rinne Abaye an ráiteas seo a léirmhíniú i tagairt do chathair Hutzal deVinyami sa Bhlón, agus mar thoradh air sin glaonn daoine air Karna deShizvat, Horn of Salvation, toisc nach mairfidh a cónaitheoirí cosáin am an Mhiséil.<br/><br/>Dúirt Rabbiza: Ní bheidh an marbh na tailte taobh amuigh de Ertz Yisal teacht beo agus a thógáil arís sa todhchaí, mar a luaitear é: "Agus beidh mé a leagtar ghlóir [tzvi] i dtír na maireachtála" (Ezekiel 26:20). Múineann sé seo maidir le talamh ina bhfuil mo mhian [zivyoni], beidh a marbh teacht beo; mar sin féin, maidir le talamh nach bhfuil mo mhian, ie, lasmuigh de Ertz Yirasal, ní bheidh a marbh ag teacht beo.<br/><br/>Tógadh an barra Rabb ab Meme agóid ó véarsa éagsúla: "Beidh do marbh beo; beidh mo chomhlachtaí marbh teacht chun cinn" (Íseáia 26:19.) Cad é, nach bhfuil sé an cás go bhfuil an abairt "Beidh do marbh beo" ag tagairt do na marbh Ertz Yisreal, ach tá an abairt ina dhiaidh sin "Mo comhlachtaí marbh" ag teacht chun cinn mar thoradh ar an marbh taobh amuigh de Ertz Yisreal? Agus má tá, cad é an bhrí atá leis an véarsa "Agus beidh mé a leagtar ghlóir [tzvi] i dtír na maireachtála"? Tá an véarsa seo scríofa i dtaca le Núeiséisdezr, mar a deir an Mercful amháin: Beidh mé a thabhairt ar tú a rí atá chomh tapa le fianna [tzvi].<br/><br/>Dúirt Rabbiza go dtí anbab bar Memel: Mo mhúinteoir, múineann mé é ó véarsa éagsúla, mar a deir sé: "Tugann sé anáil do na daoine air, agus spiorad dóibh a shiúlann ansin" (Íseáia 42:5). Léiríonn sé seo go bhfuil an t-aiséirí sa todhchaí go sonrach dóibh siúd atá ag dul i laghad in Ertz Yisal. ar ais sabba: Ach nach bhfuil sé scríofa: "Beidh mo chuid comhlachtaí marbh"? D'fhreagair Rabbiza: Ní thagraíonn an véarsa sin dóibh siúd atá ina gcónaí lasmuigh de Ertz Yisreal; ina ionad sin, tá sé scríofa maidir le leanaí atá fós ann, mar go gcuirfidh siad fiúntas le haiséirí.<br/><br/>Iarrann an Ghairmeoir: Agus cad a dhéanann an brab RabbA Melm leis an véarsa seo "Tugann sé anáil do na daoine air"? Freagra na Gáma: Éilíonn sé go véarsa sin a mhúin Rabbabhu. Mar a dúirt Rabbbahhu: Tá Fiú maidí ceannasaí Caniate i Ertz Yisreal cinnte áit sa Domhan-go-Cheiméad.Tá sé scríofa anseo: "Do na daoine [la'am] ar sé," agus tá sé scríofa ann: "Aibilid tú anseo leis [im] an dokey" (Geineasas 22: 5). Tá an véarsa seo i Genesis léirmhíniú traidisiúnta a chiallaíonn: Tá daoine [am] atá cosúil le dekey, as a d'fhéadfadh sé a bheith inferding go bhfuil fiú na baill de na daoine fiúntais a thagann ar fud an domhain le teacht.<br/><br/>Maidir leis an véarsa thuasluaite "Agus spiorad chun siúl ansin" (Íseáia 42:5) dúirt an Babóid Rabbya go bhfuil Rabbi Yoụan dúirt: Tá duine ar bith a shiúlann ceithre oireann i Ertz Yisreal cinnte de áit sa Domhan-le teacht.<br/><br/>Iarrann an Ghairmeoir: Agus de réir thuairim an coinín Elizabeth Elizabeth, an mbeidh an fhíréanach lasmuigh de Erretz Yisreal beo ag an am aiséirí na marbh? Dúirt Rabbi Ile'a: Beidh siad a thógáil ón mbás ag rollta, ie, beidh siad ag rolladh go dtí go sroicheann siad Erez Ysrael, áit a mbeidh siad ag tabhairt ar ais leis an saol. Deir Abay: Is ordú é an Rolladh a bhaineann le fulaingt do righteous.Úsáidtear tolláin sa talamh, trína dtéann siad go dtí Erez Yisreal.<br/><br/>Deir an véarsa gur thug Jacob ordú do Joseph: "Déanfaidh tú mé an Éigipt agus adhlacadh mé ina n-áit féin" (Genesis 47:30). Dúirt Karna: Tá cúrsaí istigh anseo, i.e., brí rúnda: Bhí a fhios ag ár bPairtéir Jacob go raibh sé go hiomlán righteous, agus má thagann an marbh na tailte taobh amuigh de Ertz Yisal beo, cén fáth go raibh sé deacracht a mhac a thabhairt dó le Ertz Yisreal? Is é an chúis go raibh imní air lest nach bhfuil na tolláin air.<br/><br/>Ar nóta den chineál céanna, deir tú: "Agus thóg Joseph mionn do pháistí Iosrael, ag rá: Is cinnte go gcuimhneoidh Dia ort, agus déanfaidh tú mo chnámha as seo" (Genesis 50:25). Bhí a fhios ag Joseph dó féin go raibh sé go hiomlán righteous, agus má thagann an marbh na dtailte lasmuigh de Ertz Yisrael beo, cén fáth a raibh sé deacracht a deartháireacha a iompar ar a cónif ceithre chéad parasgs chuig Ertz Ysrael? Is é an chúis go raibh imní air lest nach bhfuil na tolláin air.<br/><br/>Chuir deartháireacha Rabba litir chuig Babylon ó Ertz Ysrael, ina luaigh siad an smaoineamh seo go raibh a fhios ag Jacob go raibh sé go hiomlán righteous, mar a shonraítear thuas. Lean siad orthu ag scríobh a chuireann Ilfa le cúrsaí an ráitis seo: Tharla eachtra a raibh baint aige le duine a bhí ag fulaingt trína ghrá do bhean áirithe a raibh sé ag iarraidh pósadh, agus a d’iarr air imeacht ón Erez Yisarel. Nuair a chuala sé an smaoineamh seo maidir leis an tábhacht ollmhór a bhaineann le cónaí in Ertz Ysrael, d'fhulaing sé gan an tír a fhágáil go dtí an lá a fuair sé bás.<br/><br/>Deartháir Rabba scríobh níos mó ina litir: Agus cé gur Saoi mór tú, duine a dhéanann staidéar air féin nach bhfuil cosúil le duine a dhéanann staidéar óna mhúinteoir, agus dá bhrí sin ba chóir duit teacht go Ertz Ysrael. Agus má deir tú nach bhfuil múinteoir i Ertz Ysrael, i ndáiríre tá múinteoir agat. Agus cé é? Tá sé Coinín Yoš�an.<br/><br/>Agus más rud é nach bhfuil tú ag dul suas go Erez Ysreal, a bheith cúramach i dtrí ábhar: Ná suí iomarcach, mar go bhfuil suí díobhálach maidir le hemorrhoids; ná bíodh seasamh iomarcach, mar go bhfuil sé díobhálach do dtrioblóid chroí; agus nach siúl ró-, mar atá díobhálach do na fadhbanna súl. Ina ionad sin, roinn do chuid ama: trian ar suí, trian i gcomhair seasamh, agus trian i gcomhair siúil.<br/><br/>D’oscail bráithre an bhraibe in aice láimhe dó, mar shampla: I dtaca le haon shuí atá gan tacaíocht, i.e., ní atá i bhfad níos compordaí ná an áit sin. Iarrann an Ghemara: An féidir leis d’intinn a chur isteach go bhfuil seasamh níos fearr ná suí? Níor imigh tú ag rá go bhfuil an seasamh díobhálach maidir le trioblóid chroí? Ina ionad sin, maidir le suí<br/><br/>Is féidir a chuid féirircín a ríomh uimhríochta simplí a fheiceáil go bhfuil muid anois i dtréimhse oíche tráthnóna Erev Shabbot. Díreach mar a bhíonn ag gach seachtain tá an méid seo a leanas á ullmhú againn do Shabbos, mar sin tá a Lev Shabbot ar fud an domhain nuair a ullmhaíonn an domhan é féin Shabbot. mar sin tá an domhan seo ann ar feadh sé mhíle bliain, leis an seachtú bliain (ar a dtugtar an seachtú míle míle bliain) is é an Scáth-Chábán an domhain.Le meastachán uimhríochta simplí is féidir a fheiceáil go bhfuil muid anois i tréimhse na tráthnóna Erev Shabbot. Thosaigh an séú lá in 1240 C.E. leis an gcéad 500 bliain a bheith ar an tréimhse ama oíche, ó bhí an Giúdachas roimhe sin ar maidin. An mhaidin dár gcionn cuireadh tús le 1740 c.e. agus meán lae, 1990 C.E. An bhliain tuata 1740 agus ansin faoi chur i láthair táthoise úr comhionannais ann. Freagraíonn 1740 C.E. don bhliain 5500 ó chruthú, is é sin breacadh an lae an lae ar an Léabhaabhaabhag an domhain.Cad é an chiall seo?Tá a fhios againn, ní bheidh Moischa David ag teacht go dtí go bhfuil na tomhais go léir comhionann (Sanedrin 98). Mhínigh an Villana Gaon dó seo a chiallaíonn go mbeidh an comhionannas eacnamaíoch agus sóisialta ann sular cuireadh Moishich ar fáil (féach Koll HaTor pp. 34-35). I bhfocail eile, is réamhriachtanas é comhionannas chun teacht ar Moishach ben David, agus caithfidh sé a bheith ar fud an domhain sula dtagann Moishich ben David. Dá bhrí sin, thug Hashem na líomhaintí seo de na cumhachtaí fuascailte, ina measc saoirse agus comhionannas, ar fud an domhain.Tá sé i gceist go n-úsáidfí iad chun sochaí fhoirfe a dhéanamh mar a léirítear le giorrúchán agus le hacmhainneacht a fhágann bochtaineacht agus easpa dídine. Díreach mar a ní mór dúinn ullmhú le haghaidh Shabbot, agus mar sin deir an Zohar (Parsha Veyera lch. 117) go bhfuil ar fud an domhain, chomh maith ag ullmhú le haghaidh sé Shabbot, atá sa Seachtú Millionaire. Dar le ‘Taggam Yoson’, cuireann Yeshahu in iúl dúinn go mbeimid an-tuarthacht roimh Mhaois a bheidh le feiceáil ina dhiaidh sin ag an gcuid eile den domhan. Más rud é, áfach, nach bhfuil muid ag dul, ansin beidh Moisich le feiceáil ar dtús leis na gentiles, a bheidh in iúl ansin dúinn ar an teacht, mar a deir sé, "Behold, tá do slánú teacht" Sa Talmum tratch Sanhedrin ar leathanach 97a tá sé scríofa go bhfuil "Beidh an domhan ann ar feadh 6000 bliain, agus ansin beidh a scriosadh le 1000 bliain". Tá muid anois sa bhliain Bhíobla 5778, agus níl ach 222 bliain roimh 6000. Tá sé scríofa sa mheánbhrash go leor aiséirí a thugtar ar an Dead.Tabharfaidh anad seo isteach arís ar dtús. Tarlaíonn an chéad cheann de na hachmhainní seo 40 bliain tar éis na "exeles a bheith bailithe". Sna saoistí, déanfar na haigigireachtaí seo ar fad a tharlaíonn thar thréimhse idir 210-214 bliain. Tá a fhios go nochtfar an Meisias roimh na hathnuithe seo agus caithfidh sé seo a bheith go maith roimh 6000 ionas nach féidir leis a nochtadh i bhfad.<br/><br/>Stair, de réir an Talmu, a bheidh ach 6,000 bliain anuas.<br/><br/>De réir an Zohar le 210-£214 bliain d'aois othaithe Dia iad siúd a bhfuil fiúntas acu le linn Thiceas Hamisim an tréimhse ama a fhios mar, "Athcheangal na marbh" ar a dtugtar "Olam Haba, an Domhain le teacht.<br/><br/>De réir an Zohar an tréimhse seo leanann an Ré Messiic. Tosóidh sé sa bhliain 2026 CE, de réir Rebi Yitzchak, agus i 2030 CE, de réir Ribe Yehudah.De réir an dá thuairim beidh sé seo tar éis 40 bliain de nascadh exesic. Seo a dhéanann an tús<br/><br/>den tréimhse ar a dtugtar "Kibbutz Goliot" (an lainceáil ar deoraíocht), de réir Rio Yitzchak, i 1986, agus de réir Rebi Yehudah, 1990 - agus cuid den Ré Messianic.<br/><br/>Is í an cheist, má tá sé seo fíor, ansin cén fáth nach bhfuil stair breathnú agus gníomhú ar an mbealach seo cheana féin?<br/><br/>Is í an fhírinne, d’iarr Moshe Rabbinus ar Dhia an cheist chéanna ina chuid ama. Tar éis éileamh scaoileadh na ndaoine Giúdach agus ba chúis leis an sclábhaíocht a fháil níos measa agus ní níos fearr, thóg sé an cheist suas le Dia:<br/><br/>"Dia! Cén fáth ar chuir tú ár n-daoine seo díobháil? Cén fáth ar chuir tú dom?, Ós rud é go bhfuil mé ag teacht chun Pharaoh a labhairt i d'Ainm Tá díobháil déanta aige na daoine seo, agus nach bhfuil Tú a shábháil Do dhaoine."<br/><br/>Ní raibh mórán difriúil ansin inniu. D’fhéach sé go dona san Éigipt freisin, mar sin níor theastaigh ó 80 faoin gcéad de na Giúdaigh san Éigipt dul amach as an talamh naofa le Moses. roghnaigh siad fanacht san Éigipt, go mbeadh siad níos fearr. Ní raibh sin ag obair amach go maith leo siúd 80 faoin gcéad a fuair bás i plague de dorchadais! Tá súil agam go bhfaigheann siad ceart é an uair seo<br/><br/>Roimh an TRÍ!<br/><br/>Múineann an Ramchal dúinn i Muiris HaIkarim go bhfuil Iosrael ainmnithe agus atá beartaithe a cheangal le Dia agus a bheith chorónaithe le holiness. Tiocfaidh am ann nuair a rachaidh The Shechinah (An Láithreacht Dhiaga) i bhfeidhm ar mhuintir na nGiúdach agus beidh sé ag gabháil leo féin. Sin é an rud a thagraíonn don fháidh Íseáia nuair a deir sé, "Agus beidh siad ag rá an lá sin, "Behold, is é seo ár n-Dia a raibh súil againn leis!.... Lig dúinn lúcháir agus bí sásta lena sheachadadh!" (Íseáia 25:9). Tosaíonn sé leis an Ré Messic, agus ina dhiaidh sin hAthras na Martála Dead, a thosaíonn an Domhain a Come Beidh a bhaint amach go léir an Ramchal cinntí dúinn, éigin roimh an mbliain 6,000 beidh sé seo go léir unfold cinnte. Ciallaíonn sé seo roinnt laistigh de 22 bliana amach romhainn!Déanfar an domhan a chlaochlú ansin mar a bhí sé ráite thuas, isteach i ndáiríre nua.Sa réaltacht nua beidh an tuillte i láithreacht Dé le háthas glan nach bhfuil focail le cur síos a dhéanamh anois!. Seo é an chaoi a mbeidh sé teacht go léir chun pas a fháil: I am a teacht shliocht an Rí David a bheith ar an fíor Moishah (Méisih). Déanann an prophet cur síos ar seo mar, "Beidh A shoot earraigh amach as an gas Jesse, agus beidh twig sprout as a chuid fréamhacha. Agus beidh spiorad an YHV"H ag brath air, spiorad eagna agus tuisceana, spiorad comhairleoireachta agus laochra, spiorad eolais agus eagla YHV "H. Beidh sé beoite ag an eagla YHV"H, agus ní bheidh an radharc a shúile a mheas sé, ná le héisteacht a chluasa a chasfaidh sé. Beidh sé breitheamh an lag go héagórach leis an humble den domhan, beidh sé smite an domhain le slat a bhéal, agus leis an anáil a liopaí beidh sé a chur ar an éagóir le bás.... Beidh an talamh lán d’eolas ar YHV"H de réir mar a chlúdaíonn uisce leaba na farraige. Agus tiocfaidh sé chun pas a fháil an lá sin, go ndéanfaidh fréamhacha na hÍosa, a sheasann mar bhagair do gach uile dhuine, na náisiúin sin a fhiosrú, agus beidh a shíocháin faoi onóir "(Íseáia 11:-4, 9-10).<br/><br/>Rabb Juudah Ben Samuel an 12ú haois a rinne tuar iontach agus ar leith mar gheall ar thodhchaí Iarúsailéim agus Iosrael a tháinig fíor.<br/><br/>Dúirt Judah Ben Samuel, ar a dtugtar Judah sé-Hasid (Judah an Pious), go: "Nuair a bheidh an Ottomans (na Tuirces).<br/><br/>Thug na Tomantaigh an Maamouls, a bhí ag dul a bhí ag éirí in Iarúsailéim ó 1250, taifead orthu i 1517. D'fhan siad ar feadh ocht ngiúl (8 x 50 = 400 bliain), is é sin le rá go raibh siad in Iarúsailéim ar feadh 400 bliain. Go díreach 400 bliain ina dhiaidh sin, in 1917, thug an Bhreatain na Stuirce do na Náisiúin Aontaithe an Mandaireacht don Talamh Naofa agus Iarúsailéim chuig an mBreatain, agus ó 1917, faoin dlí idirnáisiúnta, ní raibh Iarúsailéim ar bith ina thír.<br/><br/>Ansin, nuair a gabhadh Iosrael Iarúsailéim sa Sé Lá Cogaidh na bliana 1967, díreach in aghaidh na bliana (50) tar éis 1917, d'athfhill Iarúsailéim ar an úinéireacht Giúdach-Iosraelach arís. Mar sin, de réir tairngreachtaí Judah Ben Samuel, thosaigh an Messian End Times.<br/><br/>Tugadh "Solas Iosrael" ar Ben Samuel go minic. Tháinig na easpaig leis ar chomhairle. Má d'iarr duine ar bith air nuair a tháinig a eagna uaidh, "An fáidh Élias, a bheidh roimh an Meisias, bhí an chuma air dom agus léirigh sé go leor rudaí domsa agus dúirt sé go bhfuil an réamhchoinníoll do urnaí fhreagraithe go bhfuil sé breoslaithe le díograis agus áthas ar son an mhórtais agus na naofachta Dé."<br/><br/>Thuar an coinín freisin gur "talamh gan fear" a bheadh i gceist le linn na naoú Iarúsailéim. Is é seo go díreach a tharla ó 1917 go 1967, mar gheall ar an bhfíric gur chuir Conradh na Náisiún an Talamh Naofa faoi Mhadaltas na Breataine sa bhliain 1917 agus go litriúil "ba bhain sé le náisiún ar bith".<br/><br/>Fiú amháin tar éis chogadh Iosrael a bheith deighilte in 1948-49, bhí saothar talún fós á rith ag Iarúsailéim le stiall ar dheis trí chroí na cathrach, agus an Iordáin ag rialú an chuid thoir den chathair agus den Iosrael ag rialú an chuid thiar den chathair. Breithníodh an stiall talún sin agus thug na hIosraeilítigh "tír neamh-fearach" air fiú.<br/><br/>Ní raibh sé go dtí an Cogadh Sé Lá i 1967 nuair a ghabh arm Iosrael seilbh Iarúsailéim ar fad de bharr Bhanc Iarthar na Talún. Mar sin, arís bhí an tuar a rinne an coinín 750 bliain roimhe sin comhlíonta leis an litir.<br/><br/>Is cinnte go mbeadh sé suntasach dá mbeadh an blianta beaga 1917 agus 1967 araon i bhfeidhm, agus an tábhacht a bhain leis an méid a tharla in Iarúsailéim ar na blianta sin á meas. Ach faigheann sé níos suimiúla fós, mar gheall ar mhol Juadh Ben Samuel freisin go mbeadh le linn an 10ú Iúil Iarúsailéim a bheith faoi smacht na Giúdaigh agus an "amanna deireadh" messianic. Má tá sé ceart, thosaigh an 10ú Meitheamh 1967 agus cuirfear i gcrích é in 2017<br/><br/>"Cé go bhfuil mé cúramach gan a roinnt ar an mysteries, is dóigh liom go bhfuil an rud éigin Tá mé cead a nochtadh. Ba é seo rud a fuair Rabb Isaac díreach ó na daoine a chuala sé ó bhéal an Villa Gaon, a dúirt, go gairid sula rith sé: Gabriel Moshe Sleptbuch iontas na mic léinn ar Purim nuair a léirigh siad an traidisiún rúnda a fuair siad ó Gaonna i gcomhthéacs na Crime ócáide ag an Rúis, ag cur isteach ar an bpobal idirnáisiúnta inniu." Is am é Purim chun rúin a fhionnadh, agus tá go leor rúin agam nach féidir liom iad a nochtadh," a dúirt na deisceabail, mar a luadh i 'teaghlach', ach in ainneoin an méid a dúirt mé ní ba mhaith liom rúin a nochtadh, ach mar sin ní mór dom a nochtadh ó shin i leith gur nocht mé go raibh coiníní zm zm ag éisteacht ó GraZia" amach, mac léinn ar Raibi Chaim Volohzin zx a bhí ag dul, an rún seo gar dá chuid féin "Rabbi sternbuch". Nuair a chloiseann tú na Rúiseach fuar na cathrach, tá a fhios agat gur thosaigh an t-am fuascailte, agus nuair a chloiseann tú na Rúiseach go cathair Constantinople ( Iostanbúl) ansin chaithim éadaí Sabbat, agus ruathair tú iad mar gheall ar tráth ar bith is é an messiah ag dul chun teacht. " Lean Rabb ar aghaidh a mhíniú go "an tseachtain seo caite chuaigh na Rúiseach an Crimea agus an domhan ar fad raging timpeall an ghairm seo, agus de réir traidisiún ní mór dúinn Villana Gaon Is é seo comhartha soiléir de Mesicsic."
