D'áitigh an Rav go mbeidh whoever a sheoladh an teachtaireacht seo a shábháil ó na fulaingt atá ceaptha a tharlaíonn le teacht Machi agus go bhfuil cibé duine nach ndéanfaidh sé caillteanas suntasach airgid. D’iarr an Rav an teachtaireacht seo a sheoladh chuig 12 duine ar a laghad, líon a fhreagraíonn do líon na treibheanna Iosrael. > >>>Is nóiméad an-fhabhrach anois sa spéir, agus dóibh siúd a d'fhéadfadh a bheith skeptical agus ní chreideann i bhfocail an Rav Ba chóir a fhios go mbeadh an Rav a dhéanamh riamh dearbhú den sórt sin gan chúiseanna tromchúiseacha a dhéanamh amhlaidh toisc go bhfuil sé toirmiscthe a thabhairt súil bhréagach. Ón mBeanstóras Rav Yoel.Go raibh maith agat a chur ar aghaidh go léir do teagmhálacha. Agus anois lig dúinn bogadh thar thalamh aisling, le rud éigin beagán níos tábhachtaí: Just an tseachtain seo caite, rinneadh cinneadh ar an chéad moishach, thuas staighre, an ceann a d'fhéadfadh bás a fháil ag na lámha ar ár naimhde. Tá a fhios aige cé hé féin agus tá sé ag obair i bhfad agus go crua chun é seo a bhaint amach, a phointe tosaigh. In am trátha má fheiceann tú a leithéid de chomh maith agus má chaitheann sé spéaclaí, tá a fhios go maith Béarla a fháil trí leas a bhaint as go maith, tá mikvah ina bhaile, úsáideann ríomhaire dé-mhonataire chun scríobh ar Torah (OH, cad a d'iarr gadoyleem mar sin, rinne sé fiú a sheoladh amach a Torrah tríd an idirlíon – tá tú "reiligiúnach" fainted go fóill? ) ansin ní bheidh ort aisling, ná gadoylem, pret, invented nó ar shlí eile..... Tábhachtach, athcheistiú na himeachtaí tar éis an fuadach na 3 buachaillí. Féach ar do shon féin díreach cad a leideanna (nó b'fhéidir an seans deireanach) atá curtha ar fáil againn, fiú iad siúd atá níos faide ná an coibhéiseach aisling. Lena n-áirítear ar ndóigh, tá na freagraí go léir a rugadh cé nach bhfuil na ceisteanna cuí, sabras míleata áitiúil san áireamh. Bhí iontas ar fiú an práis míleata Iosrael an oiread sin tollán a aimsiú in Iosrael, chomh fairsing agus chomh sofaisticiúil is ea líonra na n-arm, agus na hairm ag an réidh ann. Níos mó ná sin, bhí sé i gceist ag na "comhpháirtithe síochána" agus a gcairde a bhí i bhfeidhm acu ar Iosrael a ionradh le linn na bliana seo amach as gach ceann dá 39 bpoll, lena n-éideadh sceimhlitheoireachta ag caitheamh éide airm Iosrael. Seo go léir tar éis an fuadach ar an 3, agus mar thoradh air sin, d'fhéadfadh an t-am amach romhainn tríd an Liobáin agus Nasrall a bheith deacair go leor go deimhin. Mar sin sular tuairiscíodh Shabat ar an nuacht Eabhrais go bhfuil an rialtas anseo ag tosú ag soláthar cosanta faoi thalamh do na maiseanna a thosaíonn ag úsáid go leor páirceála faoi thalamh mar chosaint. Na cumhachtaí a fhios go bhfuil roic an tSalracha mór go leor, má tá tailte amháin idir 2 foirgnimh árasán, tá sé de chumhacht go leor a thabhairt ar an dá foirgnimh síos. I bhfocail eile, nach bhfuil an tógtha i scáth an tasc a chosaint i gcoinne ionsaithe den sórt sin Nasralla tá ag déanamh torann déanach go bhfuil sé itching agus imníoch a fháil ar an troid lena roic 350,000. San am atá caite bhí sé a kidnap saighdiúirí Iosraelach for forcing Iosrael freagra a thabhairt ar an ciallmhar...<br/><br/>níl an chéad Airteagal eile seo ceangailte go díreach leis an méid atá scríofa thuas<br/><br/>"Cé hé seo go bhfuil ó Edom le baill éadaigh daite de Bazha " (Íseáia 63:1).<br/><br/>Deir an Zohar go gcuireann sé síos ar a bhfuil comhionann le marú an chéad a rugadh san Éigipt. Tiocfaidh Hashem ó Bazhrah díreach cosúil le hóstach an domhain chun cogaidh ó Bazhrah (1ú Teampall). Chruthaigh páistí na hEdeamhain ballaí an Teampaill (an dara) ar róbaí na Fheadóg chun Eomhas a phionósú, agus beidh na sléibhte líonta le mairbh na náisiún. 2 In Pesicta, insítear an véarsa seo dúinn, idir í agus í féin agus a bheith ina contras ríoga ag Edom (na Róimhe), cuirfidh Dia ar ball éadaigh de chóngaraí géin chuig Edom chun a theach a scrios agus a chuid teampall a dhó agus is é pobal Iosrael a thiomáint isteach i deoraíocht na náisiún. Beidh beacha beatha ar chorpa Slaine na náisiún ar sléibhte Iosrael ar feadh dhá mhí dhéag, agus beathafidh na héin ar feadh seacht mbliana.3<br/><br/>"Cé hé seo a thagann ó Edom le baill éadaigh daite de Bazha "<br/><br/>Deir na saoi go dtugann an véarsa seo conclúid chogadh na Gog agus na Maog agus theacht na Míocach.<br/><br/>Mar a tharlaíonn ag an am seo tá beagnach gach ceann de na hIaráice ag glaoch orthu féin IS (an Stát Ioslamach) (NACH IS EILE), ach amháin má thagann Bagdadd de bharr go dtiocfaidh práis míleata Mheiriceá chun báis ag na lámha cathaigh Ioslamach. Agus caillfidh Meiriceá a rialú a n-ús ola dílsithe san Iaráic.<br/><br/>Ní ligfidh na Stáit Aontaithe leis seo, féadfaidh an cás seo an véarsa a agairt," Cé hé seo a thagann ó Edom le baill éadaigh rua de Bazhrah "<br/><br/>Nuair a dhéanaimid seiceáil ar dhátú ar imeachtaí Galut (exeile) de Bavle i gCroneolaíocht Giúdach in aghaidh dátú tuata,<br/><br/>faighimid neamhréiteach buailte<br/><br/>163 bliain (Is muid 163 bliain<br/><br/>níos faide ná an dáta Eabhrais a tugadh:<br/><br/>Scriosadh an 1ú Bit HaMikdash Teampaill<br/><br/>Dating tuata: 3338 (23BCE)<br/><br/>Dating Giúdach: 3175 (86 BCE)<br/><br/>De réir na croineolaíochta ribeach Rabbian Sose b. halafta ina chuid oibre, scrios Seder Olaam Rabbah, Nebuchadnezzar an teampall de Solom sa bhliain 428 BCE in ionad 568 BCE ag créam acadúil an iarthair. Chíris (An Chóiréis) the Great conquered Babylon i 539 BCE agus ag an féilire Giúdach go raibh sé an bhliain 368 BCE. Dhá bhliain ina dhiaidh sin, de réir an coinín, Xeses (aka Ahashverosh / Ahasuerus) a rialaigh thar Persia idir 366 - 352 BCE pósta agus rialaigh le Eisther mar a banríon, cé go dtugann an teicneolaíocht acadúil thiar an réimeas na Xers mar 486-465 BCE.<br/><br/>Dá bhrí sin de réir an dul tuata tá muid níos lú ná 63 bliain ó na 1000 bliain de dhíshuiteáil.Cé acu atá ag tagairt do chineál iomlán difriúil focal, déanfaidh sé an domhan seo pléascadh i gcomparáid leis an méid a bheidh ann ansin. B'fhéidir go raibh na Prophets agus sasings a cheilt ar an dáta fíor mar sin bheadh daoine a dhéanamh aithrí as uacht saor in aisce agus ní toisc go raibh siad ag rith amach as an am!<br/><br/>Is é an Bride Superal an Shechinah agus tá sé ar a dtugtar "Sah" [bliain] Is é atá sa bhliain na Gréine de 365 lá, tá an bhliain Lunar comhdhéanta de 354 lá, an méid céanna leis an luach uimhriúil an focal "híorbónaithe LAB�mhrán" (má táimid cuir muid "bail"). ] Is é an deisiú an Bride osnádúrtha trí 12 aingeal. Is é an Bride Superal Mallut, tá an Bride aon sonas ach amháin nuair Z"A, a fear céile gladdens a chorp i rúnda Yichud [unification], agus a 'repairs' Is é an Tzaddik an ceann a gladens Di. Tá sé Yesod, tríd dó, a chorp agus a aghaidh a dheisiú. Tugtar LAB� ar an Yosod" ATHRABHAÍOCHT [ glan]. Is é an "sadeek" an groom má tá sé absorbent" ATHSTRUCHTÚRÚ� ní bheidh sé a bheith ag obair i gcúrsaí domhanda a ghlacadh air ar shiúl ó gladening di ach beidh sé áitiú i Torah mar sin go gladened di, agus beidh sé saor in aisce ar gach brú ionas go bhfaighidh siad sonas thuas agus thíos, agus dúisigh sonas thuas. Tríd an sonas an Bride thíos beidh sonas leis an Bride thuas. Is iad sin sona ar fud an domhain, agus sásta iad siúd ar fud an domhain le teacht.<br/><br/>Cuirimid mí bhreise le hAarda ar roinnt blianta, is é Adar an mhí dheireanach den bhliain de réir an Chonartha.Tugann an Dia goid mí eile dúinn nuair a bheidh míonna na bliana ag teacht go deireadh. De réir rialacha an fhéilire, agus Dlí Chuige gach lá saoire, lá breithe agus comóradh ar bhásanna a bhíonn ar siúl in Ard Mhacha déantar ceiliúradh orthu i rith bliana léime in Ard Dara 2, ach amháin i cheiliúradh ionsá i gCinn Adar I saol na n-éan agus na mbásanna a rugadh nó a fuair bás le linn Adar I nuair a bhí sé ina bhliain léim. Tá mí bhreise Adhar i mbliain léim lasmuigh dár ngnáth-fráma, atá den chuid is mó sa bhreis ar chaighdeán.Is é an Dia a thugann níos mó ama dúinn, bain úsáid as go ciallmhar é.<br/><br/>Comhroinn é seo:<br/><br/>Mar seo:<br/><br/>Related<br/><br/>Feb.6 tá an domhan ag dul a fháil buailte ag Gamma Ray Burst/Quas de bharr Wolf-Rayet 104... agus ansin nochtfar an Meisias! Tá sé sa tsúil phoiblí, ach beidh sé seo ag teacht deireadh na seachtaine! Tá daoine ag dul a bhaint amach, tá fíor-NÍOCHT faoi aon reiligiún go bhfuil fíor! John 10:34 Íosa dúirt sé, nach bhfuil a fhios agat, tá muid GACH déithe! folks, tá muid anseo le scagadh, ionas gur féidir linn a fháil ar an chumhacht de dhia... a chruthú! Ach ar dtús, ní mór a scriosadh... go dtarlaíonn uacht ar 6 Feabhra, cibé an gcreideann tú dom nó nach bhfuil, nó cibé an bhfuil tú réidh nó nach bhfuil. Chun a bheith "réidh". Tá an spiorad poured amach os cionn talamh, ach fuair an chuid is mó leis an delusion toisc go bhfuil siad ina gcónaí REGIOUS seachas RIGHTS!<br/>Níl sé seo ag éalú, chun go mbeidh sé micreathonnta, a scrios na US... cooked, ón taobh istigh amach, NÓ íonaithe, trí thine!Is é seo an tine ifreann agus ruibh.Tá breithiúnas iomlán, anois tá sé in am a íoc leis an fidilder... ag glanadh suas go léir fiach karmacha!<br/><br/>greannmhar chuir tú síos 'daoine reiligiúnacha' agus ansin tú ag labhairt "reiligiún" dogma de jesus<br/>Dia é! Cas ó olc agus a dhéanamh go maith, agus má tá do tuar bréagach a rinne tú féin i "Bróca bréagach" agus beidh ort a repent a shocrú na damáistí a rinne tú ag rá go raibh bréagach beidh muid a fheiceáil go luath |
| Not sure if it's completely related, but my dad will sometimes talk about the "Yuppie Time" during the 80's, when he tried to get funding for some rather big IT projects. There would be well connected "yuppies" getting funding and loans for just about anything, so they could make short-term investments, and then run away with the profits as quick as they could. Most of these guys weren't interesting in building something lasting, though, so it was important to quickly identify them, and then just turn them down no matter how much funding they promised. Not easy, as these guys would seem pretty flush with cash. Seemingly. But oftentimes it would leave companies, and also employees, ruined after such a run. The hope was to make a lasting impression on the communities they wanted to start up within, by giving people something tangible and safe to build up long-term. However yuppies would offer anything but, so while you could sit in a nice and comfy office on the one day, you'd be out on the street on the next. Think Wolf of Wall Street. Hardly a secure environment to stake your future in. And indeed, eventually it did go south for a lot of folks by the crash of '87, and also the aftermath in '89.<br/><br/>All sorts of these shenanigans continue in various forms. I know of one company that received an absurd valuation and funding amount from a Russian oligarch to attack a very difficult and established industry, then got acquired 2 years later by a different Russian company for a large return. They hadn't built anything, they had leased a large office with open windows on the first floor near mine, and every time I walked by they looked dead inside.<br/><br/>Easy answer - washed a large amount of money for the oligarch after the acquisition (clean transfer from Russian acquirer to oligarch plus its advantageous tax treatment).<br/><br/>I've seen this done with New York City commercial real estate, 'accidentally' sold far-below market rate to a party which flipped the property within 90 days at (many) millions higher valuation. After this transaction occurred, coincidentally an IRS investigation into offshore Cayman accounts was decided to have no merit and dropped.<br/><br/>Of course, all investments these days are a form of money laundering. Most funding is derived from loan credit which has been moved around a network of companies.<br/>The companies on the front line keep funneling the credit to big corporations and then eventually declare bankruptcy, then new companies spawn up to take their place. It's a systematized global money laundering scheme which extends all the way to the Federal Reserve Bank of the United States.<br/><br/>This game involves separating credit from its underlying debt obligation, collecting the credit and then letting another limited liability corporate entity take the fall for not being able to meet the debt obligation. This is basically what Carl Icahn did with Hertz - He made Hertz take huge loans from banks, then Hertz awarded one of Carl's other companies a huge multi-million dollar contract using that loan money, then Hertz declared bankrupcy. Carl gets to keep the money he received from that big contract. Carl gets the credit, Hertz takes the fall. Carl took no risks and added no value.<br/><br/>When you also consider that those initial loans are printed out of thin air by banks, it becomes clear that our economy is founded on legitimized fraud.<br/><br/>We have a system which rewards fraudsters at the expense of honest people.<br/><br/>What is the difference between right and wrong when the law itself encourages wrongdoing? Nobody seems to knows what is the difference between right and wrong anymore. If the law said it was OK to kill people, we would all start killing each other the next day. That's how superficial our morals have become.<br/><br/>The problem with your logic is that Hertz has physical assets and IP (at a minimum the brand) which the creditors will then own. But I agree with you that the rot at the bottom of the system begins with the federal reserve and our endless money printing. In a hard-money system banks actually care about the loans they make.<br/><br/>When companies go bankrupt these days, usually there aren't enough assets left to cover the loss for creditors - Especially tech companies whose main assets are intangible IP which loses all of its value when the company goes down.<br/><br/>Taking money from various Russian and Chinese newly rich crowd usually means dealing with mafia or governments/military (and thugs and government are much closer together in those places than elsewhere).<br/><br/>What worries more are the hidden economies. The traditional trifecta of drugs, arms, human trafficking. Which would probably now include industrial espionage, demographic data, computer security stuff. (Just occurs to me this may be the "Dark Web" category.) The kind of stuff mid-career Seymour Hersh covered.<br/><br/>Agreed. Honestly, I try to not think too hard about all the people who've been killed, punished. It quickly takes me to a very dark place.<br/><br/>Back when I was working on election integrity I fully expected to be arrested, end up in jail and then prison. Maybe a dozen activists before me (nationwide) got pretty roughed up for telling the truth.<br/><br/>What's that? You mean they took gambling addicts and gave them control of humanities efforts at scale? It's all gone wrong? Shocking! Why, a minority of global power brokers have NEVER, in the history of humanity, used their sway over the masses to abuse their trust.<br/><br/>Most financial crimes are still just plain old "paper cons". They lie on the paperwork.<br/><br/>At least in the US, regulatory agencies are so underfunded or had their mission altered such that "paper cons" happen constantly.<br/><br/>Panama Papers, many of Trumps tax dodge schemes (among others, calling him out because of viz, all those guys "do it") etc<br/><br/>All very obvious, well known financial schemes.<br/><br/>Basically the origin of the word privilege comes from Latin "privy lege" ... "privileged by the law".<br/><br/>Humanity is still ruled by ruthless tribalists looking to conquer the globe.<br/><br/>Ain't nothing changed about it except the literal "who", and sound forms and visual glyphs used to describe. We use a lot less Latin and romantic notions of higher powers in the sky, but still kowtow to hierarchy based upon scientific bullshit, convincing people "THESE annotations of the numbers are it, boy howdy! We def got IT right this generation! Sorry about rocket cars, but our finance scheme numbers keep going up so we did it!"<br/><br/>TBH, the satirical tone makes your reply difficult to read and weakens your argument, if any. I'm really not sure what you're position even is - you could have just said, "history is cyclical" or "nothing has changed"<br/><br/>Putting aside the important issues of diversity and inclusiveness, there's some really strong criticism of the business ethics and financial soundness of the VC model.Looking at some prominent examples in the last 1-2 years (Uber, WeWork, Lyft) and the recent batch of S-1 filings, I indeed see a lot of companies still operating at a loss. The analogy to CDOs and finding a bigger sucker starts ringing true. So far, the bigger suckers were Softbank and public markets. It would be interesting to see what happens going forward.<br/><br/>To that point on public markets: who on Earth is buying the equities of a Zynga or a SurveyMonkey? (no offense to any current or former employees)<br/><br/>I get that speculators exist, but I see more and more the public listing of IT startups as "siphoning" passive index funds as part of a necessary diversified portfolio strategy - this is how VCs are exiting. I haven't done the research, but this is merely the extension of principally why VC exists: the statistical inference of putting X% (typically less than 3) into high risk investments from 10 and 11 figure funds.<br/><br/>Zynga made a profit in 2019. They appear to be a sustainable business which can keep operating at roughly break-even levels while hoping for another big breakout hit that makes a lot of money. Not a great investment, but certainly not a money pit.<br/><br/>This interview resonantes with something I have been thinking for a while.<br/><br/>Essentially the whole VC fueled hypescaling in a race to become the next unicorn especially in the "sharing economy" seems like a giant pyramid scheme. Like it's all about scaling so the value increases so that in the next round the VC of that previous round can cash in on the next VCs who hope to do the same thing in the next and so on, with the end goal of finding gullible regular investors once it comes to the IPO. All that without a sustainable business model, look at all the bike hire companies a couple of years ago, the scooter companies now etc.. Even Uber might never be profitable.<br/><br/>All that is done on the back of society leaving burned soil in the wake. And yes it's an instrument for the wealthy, because they have the means to be among the early investors.<br/><br/>I keep coming back to Block Buster. Their model was borrow money to open stores and drive smaller mom and pop rental places out of business. I'm not sure but I think when you add everything up they never made a positive return doing that. They just ended up leaving banks holding the bag when they went bankrupt.<br/><br/>When there's more capital available (and there's been an abundance of it in the last decade plus), the time it takes to make a new market/industry competitive enough to shred margins goes down a by lot. In recent history, this has happened before such companies can float shares in the public equities market. Great examples are ridesharing, online food delivery, and meal kits.<br/><br/>In an environment flush with capital chasing returns, any company without a true monopolistic moat will ultimately be forced to operate at or below cost, yielding no meaningful profit. Many of these tech unicorns thought they would be monopolistic but ended up looking more like airlines.<br/><br/>> It's part of the structural inequality in our society, where we're building businesses that are optimized for their financial return rather than their return to society.<br/><br/>Isn't this the whole problem? If people cared about society more than money it wouldn't be a problem to invest or take investment money, because you'd all be working toward the same goal of maximizing returns to "society". The problem is greed and selfish pursuit of wealth by certain kinds of people who see that as a worthwhile purpose for their life.<br/><br/>A related problem is the compounding nature of wealth. Let's say there are 100 investors with a million dollars each. 90% of the investors pursue socially beneficial projects and make a respectable 10% average annual return. The remaining 10% of investors pursue profit maximizing ventures and make 20% per year. Within about 25 years the profit maximizing investors have more wealth. Within about 50 years the profit maximizing group has 90% of the wealth. Chastising the profit maximizing group doesn't really change anything. Not because they don't deserve to be chastised, but because even if the vast majority of them defect to the social good group it only delays the outcome where the profit maximizers own everything. I think the solution is to incentivize socially valuable businesses and de-incentivize less socially valuable businesses. One example of this would be a carbon tax. Another example would be for Medicare to only pay hospitals that charged the same price to both insurance companies and individuals. There are major concerns about companies gaming these incentives, but companies are taking advantage of the lack of disincentives right now anyways, so I don't think the outcome would be any worse.<br/><br/>Corporations used to have to justify their charter by showing how their enterprise helped society. And charters were temporary. Now corporations have more rights than humans and people who have corporations are nigh untouchable by law for their crimes and civil torts.<br/><br/>There's no justification for this. A sufficiently ethical government could rein in corporations tomorrow.<br/><br/>> If people cared about society more than money it wouldn't be a problem to invest or take investment money, because you'd all be working toward the same goal of maximizing returns to "society".<br/><br/>We have a hard time deciding what is best for "society" and how to achieve it. Using money as votes is working pretty well: people get to choose what they prefer from multiple offers, they clearly say what they care about the most (by spending most of their disposable income on it), which then incentivizes more people to look at solving problems in that area.<br/><br/>Sure, we could have lots of large meetings, or 100 levels of small elections etc pp, but this works pretty efficiently and without too much coordination. Yes, it also creates some issues, but in general I think the issues are by far outweighed by the benefits.<br/><br/>A fish doesn't understand the water it swims in. Have you truly thought about what the "some issues" of money in todays society truly are. What are the true costs? What did we, as a society, lose?<br/><br/>Here's an example. Just a few hundred years ago, many (most?) transactions were not via money, but via person to person interactions. These interactions were the binding connection points in the society. The people you would interact with , depend apon, and be depended apon, etc. These have been ripped away and we've been given a very poor substitute.<br/><br/>It's a drug. A quick fix and a substitute for true wealth. Need a shirt, go buy a shirt with no idea how, where, when, or why it was made and no connection to the ecological damage that it most probably contributed. Acquiring a shirt, back in the day, could involve many local interactions and could help further strengthen your local economy and society.<br/><br/>> These interactions were the binding connection points in the society.<br/><br/>They were also terribly inefficient.<br/><br/>If you had apples, I wanted apples and had chickens but you didn't need chickens, but would love some potatoes, we'd now have to go and find somebody that would complete the chain at the right time, for the right amount. That quickly gets annoying, so we might say "ah, whatever, you're going to need chickens some day in the future, how about I just give you a paper that guarantees to you that I will give you three chickens when you bring back that paper to me?" and that's just fine.<br/><br/>Now, next week you want some honey, and the bee keeper will trade for chickens, but you really don't want to keep running back and forth and transport the chickens around town, so you just give him the note and he will come to me and get the chickens. We've now created a currency, albeit a simple one, backed by chickens. Money is such a currency, it just makes trading more efficient.<br/><br/>I'm all with you that local economies and intricate webs of relationships are important, but I disagree that they're so important that it makes sense to run after a shirt for days. Yes, sure, you'll meet plenty of interesting people, talk to old friends etc etc, but at some point you have to raise your chickens and fix your roof, you can't spend all of your time doing side quests for barter items.<br/><br/>"They" -- we would have to nail down what you are referring to as I doubt that you or I know about all the previous economic systems or if it is even fair to lump them all together.<br/><br/>And we'd have to figure out what "inefficient" means here and if it is even a bad thing. I'd love some "inefficiency" in todays chasing after god money -- clear-cutting old growth forests, fracking massive expanses of untouched land, fast fast fast FAST. Better get out of the way or we'll call in TigerSwan and the like because you're getting in the way of "efficiency".<br/><br/>Your story about the apples is nice, but it is a story that probably has little resemblance to some of these systems. Many of the systems did not operate like the mythical barter system that is told to young aspiring economists. At all.<br/><br/>Tribal gift economies.<br/><br/>Not chasing after shirts for days, but playing your role in the small society and having all of your needs met.<br/><br/>I was using "they" to refer to bartering economies without currency as a value placeholder, where you personally would exchange one item for another.<br/><br/>> And we'd have to figure out what "inefficient" means here and if it is even a bad thing.<br/><br/>That depends on what you want to achieve. Feeding a large population? Advancing human progress? Not working 18 hours a day in back breaking labor? Art? Knowledge? You'll want efficiency.<br/><br/>That's not an argument for unsustainable resource usage as in cutting down forests, fracking etc, it's about using human time efficiently.<br/><br/>> Not chasing after shirts for days, but playing your role in the small society and having all of your needs met.<br/><br/>With "your needs" being very basic because there is practically nothing advanced. I don't think that "back to a simpler life" is going to be the answer. The Amish are doing it (but not really, they do use the surrounding health care system etc), but very few others even try. And those societies that have not yet achieved the comforts of an advanced modern society are usually quite happy to exchange the simpler life for a better, longer one.<br/><br/>Yea, thats what I'm trying to point out. Tribal gift economies were not "barter economies".<br/><br/>Pretty insulting to those cultures to ignore their art and knowledge. Many would argue, me included, that their art was ,in many ways, superior and they possesed knowledge in subjects much deeper and nuaced than our "modern" culture posesses.<br/><br/>Your "18 hours a day back breaking labor" betrays an ignorance of the amount that people in many of those culutures had to work to survive.<br/><br/>If you think having most of the planet staring at flashing lights and working 40+ hours a week (unhappy and uncertain) to further a system that is rapidly destroying everything of true value on this planet, "advancement", then we'll just have agree to disagree.<br/><br/>Money is only a way to measure relative value of one thing vs another to people who have things (or time) want things (or time). Money is the wrong thing to focus on. It's like blaming air for carrying bad words.<br/><br/>But "acquiring money" from others means, if you follow the idea of money as a placeholder for value, "producing value" for others. That's absolutely what we want.<br/><br/>That is: if you care about society, you will want to produce something of value for your fellow beings in that society. Them giving you money is them saying "I like what you do", essentially.<br/><br/>Of course it's not that simple, and of course every system gets gamed and you have irrational actors everywhere and customers get lured with false promises etc, but does it matter at the scale of the economy itself?<br/><br/>For example, if you look at the US with a GDP of 20 trillion dollars and you believe that celebrities getting huge amounts of money is stupid and they don't actually add value ... How much do they "misdirect", 10bn per year? That seems like a small price to pay compared to the big picture. Like taking a life-saving medication but having to deal with a rash or occasional constipation as a side effect, not using the medication because of this undesired side-effect seems foolish.<br/><br/>Merely providing value to others is not necessarily making society better. That's the point. Otherwise we'd celebrate slavery as selling one person to another is just telling them "I like what you do" and providing value. So there is clearly a whole different set of criteria where it's possible to take a moral stance and not provide value in terms of acquiring money but sacrifice potential profit for social good. That this is hard for people to understand sometimes is why they get described as morally bankrupt.<br/><br/>They may well be worse than celebrities (though I don't see the large value generated there either, tbh; they are getting paid for advertising effectiveness), but even those are a small fraction of the economy.<br/><br/>The old doing things for money is inherently evil philosophy coming out again?<br/><br/>If everybody could just live in love and harmony and to work towards a common goal.<br/><br/>> The problem is greed and selfish pursuit of wealth by certain kinds of people who see that as a worthwhile purpose for their life.<br/><br/>No. The problem is not pursuit of wealth but when that pursuit leads to breaking of the law or focus on changing political outcomes.<br/><br/>To be successful society needs to have good laws and enforcement and a stable political system.<br/><br/>The problem is when those things are not correct, not 'greed and selfishness'. Trying to change human greed and selfishness or setting changing that as a necessary goal to improve the world will always fail.<br/><br/>In fact people who wanted to earn money for themselves and their families have overall done a huge mount of good in the world.<br/><br/>according to standard economics, and all economists from right to left agree on this: money is the measure of value to society.<br/><br/>(I changed the word "return" to "money" because return essentially means profit and when you make a selling or purchasing decision, you receive or pay the full price, not just the profit so your decision is a money decision, not a return decision.)<br/><br/>That doesn't mean the rest of the argument he's making (and you are believing) fails, it means you are getting confused by stuff that economists clarified a long long time ago.<br/><br/>There are many effects that distort people's decisions such that the wrong people benefit vs the wrong people who pay, but try to fix that and then just never never let it be said that we should not pursue returns, that's pure nonsense.<br/><br/>Money is a measure of value, but the standard for assigning value is mostly emotional. Buy anything at retail, then try to sell it. You'll always take a bath, because the retail (whether a store or Amazon) experience adds value.<br/><br/>The internalization of this leads rich people to conclude they are rich because the provided even more value to the world than they kept to themselves, otherwise they wouldn't have paid them so well. The richer they are the more they think they think they are benefactors and any complaints about how they acquired their wealth is just jealousy or, uh, socialism or something.<br/><br/>Sometimes it is the case that the wealthy have contributed something of such value that the world is better off, sometimes not. A drug company taking a 40 year old out of patent drug that has a stable but fixed market can increase shareholder returns by increasing the price 5-fold if they've cornered the market on this niche drug. Did they really do anything good for society? It sure doesn't seem that way, even if they are "creating value" by jacking up the price.<br/><br/>The kneejerk reaction of many is to say I should start a company to compete with that predatory pricing model. But that is naive because (a) there are lots of moats to cross to get there and (b) even if stuck it out, I know that the predatory company can drop the price in an instant and use their scale and place as the market leader to crush my nascent company. So nobody does it.<br/><br/>If I'm able to force the competition to drop their predatory rates to sane levels, then even if I go out of business, I've still won. The objective was for there to be reasonable rates, not to make money. We saw this with Google Fiber, where the incumbent cable monopolies speeds hadn't kept pace with technology. As soon as ATT started offering fiber, Google Fiber "won", in a sense that consumer gigabit fiber Internet now exists.<br/><br/>some people internalize ideas that lead them to commit suicide, and those type of problems afflict rich and poor people. So, having internalized wrong ideas does not cast doubt on what money is.<br/><br/>rich people can internalize whatever they like, but the money they control allows them to effect more changes to society than the money controlled by people with less and in proportion to the amount of money, whether they are investing for the future or simply consuming today.<br/><br/>it is inescapable what money is measuring, and the rubber meets the road as to how you measure your values in how you allocate your money resources too.<br/><br/>you are putting a lot of adjectives on things like "predatory"; money is neutral in this regard. How much does society value health care vs food? Just how much is spent. I'm not telling you anything about what to think, I'm just telling you what money is.<br/><br/>> The internalization of this leads rich people to conclude they are rich because the provided even more value to the world than they kept to themselves, otherwise they wouldn't have paid them so well.<br/><br/>That's right, and that's a consequence of free markets and the astounding rise in overall standard of living in countries with free markets.<br/><br/>> The richer they are the more they think they think they are benefactors and any complaints about how they acquired their wealth is just jealousy or, uh, socialism or something.<br/><br/>Most of the complaints I see are based on envy.<br/><br/>> cornered the market on this niche drug<br/><br/>Most of that appears to be the result of FDA regulations making it very difficult for competitors.<br/><br/>> according to standard economics, and all economists from right to left agree on this: money is the measure of value to society.<br/><br/>Only for a very strict definition of "value to society" that is a tautological summing of individual relative desire that is measured in dollars. Luckily, we're not restricted to defining value that way when speaking generally.<br/><br/>edit: coincidentally, I was recently reading an article by an ex-scientologist reflecting on the complicated process of indoctrination that keeps them in the church. A large part of it seems to be redefining common words (usually making them more specific, but sometimes intentionally creating definitions of words which explicitly denigrate their common meanings) and justifying this as a technical language (or I guess in Scientology, the "words" of a "tech"?) Next, they teach that deviation from those definitions is the source of all error, and that all doubt can be cured by going between the sentence that has caused you to doubt and the official definitions of the words in that sentence, back and forth, until you understand that the sentence is correct, and that LRH is always correct.<br/><br/>Doing that for long enough could probably convince one that somebody buying horrible stepped on street heroin to keep from being sick is valuable to society. Isn't not being sick nice?<br/><br/>"when I said "money is the measure of value to society" I accompanied it with a quotation that mentioned both "money" and "value to society" and I did so to make the point that OP was failing to make a point.<br/><br/>if you'd like to defend the original quote, go ahead, I'll bite on that, but here you've just changed the context to no profit.<br/><br/>I will cite your statement "money is merely a proxy for utility" because that makes utility a suitable proxy for money, and you agree to that.<br/><br/>To expand on my original point and a statement I made elsewhere, to make it more palatable to people who want to object to it: I'm just talking about definition here; I'm not changing your ideas about greed, or distribution of wealth or sustainable wages. Adopt the definiton I gave, and then say what you want about those other things, and you'll make more sense, or understand why you're failing by your own standards.<br/><br/>I think there are some pretty obvious things. Improvement of the environment, health, safety, educational attainment, military prowess and so on.<br/><br/>As Michael Sandel put it, since the 80s we've moved from a market economy to a market society. Markets aren't used as tools any more to further social ends, they've become ends in themselves. We don't measure success in human flourishing, we measure it in dollars.<br/><br/>Ironically this has made much long-term wealth creation impossible. Many companies in Silicon Valley were sustained by the government pursuing strategic goals, in particular the military.<br/><br/>Industrial ecosystems don't spring out of the ground because of selfishness and greed, they're created by institutions.<br/><br/>Our representatives are theoretically responsible for balancing different social interests, including social welfare and money. I think that is better.<br/><br/>I'd rather fix the thing where elected officials don't seem to do that job rather than just throwing up my hands and saying the market knows best... seems like a pretty extreme position to just say that money and pricing is the only tool in the toolbox.<br/><br/>Um... I agree the word "free" is misleading but once you're in a legal system that condones slavery then of course it would be a free market. I'm having a hard time imagining the slave trade arising without a market much more free than the people forced through it.<br/><br/>Markets are amoral. That's why we shouldn't rely on them to solve social problems.<br/><br/>Couldn't it just be a coincidence that the most recent wealthy societies are the wealthiest societies in history, and they also tend to claim to adhere to "free markets" ideas? Isn't it how the deeply individualist Western democracies work - let people follow their greed and ambition, but surround them with legal fences, milk them for taxes, and use the collected taxes for the common good.<br/><br/>It's like operating a nuclear reactor. Give it too much freedom and it will blow up and poison everything around. Restrain it too much, and it's output will quickly drop.<br/><br/>The current social model isn't perfect, but no better social model has been invented so far.<br/><br/>I would argue that, depending on your value sytem, many better social models have been invented and flourished for thousands of years. They didn't value property or money nor care to commit genocide to further expansion and hence were basically wiped out. Just because the psycopathic murderers won does not make their world view a "better social model".<br/><br/>I would consider the tribal gift economies of many of the American Indian tribes as "better". Moraly, ecologically, and spiritually. Minimal ecological damage and some would argue, me included, that they operated more as stewards improving much of the land they lived on. They cherished and took care of their elders. They worked a hell of a lot less hours than we do.<br/><br/>And here's the kicker, they valued listening, honesty and respect. Can you even imagine living in such a society?<br/><br/>I am reminded of a quote from The Innovator's Solution about how to build a spinoff in a disruptive industry:<br/><br/>Dumb money is impatient for growth and patient for profit. Smart money is impatient for profit and patient for growth.<br/><br/>That book has a lot to say about the dynamics of why this is so. But the lesson is worth thinking about. If you want sustainable returns, focus on being profitable and let growth take care of itself.<br/><br/>However Venture Capital has a fundamental problem that leads to their being "dumb". It is in the interests of the people running the fund to get as much money in the fund as possible (so that they can get the management fees). But then they need to invest too much money in startups. Which forces those startups to search for big enough business opportunities to justify the investment. And if along the way they happen to find a smaller opportunity, they can't pursue it. Therefore they have to look for growth, growth, growth.<br/><br/>Technological change always causes collateral damage, and, having been in the business for 25+ years, I can't say people were previously more tuned into the negative consequences of our creations in 1994. With every passing day, however, our power to quickly and sweepingly change the structure of physical and social reality grows, and too many investors give their ethical and moral obligations no more thought than an eight year-old does while burning ants with a magnifying glass.<br/><br/>There's a place for thinking about the world through a spreadsheet, but before pouring tens of billions of dollars into science experiments on human civilization, as a professional and a citizen and a human, you have an obligation to pause and look around before pulling the trigger on a deal.<br/><br/>Taking an example from some time ago, I believe Groupon was a singularly ill-conceived and pernicious business akin to payday lending. And perhaps there's a place for payday lending (or microfinance) but the ability to wave your hands and exclaim "(fin)tech start-up!" allows you to avoid the reputational damage usually associated with working in such milieus.<br/><br/>The point is not that VCs are a bad funding option for founders and business models (compared to other funding options). The point is that VCs are bad for the world by funding things that do not create long term value, so they're effectively destroying and misallocating wealth.<br/><br/>>>government's backing<br/>>For those of us who aren't Musk, Thiel, or Vitalik, there's only loans and patronage and crowdfunding then :)<br/><br/>The US SBIR program provides up to $1.7MM per project over 2.5 years to small businesses or startups. It's a pain in the ass process that takes forever, but hundreds of start ups get funded this way every year, and it costs literally nothing to apply other than you time.<br/><br/>They were in 1987 when SBIR gave them their first grant [0]. That's sort of the point, parent comment asked for an example of a company that exited with SBIR help, and I gave him a wildly successful one.<br/><br/>The SBIR program is not a loan. It's product development contract program. You tell the program manager what you want to build with their money, they give you the money, you figure out how to build it. If you don't meet delivery expectations, you owe them nothing. If you deliver on the end product, they are likely to become your customer.<br/><br/>If you look through the list of companies with SBIR funding, and what they are working on, you'd be really impressed. A lot of the ventures funded are a lot more high risk than any sane VC would invest in, but that doesn't make them any less cool.<br/><br/>> There are a lot of them in the DC area. You just don't hear much about them.<br/><br/>For those of us entirely and woefully ignorant on where to start, could you provide a few names? I'm entirely apprehensive of this model on principal after having to deal with a project that relied mainly on Departmnt of Ag grants that stifled me in every step despite making them profitable for the first time in its 8 year history. But the truth is that as a former bootstrapping founder I hope funding is actually an issue that UBI can mitigate.<br/><br/>The idea of not having to rely on government programs, VC, or banks but rather a system that relies entirely on private funding from members of your family, friends and community with an incentive structure is something that interests me a great deal.<br/><br/>One of the more creative things I saw when I worked in Kitchens was this model where the Mexican employees would group their paychecks and give it to one member of the group of 5-7 workers/employees and it would allow them to have substantially more money than their single paycheck to take care of things that they would otherwise would need predatory payday loans for or simply have to do without until they could save enough to do so. And this would continue until every member of the group was served and it would start again, its an interesting system but it ultimately relies on Trust that I think could and should be done away with given that the technology exists to do so. That's not to say Trust isn't a valuable commodity, but is entirely unnecessary concern if well designed.<br/><br/>Remittances are another meaningful business model that I think are not only under-served but attract the worst people to them in hopes of keeping people in desperate situations to retain their demographic.<br/><br/>There are some case studies from a government review of the program here [0] if you are interested.<br/><br/>Your skepticism is warranted, especially since the government doesn't make programs like this as accessible as they should to people who aren't in the know/not already mired in government funding through DOD, NSF, etc. The list of massive companies that have taken this funding at an early stage is quite impressive though: Genentech, Illumina, Qualcomm, Biogen, and Symantec for example.<br/><br/>Yeah, that sounds about right from what they'd tell me when I asked. I lived in many rural Ag areas in Europe and in the US and never saw anything like that before. The difference being that in both those regions Ag is heavily subsidized by the State and access to loans are far more accessible.<br/><br/>But one of the cooler things we saw with Bitcoin was microfinancing in Africa [1] specifically towards subsistence (often female) farmers, where small investments (sub $1000) would allow a mother to send her children to school in order to focus and invest in equipment or repairs that would eventually allow the business to grow and become profitable. From what I recall the default rate for that demographic is incredibly low, a guy who was making up-cycled glass bottle homes in Africa in a ted-talk or something similar went through the numbers during his talk and it stuck with me.<br/><br/>During my apprenticeship one of the farmer's son in Germany I briefly worked with when I got there had just gotten back from a year abroad in Africa for his Ag degree and he had talked about how critical it would shift the entire landscape if greater access to loan programs had emerged while he was there, this was during the Banking collapse in Cyprus, mind you.<br/><br/>He had started to make biochar/pyrolysis device from old oil drums he could source locally at scale and had tried to create a credit system to just get them into people's hands; eventually he gave up because while the demand was there, as were the results in his test fields, the money to achieve a success model was not as many simply couldn't risk defaulting on payment.<br/><br/>Also now that you mention it, I had a regular customer who said they were from a car club when I had my car part business during University that had a similar scheme; they would focus and budget for the costs of a member's car, and with the members dues and money they got from various fundraisers and any donations they got that period they pooled all that money and they'd finish a persons car for that term/year.<br/><br/>I was introduced to them by a girl I met when I lived in the dorms one night and we I only ever spoke to them via AIM, they often always bought misc things like headlights, used seats and fenders from me in Fall and Spring. I totally forgot about that until I read that wiki entry.<br/><br/>Human's have a great ability to adapt to a situation and through effective collaboration it reminds you how amazing we can be at overcoming often insurmountable odds and it also shows that ultimately we are all the same regardless of where we're from as the same means and methods are seen and reflect a collectively held value system.<br/><br/>Here in the EU, government funding is available and used by businesses of all sizes, although you do have to navigate the bureaucracy and be able to handle the paperwork. There is even books "The age for exponential" but I hypothesize some may have gotten the exponential curve wrong which has impact for growth. Ie I argue that growth may follow mathematical logistic curves/sigmoid s curves and not necessarily exponential hyper growth. Ie there is not an ifinit number of customers there is natural limits to growth. Nor is there infinite number of energy for example. This put some limit on growth which may shape on logistic curves. Has there been research on which types of exponential curves some startups grow with? This article loads fine in Brave without scripting.<br/><br/>VC is fundamentally a power/money alliance which exists to further the allies' interests. Capitalism ideology says that it turns out that this is fairly optimal for consumers, given competitive forces to keep it checked and balanced. But this balance is only healthy to the extent that the homeostasis can be maintained. Unconstrained growth (prioritized above all else and with disregard for externalities) is less like homeostasis and more like cancer ("Wall Street: As long as someone wants to buy this [collateralized debt obligation], we're good.")<br/><br/>He's not saying venture capital is starting to do more harm, he's addressing where and how venture capital is being expended. He actually has a good point regarding funding of small to medium sized business with strong business models that emphasize being cash flow positive. Venture capital cannot do harm, most people don't know this but small businesses need the protection via social connections that a venture capital provides. If you don't believe that try running an independent small business and see how exhausting it is when you run into problems and don't have the backing of a VC. I grew up with people who did this and they get burned out when they don't have a larger investor who is connected enough to solve what seem like small problems. Everyone thinks they are better off being completely independent as a small business, that is always a bad idea.<br/><br/>There are certain types of businesses that are difficult to grow due to either legal hurdles or local protectionist tactics, I've seen this growing up in different states. If you start a business in the wrong place and don't have the right connections your business is never going to succeed, it doesn't matter what your business model is. In certain states they simply do not want large multi-national corporations, interstate corporations or franchises. If you succeed on a small scale and decide to expand you're going to run into all kinds of problems because people are going to come out of the woodwork trying to slow down your expansion (yes, this is a real thing it caught me off guard when I first found out there are people who do this).<br/><br/>There are also some types of businesses (particularly in the financial industry) that when implemented on a large scale are a nightmare to understand because there are so many rules and regulations. No one has a clue what is happening and it is easier to have hundreds of medium sized businesses with narrow focus that anyone can understand easily. Funding these types of businesses because everyone wants some sort of large IPO just makes the problem worse.<br/><br/>Most VC backed businesses fail, and less obvious, changing an operational model (= company dna) into a VC-optimized one instead of a sustainable revenue optimized one puts a company on that failure path. It is not the capital, but the associated changes for the pursuit of the next round, and dangers of not hitting it. He is pushing back against the hits based business that burns out otherwise good companies, and Bryce is focusing on alternative funding strategies for companies so they can succeed without mega-succeeding.<br/><br/>It's pretty non-obvious. Eg, a founder yesterday in my area cited a ~$50M exit as a success example for what he is doing. Except to O'Reillys point, it may only be a success for a couple of investors, and possibly an income loss for most of the team. They looked good -- raised $2M, $10M, $23M, grew to 50-100 employees, sold for $50M after 5-9yr, helped Global 2000s and govs tackle some hard problems, probably had revenue around $5M+yr, and are now a reasonable part of BigCo's product portfolio for much more $. AND YET... if either the $10M or $23M have a 2x participation, only the VCs really made money (and less than their model needs, so a miss!), and only 0-$5M left to split with the 50+ employees who took years of reduced salary. That's the financial instrument stuff going on. On the leadership side, the hiring was fueled by funding, always more then operating revenue (otherwise no VC needed), so needed the next round or otherwise layoffs and a death spiral. (Only a few % of funded startups can choose to slow growth and switch to sustainable revenue.)<br/><br/>There are reasons to do today's model, but a lot of nuance, and from a creator's / equitibilty perspective, fundamental room for improvement. Bheadh nascadh mhaith "yuppies" ag fáil agus iasachtaí le haghaidh díreach faoi rud ar bith, ionas go bhféadfadh siad a dhéanamh infheistíochtaí gearrthéarmacha, agus ansin rith ar shiúl leis na brabúis chomh tapa agus is féidir leo. Ní raibh an chuid is mó de na guys suimiúil i bhfoirgneamh rud éigin buan, áfach, mar sin bhí sé tábhachtach a aithint go tapa iad, agus ansin iad a chur síos is cuma cé mhéad maoiniú a gheall siad., Ní éasca, mar go mbeadh na guys cosúil flush go leor le airgead tirim. (gan aon chion d'aon fostaithe reatha nó iar-fhostaithe)<br/><br/>Faighim go bhfuil lucht féachana ann, ach feicim níos mó agus níos mó an liosta poiblí de tosaithe TF mar chistí innéacs éighníomhach mar chuid de straitéis punainne éagsúla riachtanach - is é seo an chaoi a bhfuil CVanna ag imeacht. Ní dhearna mé an taighde, ach níl anseo ach síneadh na fáthanna go bhfuil VC ann: an t-inchur staidrimh chun X% (níos lú ná 3) a chur isteach in infheistíochtaí ardriosca ó chistí 10 agus 11 figiúr.<br/><br/>Zynga rinne brabús i 2019. Is cosúil gur gnó inbhuanaithe é sin is féidir a choinneáil ag feidhmiú ag leibhéil thart bhriseadh-fiú agus ag súil le haghaidh bhriseadh mór eile bhuail a dhéanann a lán airgid., Ní infheistíocht iontach, ach ní cinnte poll airgid.<br/><br/>Tá an t-agallamh seo ag brath ar rud éigin a bhí mé ag smaoineamh ar feadh tamaill.<br/><br/>Go bunúsach is cosúil go bhfuil an heivalical VC ar fad a bhreoslaítear i rás a bheith ar an gcéad aonbhrainse eile go háirithe sa "comhgheilleagar a roinnt" cosúil le scéim pirimid ollmhór. Cosúil tá sé ar fad faoi scálaithe ionas gur féidir leis an luach sa chéad bhabhta eile den bhabhta sin roimhe seo airgead a dhéanamh ar na VC a bhfuil súil acu a dhéanamh ar an rud céanna sa chéad cheann eile agus mar sin de, leis an sprioc deireadh a aimsiú infheisteoirí rialta gullible uair a thagann sé chun an IPO. Gach sin gan múnla gnó inbhuanaithe, breathnú ar na cuideachtaí rothar ar cíos cúpla bliain ó shin, na cuideachtaí scútar anois etc Fiú Uber d'fhéadfadh a bheith brabúsach riamh.<br/><br/>Gach a dhéantar ar chúl na sochaí ag fágáil ithir dóite sa dúisigh., Agus tá sé ionstraim le haghaidh an saibhir, toisc go bhfuil siad an acmhainn a bheith i measc na n-infheisteoirí luath.<br/><br/>Coinnigh mé ag teacht ar ais go dtí Bloc Busta. Bhí a samhail ar iasacht airgead chun siopaí níos lú mamaí agus áiteanna ar cíos pop a oscailt as gnó., Níl mé cinnte ach is dóigh liom nuair a chuireann tú gach rud suas riamh a rinne siad ar ais dearfach ag déanamh sin. Níor chríochnaigh siad ach ag fágáil bainc a raibh an mála acu nuair a chuaigh siad féimheach.<br/><br/>Nuair a bhíonn níos mó caipitil ar fáil (agus tá raidhse ann le deich mbliana anuas móide), an t-am a thógann sé chun margadh nua / tionscal a dhéanamh iomaíoch go leor chun imeallanna a shleamhnú síos go mór. Le stair le déanaí, tharla sé seo sular féidir le cuideachtaí den sórt sin scaireanna a snámh sa mhargadh cothromais phoiblí.Tá samplaí iontacha ag comhroinnt, ag seachadadh bia ar líne agus ag feisteáin bhéile.<br/><br/>I dtimpeallacht flush le tuairisceáin chasing caipitil, beidh aon chuideachta gan móna monopoliistic fíor éigean a oibriú ar deireadh thiar ar chostas nó faoina bhun, ag teacht amach gan aon bhrabús bríoch. Shíl go leor de na hionróirí teicneolaíochta seo go mbeadh siad monaplóideach ach go raibh siad ag breathnú níos cosúla le haerlínte.<br/><br/>> Tá sé mar chuid den éagothroime struchtúrach inár sochaí, áit a bhfuilimid ag tógáil gnólachtaí a optamaithe lena dtuairisceán airgeadais seachas a bhfilleadh ar an tsochaí.<br/><br/>Mura bhfuil an fhadhb iomlán seo?Mura mbeadh daoine i gceannas ar an tsochaí níos mó ná airgead, ní bheadh sé ina fhadhb airgead infheistíochta a infheistiú nó a ghlacadh, mar ba mhaith leat go léir a bheith ag obair i dtreo an sprioc chéanna torthaí a uasmhéadú chun "cumann". Tá an fhadhb seo saint agus tóir ar shaibhreas ag cineálacha áirithe daoine a fheiceann mar chuspóir fiúntach dá saol.<br/><br/>Is fadhb comhdhúile den saibhreas í fadhb ghaolmhar.Ligeann le rá go bhfuil 100 infheisteoir ann le milliún dollar gach. 90% de na hinfheisteoirí a shaothrú go sóisialta tairbheach agus toradh bliantúil 10 % a dhéanamh. Leanann an 10% d'infheisteoirí atá fágtha ag dul ar fhiontair uasmhéadú brabúis agus déanann siad 20% in aghaidh na bliana. Laistigh de 25 bliana tá níos mó saibhris ag na hinfheisteoirí is mó a mhéadaíonn brabús. Laistigh de thart ar 50 bliain tá 90% den saibhreas ag an ngrúpa optamaithe brabúis. Ní hionann Casting an grúpa uasmhéadú brabúis agus rud ar bith. Ní toisc nach fiú iad a bheith chastaithe, ach mar gheall ar fiú má dhiúltaíonn formhór mór na n-easnamh iontu don ghrúpa maith shóisialta ní mhoillíonn sé ach an toradh ina ndéanann an brabús gach rud féin. Sílim gur é an réiteach ná gnólachtaí luachmhara sóisialta a dhreasú agus nach mbeadh chomh luachmhar le dí-shaincheapachán sóisialta. Sampla amháin de seo a bheadh ina cháin charbóin. Sampla eile a bheadh ann do Mhaidre seachas d’ospidéil a ghearrann an praghas céanna ar chuideachtaí árachais agus ar dhaoine aonair araon. Tá imní mhór ann faoi chuideachtaí atá ag cearrbhachas na dreasachtaí seo, ach tá cuideachtaí ag baint leasa as an easpa díghalráin anois is cuma ar aon nós, mar sin ní dóigh liom go mbeadh an toradh níos measa.<br/><br/>Ba cheart do chorparáidí a bhí in úsáid a bheith de cheart acu a gcairt a léiriú trí a gcuid fiontar a léiriú agus a gcuid cairteacha a chur ar fáil go sealadach. Anois tá níos mó cearta ag corparáidí ná daoine agus daoine a bhfuil corparáidí acu neamh-inmhianaithe ag dlí dá gcoireanna agus dá dtortaí sibhialta.<br/><br/>Níl aon údar leis seo. D'fhéadfadh rialtas sách eiticiúil a bheith ag fanacht i gcorparáidí amárach.<br/><br/>> Más rud é daoine a bhí faoi chúram an tsochaí níos mó ná airgead nach mbeadh sé ina fhadhb a infheistiú nó a ghlacadh airgead, mar go mbeadh tú ag obair go léir i dtreo an sprioc chéanna torthaí a uasmhéadú chun "cumann".<br/><br/>Tá am crua againn ag cinneadh a dhéanamh cad is fearr don "cumann" agus conas é a bhaint amach. Ag baint úsáide as airgead mar tá vótaí ag obair go maith go leor: daoine a fháil a roghnú cad is fearr leo ó iliomad tairiscintí, deir siad go soiléir cad a thugann siad aire don chuid is mó (trí chaiteachas a n-ioncam indiúscartha air), a spreagann ansin níos mó daoine chun breathnú ar fhadhbanna a réiteach sa réimse sin.<br/><br/>Cinnte, d’fhéadfadh go mbeadh go leor cruinnithe móra againn, nó 100 leibhéal toghchán beag srl pp, ach oibríonn sé seo go héifeachtúil agus gan an iomarca comhordaithe. Sea, cruthaíonn sé roinnt saincheisteanna freisin, ach go ginearálta sílim go bhfuil na saincheisteanna i bhfad níos measa ag na buntáistí.<br/><br/>An dtuigeann iasc an t-uisce a snámhann sé isteach. Ar smaoinigh tú go fírinneach ar cad iad na "roinnt saincheisteanna airgid" sa tsochaí sa lá atá inniu ann.Cad iad na costais fhíor?Cad a bhí againn, mar shochaí, a chailleadh?<br/><br/>Seo sampla. Ní raibh ach cúpla céad bliain ó shin, go leor (an chuid is mó?) idirbhearta trí airgead, ach trí dhuine le hidirghníomhaíochtaí.Ba iad na hidirghníomhaíochtaí seo na pointí nasctha sa tsochaí. Na daoine a mbeadh tú ag idirghníomhú leis, ag brath ar ap, agus a bheith ag brath ar ap, etc Tá siad seo curtha sracadh ar shiúl agus tá muid ag tugadh ionad an-lag.<br/><br/>Is druga mear é. Tá léine de dhíth, téigh léine gan aon smaoineamh conas, cén áit, nó cén fáth gur rinneadh é agus nach raibh aon nasc aige leis an damáiste éiceolaíoch is dócha gur chuir sé leis. D’fhéadfadh go mbeadh go leor idirghníomhaíochtaí áitiúla i gceist le léine a fháil, ar ais sa lá, agus d’fhéadfadh sé cabhrú le do gheilleagar agus don tsochaí áitiúil a neartú tuilleadh.<br/><br/>> Ba iad na hidirghníomhaíochtaí seo na pointí ceangail sa tsochaí.<br/><br/>Bhí siad neamhéifeachtach freisin.<br/><br/>Má bhí úlla agat, theastaigh uaim úlla agus sicíní ach níor theastaigh sicíní uait, ach ba bhreá linn roinnt prátaí, ba mhaith linn anois dul agus duine éigin a fháil a chuirfeadh an slabhra i gcrích ag an am ceart, don mhéid ceart. Go bhfaigheann annoying go tapa, mar sin d'fhéadfadh muid a rá "ah, is cuma cad, tá tú ag dul a bheith ag teastáil sicíní roinnt lá sa todhchaí, conas mar gheall ar mé a thabhairt duit ach páipéar a ráthaíonn duit go mbeidh mé a thabhairt duit trí sicíní nuair a thugann tú ar ais go páipéar dom?" agus sin díreach fíneáil.<br/><br/>Anois, an tseachtain seo chugainn is mian leat roinnt mil, agus beidh an coimeádaí beach trádáil le haghaidh sicíní, ach ní gá duit i ndáiríre a choinneáil ag rith ar ais agus amach agus na sicíní iompar timpeall an bhaile, mar sin leat a thabhairt ach dó an nóta agus beidh sé ag teacht chugam agus a fháil ar an sicíní. Tá airgeadra cruthaithe againn anois, cé go bhfuil sé simplí, ag tacú le sicíní.Is airgeadra den sórt sin é airgead, ní dhéanann sé ach trádáil níos éifeachtaí.<br/><br/>Tá mé go léir in éineacht leat go bhfuil geilleagair áitiúla agus láithreáin ghréasáin intricate caidrimh tábhachtach, ach ní aontaím go bhfuil siad chomh tábhachtach sin go ndéanann sé ciall a reáchtáil tar éis léine do laethanta. Sea, cinnte, beidh tú ag freastal ar neart daoine suimiúla, labhairt le cairde d'aois srl, ach ag pointe éigin caithfidh tú a ardú do sicíní agus do díon a shocrú, ní féidir leat a chaitheamh ar fad de do chuid ama ag déanamh quests taobh le haghaidh míreanna bertier.<br/><br/>"iad" - ba mhaith linn a ingne síos cad tá tú ag tagairt mar amhras liom go bhfuil tú nó a fhios agam faoi na córais eacnamaíocha roimhe seo nó má tá sé fiú cothrom a cnapshuim iad go léir le chéile.<br/><br/>Agus ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cad a chiallaíonn "inf éifeachtach" anseo agus má tá sé fiú an rud dona., Ba mhaith liom grá roinnt "infionchair" sa lá atá inniu chasing tar éis an t-airgead dia - soiléir-fores fás d'aois, fracking ollmhór talún untouched, Fast Fast Fast Fast FastAST. Níos fearr a fháil amach ar an mbealach seo nó beidh muid ag glaoch i TigerSwan agus a leithéidí mar go bhfuil tú ag dul ar an mbealach "éifeachtúlacht".<br/><br/>Tá do scéal faoi na úlla deas, ach is scéal é go bhfuil is dócha nach bhfuil beagán cosúlachta ag cuid de na córais seo.Ní raibh go leor de na córais ag feidhmiú cosúil leis an gcóras ber mythical a dúradh le eacnamaithe óga.<br/><br/>Geilleagar bronntanais treibhe.<br/><br/>Gan chasing tar éis léinte ar feadh laethanta, ach ag imirt do ról sa tsochaí beag agus a bhfuil gach ceann de do chuid riachtanas le chéile.<br/><br/>Bhí mé ag úsáid "iad" chun tagairt a dhéanamh do gheilleagair a mharú gan airgeadra mar shealbhóir ionaid réadmhaoine, áit a mhalartódh tú mír amháin go pearsanta do dhuine eile.<br/><br/>> Agus ba mhaith linn a fháil amach cad a chiallaíonn "neamhéifeacht" anseo agus má tá sé fiú rud dona.<br/><br/>Go mbraitheann sé sin ar cad ba mhaith leat a bhaint amach.Ag cur isteach ar an dul chun cinn mór daonna?Ní obair ar feadh 18 uair sa lá ar ais le haghaidh briseadh saothair? Ealaín?<br/><br/>Ní argóint é sin le haghaidh úsáide neamh-inbhuanaithe mar atá ag gearradh síos foraoisí, fracking srl, baineann sé le húsáid am daonna go héifeachtach.<br/><br/>> Gan chasing tar éis léinte ar feadh laethanta, ach ag imirt do ról sa tsochaí beag agus a bhfuil gach ceann de do chuid riachtanas le chéile.<br/><br/>Le "do chuid riachtanas" a bheith an-bhunúsach toisc nach bhfuil aon rud casta go praiticiúil., Ní dóigh liom go bhfuil "ar ais go saol níos simplí" mar fhreagra. Tá an t-Iompas á dhéanamh (ach ní i ndáiríre, úsáideann siad an córas cúraim sláinte máguaird srl), ach is beag duine eile a thriail fiú. Agus is gnách go mbíonn na cumainn sin nár bhain chompord sochaí nua-aimseartha chun cinn sásta an saol níos simplí a mhalartú ar cheann níos fearr, níos faide.<br/><br/>Sea, sin an méid atá á dhéanamh agam chun a chur in iúl nach raibh geilleagair mhalartacha "borgaire" i gceist leis an imeacht seo.<br/><br/>Go leor insulting do na cultúir sin neamhaird a dhéanamh ar a n-ealaín agus a gcuid eolais.Mholfainn, chuirfeadh go leor daoine a n-ealaín san áireamh, ar go leor bealaí, níos fearr agus bhí eolas acu in ábhair i bhfad níos doimhne agus níos nua-aimseartha ná mar a bhí ag ár gcultúr "nua-aimseartha".<br/><br/>Dearbhaíonn "18 uair an chloig sa lá ar ais saothair" aineolas ar an méid a bhí ar dhaoine i go leor de na cultúir a bheith ag obair chun maireachtáil.<br/><br/>Má cheapann tú go bhfuil an chuid is mó den phláinéid ag stánadh ar soilse flashing agus ag obair 40+ uair sa tseachtain (neamhshásta agus neamhchinnte) a chur chun cinn córas go bhfuil ag milleadh gach rud de luach fíor ar an phláinéid, "chun cinn", ansin beidh orainn aontú ach a easaontas.<br/><br/>Níl airgead ach bealach chun luach coibhneasta rud amháin a thomhas vs duine eile a bhfuil rudaí (nó am) ag iarraidh rudaí (nó am).Is é airgead an rud mícheart chun díriú ar.Tá sé cosúil le haer a fheictear chun drochfhocail a iompar.<br/><br/>Ach ciallaíonn "airgead a fháil" ó dhaoine eile, má leanann tú an smaoineamh ar airgead mar shealbhóir an áitíochta ar luach, "luach a tháirgeadh" do dhaoine eile.<br/><br/>Is é sin: má tá cúram ort faoin tsochaí, beidh tú ag iarraidh rud éigin luach a tháirgeadh do do chomhdhaoine sa tsochaí sin.<br/><br/>Ar ndóigh, nach bhfuil sé chomh simplí sin, agus ar ndóigh faigheann gach córas cluiche agus tá tú imreoirí i ngach áit agus custaiméirí a fháil lured le gealltanais bréagach etc, ach a dhéanann sé ábhar ar scála an gheilleagair féin?<br/><br/>Mar shampla, má fhéachann tú ar na SA le OTI 20 dollar trilliún agus creideann tú go bhfuil daoine cáiliúla ag fáil méideanna ollmhóra airgid dúr agus nach gcuireann siad luach orthu i ndáiríre... Cé mhéad a dhéanann siad "mí-iompar", 10bn in aghaidh na bliana? Is cosúil go bhfuil praghas beag le híoc i gcomparáid leis an bpictiúr mór.Cosúil le cógais a thógáil beo ach chun déileáil le gríos nó constipation ócáideach mar fho-éifeacht, gan an cógas a úsáid mar gheall ar an bhfo-iarmhairt neamh-inmhianaithe seo.Is cosúil go bhfuil sé amaideach.<br/><br/>Ní gá go mbeadh an tsochaí níos fearr ag soláthar luach do dhaoine eile.Is é sin an pointe. Seachas sin ba mhaith linn an sclábhaíocht a cheiliúradh mar dhuine amháin a dhíol le duine eile ach ag insint dóibh "Is maith liom an méid a dhéanann tú" agus luach a sholáthar. Mar sin, is léir go bhfuil sraith iomlán critéar difriúil ann inar féidir seasamh morálta a ghlacadh agus gan luach a sholáthar i dtéarmaí airgead a fháil ach brabús féideartha a íobairt ar mhaithe le leas sóisialta. Go bhfuil sé seo deacair do dhaoine a thuiscint uaireanta is é an fáth go gcuirtear síos orthu mar fhéimheacht morálta.<br/><br/>B’fhéidir go mbeadh siad níos measa ná daoine cáiliúla (cé nach bhfeicim an luach mór a ghintear ann, tá siad ag fáil íoctha as éifeachtacht fógraíochta), ach is codán beag den gheilleagar iad siúd fiú.<br/><br/>Is é an sean-rudaí a dhéanamh ar airgead ná fealsúnacht olc a thagann amach arís?<br/><br/>Dá bhféadfadh gach duine maireachtáil díreach i ngrá agus ar aon dul agus oibriú i dtreo sprioc choiteann.<br/><br/>> Is é an fhadhb atá saint agus tóir ar saibhreas ag cineálacha áirithe daoine a fheiceáil go bhfuil mar cuspóir fiú le haghaidh a saol.<br/><br/>Ní gá go mbainfeadh an fhadhb seo an-mhaith ach nuair a tharlaíonn briseadh an dlí nó nuair a dhíríonn an tóir sin ar thorthaí polaitíochta a athrú.<br/><br/>Chun a bheith rathúil sa tsochaí, ní mór dea-dlí agus forfheidhmiú agus córas polaitiúil cobhsaí a bheith ann.<br/><br/>Is í an fhadhb ná nuair nach bhfuil na rudaí sin ceart, nach 'gréis agus selfishness'. Ag iarraidh a athrú saint agus selfishness nó a leagan síos go mbeidh mar sprioc is gá chun feabhas a chur ar an domhan theipeann i gcónaí.<br/><br/>Go deimhin tá daoine a bhí ag iarraidh airgead a thuilleamh dóibh féin agus a gcuid teaghlach a bheith déanta ar an iomlán méid ollmhór maith ar fud an domhain.<br/><br/>de réir an gheilleagair chaighdeánach, agus gach eacnamaí ó cheart a fhágáil ar chomhaontú ar seo: is é airgead an beart luach don tsochaí.<br/><br/>(D'athraigh mé an focal "filleadh" le "airgead" mar gheall ar ais Ciallaíonn sé go bunúsach brabús agus nuair a dhéanann tú a dhíol nó a cheannach cinneadh, a fhaigheann tú nó a íocann tú an praghas iomlán, ní hamháin go bhfuil do chinneadh sin cinneadh airgid, ní cinneadh ar ais.)<br/><br/>Ní chiallaíonn sé sin an chuid eile den argóint atá á dhéanamh aige (agus tá tú ag creidiúint) go mainneoidh sé tú a bheith trína chéile a shoiléiríonn eacnamaithe i bhfad ó shin.<br/><br/>Tá go leor éifeachtaí ann a dhéanann cinntí daoine a shaobhadh ar nós go mbaineann na daoine mícheart leas as vs na daoine míchearta a íocann, ach déan iarracht é a shocrú agus ansin gan ligean riamh go gcaithfimid gan tuairisceáin a shaothrú, sin nonsense íon.<br/><br/>Is beart luach é airgead, ach tá an caighdeán chun luach a shannadh den chuid is mó mhothúchánach.Ceannaigh rud ar bith ar an miondíola, ansin déan iarracht é a dhíol.Tabharfaidh tú folctha i gcónaí, toisc go gcuireann an t-eispéireas miondíola (stór nó Amazon).<br/><br/>Mar thoradh ar idirghníomhaíocht seo, tá daoine saibhir chun a thabhairt i gcrích go bhfuil siad saibhir mar gheall ar an luach a chuirtear ar fáil fiú níos mó ar an domhan ná mar a choinnigh siad leo féin, ar shlí eile ní bheadh siad íoctha chomh maith sin. An níos saibhre is ea a cheapann siad gur toisc iad na tairbhithe agus go bhfuil aon ghearáin faoi conas a fuair siad a saibhreas díreach éad nó, uh, sóisialachas nó rud éigin.<br/><br/>Uaireanta is amhlaidh a chuir an saibhir rud éigin den luach sin go bhfuil an domhan níos fearr, uaireanta ní. Is féidir le cuideachta drugaí atá ag glacadh druga 40 bliain d'aois as druga paitinne a bhfuil margadh cobhsaí ach seasta acu torthaí scairshealbhóirí a mhéadú tríd an bpraghas 5-fhillte a mhéadú má tá an margadh cúinne curtha ar an druga nideoige seo. Tá sé cinnte nach bhfuil cosúil leis an mbealach sin, fiú má tá siad "luach a chruthú" ag jacking suas an praghas.<br/><br/>Is é an t-imoibriú glúine go leor ná a rá gur chóir dom cuideachta a thosú chun dul san iomaíocht leis an tsamhail praghsála cryptocurrency sin. Ach is é sin naive toisc go bhfuil (a) go leor de na mótaí ann le dul ann agus (b) fiú má iarrann sé air, tá a fhios agam gur féidir leis an gcuideachta creachadóra an praghas a scaoileadh ar an toirt agus a scála agus a áit a úsáid mar cheannaire margaidh chun mo chuideachta snas a threascairt. Mar sin ní dhéanann aon duine é.<br/><br/>Má tá mé in ann a chur i bhfeidhm ar an iomaíocht a ligean ar a rátaí creachadóra do leibhéil nanas, ansin fiú má théann mé as gnó, tá mé fós a bhuaigh., An cuspóir a bhí ann le haghaidh a bheith rátaí réasúnta, gan airgead a dhéanamh. Chonaiceamar é seo le Google Fiber, áit nach raibh na luasanna in-inúsáidte monaplachta in airde coinnithe suas leis an teicneolaíocht.Thosaigh NATO ag tairiscint snáithín, Google Fiber "bhuaigh", i chiall go bhfuil snáithín gigighit Idirlín ag an tomhaltóir anois.<br/><br/>roinnt daoine inmheánaigh smaointe a mbíonn deacrachtaí acu daoine saibhre agus bochta a dhéanamh.Mar sin, ní chaitheann smaointe míchearta le hinmheánaithe ar an airgead atá i gceist.<br/><br/>Is féidir le daoine saibhir inmheánaigh is cuma cad is maith leo, ach an t-airgead a rialaíonn siad iad chun athruithe níos mó ar an tsochaí ná an t-airgead a rialaítear ag daoine le níos lú agus i gcomhréir leis an méid airgid, cibé an bhfuil siad ag infheistiú don todhchaí nó go simplí Tógann sé lá atá inniu ann.<br/><br/>tá sé do-ghlactha cad é an t-airgead a thomhas, agus comhlíonann an rubar an bóthar maidir le conas a thomhaiseann tú do luachanna i conas a leithdháileann tú do chuid acmhainní airgid freisin.<br/><br/>Tá tú ag cur a lán de na aidiachtaí ar rudaí cosúil le "réamhbhreith"; Tá airgead neodrach ina leith seo. Cé mhéad a dhéanann an tsochaí luachmhar cúram sláinte vs bia? Just a mhéad a chaitear. Níl mé ag rá leat cad is féidir a cheapann, tá mé ag insint duit ach cad é airgead.<br/><br/>> An t-inmheánú seo mar thoradh ar dhaoine saibhir a thabhairt i gcrích go bhfuil siad saibhir mar gheall ar an luach a sholáthraítear fiú níos mó ar an domhan ná mar a choinnigh siad dóibh féin, ar shlí eile ní bheadh siad a íocadh chomh maith sin.<br/><br/>Sin ceart, agus sin é an toradh ar mhargaí saor in aisce agus an ardú astounding caighdeán foriomlán maireachtála i dtíortha le margaí saor in aisce.<br/><br/>> An níos saibhre is ea is mó a cheapann siad go bhfuil siad bewares agus aon ghearáin faoi conas a fuair siad a saibhreas ach éad nó, uh, sóisialachas nó rud éigin.<br/><br/>Tá an chuid is mó de na gearáin a fheicim bunaithe ar éad.<br/><br/>> i gceannas ar an margadh ar an druga nideoige<br/><br/>Is cosúil go bhfuil an chuid is mó de sin mar thoradh ar rialacháin FDA rud a fhágann go bhfuil sé an-deacair d’iomaitheoirí.<br/><br/>> de réir an gheilleagair chaighdeánach, agus gach eacnamaí ó cheart a fhágáil ar chomhaontú ar seo: is é airgead an beart luach don tsochaí.<br/><br/>Nílimid teoranta ach do luach a shainiú an-dian ar "luach don tsochaí" is é sin cur suas intological de dúil coibhneasta aonair a thomhas i ndollair.Ar ámharaí an tsaoil, nílimid ag iarraidh luach a shainiú ar an mbealach sin nuair a labhraíonn muid go ginearálta.<br/><br/>in eagar: dá réir sin, bhí alt á léamh agam le déanaí ag iar-eolaithe a dhéanann machnamh ar an bpróiseas casta neamhshuimithe a choinníonn iad sa séipéal. Cuid mhór de is cosúil go bhfuil sé ag ath-shainmhíniú focail choitianta (a dhéanamh de ghnáth iad a dhéanamh níos sainiúla, ach uaireanta ag cruthú sainmhínithe ar fhocail a n-ainmhíniú go sainráite) agus a thugann údar leis seo mar theanga theicniúil (nó buille faoi thuairim mé i nEolaíocht, "focail" de "teicneolaíochta"?) Ansin, múineann siad gurb é foinse an earráide ar fad an diall ó na sainmhínithe sin, agus gur féidir an amhras go léir a leigheas trí dhul idir an abairt a chuir tú in amhras agus sainmhínithe oifigiúla na bhfocal san abairt sin, ar ais agus amach, go dtí go dtuigeann tú go bhfuil an abairt ceart i gcónaí, agus go bhfuil LID ceart.<br/><br/>Is dócha go bhféadfadh sé go gcuirfeadh sé ina luí ar dhuine go bhfuil duine éigin a cheannach uafásach ag dul ar hearóin sráide luachmhar don tsochaí.<br/><br/>" nuair a dúirt mé "is é airgead an beart luach chun na sochaí" in éineacht liom é le luachan a luaitear araon "airgead" agus "luach chun an tsochaí" agus rinne mé amhlaidh a dhéanamh ar an bpointe go raibh OP ag teip pointe a dhéanamh.<br/><br/>más mian leat a chosaint ar an luachan bunaidh, dul ar aghaidh, beidh mé bite ar sin, ach anseo tá tú athrú ach an comhthéacs a aon bhrabús.<br/><br/>luafaidh mé do ráiteas "airgead ach seachfhreastalaí le fóntais" toisc go bhfuil fóntais a dhéanann proxy oiriúnach ar airgead, agus aontaíonn tú leis sin.<br/><br/>Chun leathnú ar mo phointe bunaidh agus ráiteas a rinne mé in áiteanna eile, a dhéanamh níos mó inphlacable do dhaoine ar mian leo cur i gcoinne é: Tá mé ag caint faoi sainmhíniú anseo; Níl mé ag athrú do chuid smaointe faoi saint, nó dáileadh saibhreas nó pá inbhuanaithe. Glac an sainmhíniú a thug mé, agus ansin a rá cad ba mhaith leat faoi na rudaí eile, agus beidh tú a dhéanamh níos ciall, nó a thuiscint cén fáth go bhfuil tú ag teip ar do chaighdeáin féin.<br/><br/>Sílim go bhfuil roinnt rudaí an-soiléire ann. Feabhsú an chomhshaoil, na sláinte, na sábháilteachta, an abhfaíocht oideachais, an fhlaitheachas míleata agus mar sin de.<br/><br/>Mar a chuir Michael Sandel é, ó na 80idí atá againn ar athraíodh a ionad ó gheilleagar margaidh go sochaí margaidh.Ní úsáidtear margaí mar uirlisí ar bith níos mó chun deireadh sóisialta a chur leo, tá siad tar éis deireadh leo féin. Ní chuirimid tomhas rath i bláthú daonna, táimid ag a thomhas sé i ndollair.<br/><br/>Go híorónta, tá cruthú saibhris fadtéarmach i bhfad dodhéanta, chuir an rialtas go leor cuideachtaí i Silicon Valley i gcrích spriocanna straitéiseacha, go háirithe an míleata.<br/><br/>Ní thagann éiceachórais tionsclaíocha amach as an talamh mar gheall ar selfishness agus saint, tá siad cruthaithe ag institiúidí.<br/><br/>Tá ár n-ionadaithe freagrach go teoiriciúil as leasanna sóisialta éagsúla a chothromú, lena n-áirítear leas sóisialta agus airgead.<br/><br/>Ba mhaith liom a shocrú in áit nach bhfuil oifigigh toghcháin cosúil go bhfuil an post sin seachas díreach caitheamh suas mo lámha agus a rá go bhfuil an margadh a fhios is fearr... is cosúil go bhfuil seasamh an-mhór go leor a rá go díreach go bhfuil airgead agus praghsáil an t-aon uirlis sa bhosca.<br/><br/>Um... Aontaím go bhfuil an focal saor in aisce míthreorach ach nuair a bhíonn tú i gcóras dlíthiúil a Comhdhlúthaíonn sclábhaíocht ansin ar ndóigh, bheadh sé ina mhargadh saor in aisce. Tá sé deacair orm an trádáil sclábhaithe a éiríonn gan mhargadh a bheith i bhfad níos saor in aisce ná na daoine a cuireadh iachall air.<br/><br/>Tá margaí morálta. Sin an fáth nár cheart dúinn a bheith ag brath orthu chun fadhbanna sóisialta a réiteach.<br/><br/>Níorbh fhéidir ach a bheith ina gcomhthráth gurb iad na sochaithe saibhre is déanaí sa stair, agus go mbíonn claonadh acu freisin cloí le smaointe “saormhargaí”?
