Mar sin, cé go gcuirfeadh sé deireadh le scoláirí a bhfuil beagán marcanna acu agus go laghdódh sé strus scrúdaithe do roinnt daoine, ní réiteoidh sé na fadhbanna go léir.<br/><br/>Mar sin, in éagmais aon rogha eile sochreidte eile, b'fhéidir go bhfuil rogha beag againn ach chun scrúduithe a choinneáil le déanaí an lá mór nuair a fuair daltaí amach a dtorthaí scrúdaithe agus fuair siad fios cén áit a raibh siad ag dul go deireadh an bhliain seo chugainn. Mar sin, am gnóthach d'ollscoileanna. Agus le huimhreacha síos, agus tá sé seo roimh an titim mór táimid ag súil le bliain seo chugainn a bhuíochas sin do Brexit, am imní d'ollscoileanna. Cé go mbíonn an lucht acadúil inslithe de ghnáth ó iontrála, bíonn muid páirteach le fiosrúcháin ó mhic léinn ionchasacha.Cé acu le haghaidh meabhraíonn siad dom féin conas a oibríonn córas iontrála ollscoileanna na Ríochta Aontaithe (UCAS).<br/><br/>Is é an deacracht a aimsíonn mé le UCAS ná nach bhfuil ach córas róchasta amháin atá aige. Líon isteach foirm iarratais UCAS agus liostaigh na cúrsaí a bhfuil suim agat iontu. Ansin caithfidh tú teagmháil a dhéanamh leis na hollscoileanna a sheolann a gcoinníollacha chugat chun áit a thairiscint duit (i.e. na gráid a theastaíonn uait). D’fhéadfadh roinnt mhaith glaoch ort chun agallamh a dhéanamh ar dtús nó a cheangal ort freastal ar imeacht lae oscailte. Ansin caithfidh tú dhá áit a roghnú, tairiscint phríomha agus cúltaca mar d’áiteanna aontaithe.<br/><br/>Tar éis na torthaí a thagann amach, go hidéalach beidh tú ag deireadh suas le ceachtar do chúrsa is fearr leat nó an cúltaca., Mar sin féin dosheachanta nach bhfuil a lán. Ní raibh siad chomh maith agus a bhí súil acu agus is fearr a mhínítear le linn an imréitigh (a d’eagraigh anord) nó má rinne siad níos fearr ná mar a bhíothas ag súil leis, cuireann siad isteach ar áit níos fearr le tairiscint neamhchoinníollach.<br/><br/>Déan é sin a chur in iúl do chóras CAO na hÉireann, mar shampla. Líonann tú foirm amháin (ar líne) agus liostaíonn tú na cúrsaí ollscoile a bhfuil spéis agat iontu in ord tosaíochta (mar is cuimhin liom, is féidir leat suas le deich gcúrsa a chur síos, ach is blianta beaga ó líon na foirme isteach a bhí sé, mar sin i bhfianaise a drochamhras go léir ar líne gur féidir leat a chur i líon gan teorainn anois...... sin! Ar lá na dtorthaí, téann an ríomhaire trí do liosta in ord agus an chéad uair dhearfach a bhuaileann sé (i.e. tá na gráid éigeantacha íosta agus go leor pointí CAO) atá tú.<br/><br/>Anois agus anois bronnta ar chóras an CAO, níl an córas CAO foirfe, go háirithe nach bhfuil cruthúnas ar leith ann. Glacann sé leis go bhfuil tú déanta do chuid taighde, nach bhfuil tú ach ag cur síos ar chúrsaí a bhfuil suim acu i staidéar agus go bhfuil seans éigin agat dul i ndáiríre orthu (m.sh. mura bhfuil tú ag staidéar ar bhitheolaíocht nach bhfuil tú ag dul chun leigheas a fháil i gColáiste na Tríonóide) agus go bhfuil tú ag cur iad san ord ceart. Ach ar bhealach, cuidíonn sé réasúnta simplí, gaelach agus cuidíonn sé le scoláirí rud éigin a fháil ar a laghad, agus ar an taobh eile, ag cinntiú an oiread áiteanna aontaithe agus is féidir (mar sin ní féidir linn deireadh a chur le teagasc do hallaí léachtaí folamh).<br/><br/>Ar an iomlán, tá mé iachall a thabhairt i gcrích ar an gcúis amháin go bhfuil an córas UCAS chomh casta sin is é a fíorchuspóir ná a chinntiú go gcuirtear an t-iarracht chuig an gColáiste pobail síos an bóthar (ionas gur féidir leo foghlaim conas ba chóir a choinneáil suas an máistir agus an rith). Agus díreach i mbliana seo caite, tá comharthaí soiléire ann faoin gcaoi a bhfuil córas UCAS á imirt, agus ardú géar ar thairiscintí neamhchoinníollach a dhéantar do mhic léinn a chuirtear go maith ar na scoileanna cearta (ionas go bhfaighidh siad áit beag beann ar a gcuid marcanna). Agus tugaim suas seo mar gheall ar a rud a fheiceann tú go leor sa Ríocht Aontaithe. Córais atá ró-bhulcratic, gan iad a dhéanamh níos tapúla, ach is minic go leor a mhalairt.<br/><br/>Ceann de na hargóintí i bhfabhar an éirí amach ab ea go mbeadh an tír ag "rialú" agus go laghdódh sé an t-ualach a bhí ar an AE ar an téip dearg. Bhuel ar dtús ba mhaith liom a mhaíomh go bhfuil téip dearg minic freastal ar cuspóir tábhachtach (mar a chiallaíonn sé mídhleathach chun bréagáin leanaí i ceimiceáin contúirteacha, cosc a chur ar oibrithe a bheith ar an obair agus a chinntiú nach bhfuil tú ag impaled ar an colún stiúrtha i gcás timpiste carr). Agus ar an dara dul síos, is gnách go mbíonn an Ríocht Aontaithe níos mó de lucht leanúna maorlathais agus de théip dearg ná an chuid eile den Eoraip.Dá bhrí sin, bheadh sé réasúnta a bheith ag súil go bhfaighidh an Ríocht Aontaithe níos mó maorlathais nach lú.<br/><br/>Is minic a ghuímid in Éirinn go dtagann na rialacha agus na céimeanna chun cinn. Sa Ríocht Aontaithe tá a cheart mícheart, bíodh sé dlíthiúil nó mídhleathach. In Éirinn téann sé "ah, cinnte go bhfuil tú deontas", ansin "go cúramach anois", "síos leis an gcineál rud" agus ar deireadh "ceart, anois tá tú ag glacadh an phess". Agus is minic a bhíonn an rud céanna i gcodanna eile den Eoraip, go háirithe i ndeisceart na hEorpa. Go deimhin, d’fhéadfaí a mhaíomh gurb é pointe iomlán an Aontais Eorpaigh caighdeáin íosta a chomhaontú maidir leis na nithe a dtugtar "an Ghaeilge as "gan bheith ag cloí le rialacháin an AE". Is léir nach bhfeicfidh aon duine a dhéanfaidh rudaí na Ríochta Aontaithe, tar éis dóibh teacht i dtír ar thurasóirí an Bhéarla (nó na Gearmáine).<br/><br/>Agus caithfidh sé a admháil freisin go bhfuil cúiseanna ann leis an Ríocht Aontaithe a bhfuil go leor dlíthe agus rialacha ann. Tá siad aitheanta le fada go bhfuil an bealach is fearr le páipéir a dhíol ná an dochar a bhaineann le daoine a scanradh (m.sh. scéalta faoi inimircigh, drugaí, coireacht scian, cochaill, sceimhlitheoireacht agus bagairtí a fheictear do leanaí). Is minic a bhrúíonn sé seo ar pholaiteoirí rud a dhéanamh, rud a chiallaíonn go dtéann siad amach roinnt dlíthe droch-mheasa chun na tairbóideachs a bhaint amach ar a dhroim.<br/><br/>Ach is minic a bhíonn an diabhal níos cothroime, ach mar a thugtar faoi deara é, uaireanta bíonn a mhalairt de chás mar thoradh ar rialacha casta, mar shampla eile, glacadh le córas sochair na Ríochta Aontaithe. In Éirinn, cé nach córas foirfe é agus beagán chaotic (mar atá i gcónaí ag déileáil le hoifigeach na hÉireann), níl ach páipéarachas ar a laghad ag tús. Tá obair na hÉireann faoin tuairim go mbeidh obair le fáil laistigh de chúpla seachtain nó míonna agus mar sin níl aon bhéim ann ar chéad ceist a chur agus ciallaíonn sé sin go mbeidh na héilitheoirí á dtástáil go dtí go bhfuil sé soiléir nach bhfuil siad ag dul ar aghaidh le hobair a fháil go tapa.<br/><br/>I gcodarsnacht leis sin sa Ríocht Aontaithe, déanann siad tumfaidh tú trí fonsaí éagsúla agus ciallaíonn sé tairbhí tástála láithreach, le himeacht ama níos mó ar aghaidh.Toisc gurb é am daoine a chur amú agus an mhuinín féin a scriosadh an bealach is fearr chun cabhrú leo post a fháil. Is léir go bhfuil córas na Ríochta Aontaithe bunaithe ar an dóchas a thabharfaidh daoine suas agus imeacht, seachas éileamh a dhéanamh ar na buntáistí a bhfuil siad ina dteideal freisin (rud a théann chun sochair don saibhreas is ndóigh).<br/><br/>Ina theannta sin, contrártha leis an méid a chreidfeá a bheadh sa Phost Laethúil, níl an chuid is mó de na rialacháin ón AE beartaithe mar dheachtú ag brath ar an ard. Is é an sprioc na caighdeáin a shocrú agus daoine a chur ar an treo ceart, le ionchúiseamh nó gníomh cúirte le feiceáil i gcónaí mar rogha dheireanach. Agus de ghnáth, d'fhág sé sin ar stáit na náisiún chun cinneadh a dhéanamh nach bhfuil an tAontas Eorpach ach ag glacadh páirte i gcomhall a ndualgas i ndáil le Stát an AE (nó chun é a chur i dtéarmaí na hÉireann arís "ag glacadh an phaisis").<br/><br/>Agus is é an fhadhb atá ann ar ndóigh go bhfuil an t-oifigeach sa Ríocht Aontaithe i bhfad níos trom.Tar éis an tsaoil, tá mé fós chun éisteacht le duine ar bith ón AE a éileamh ba chóir a ghabháil as cáis a rolladh síos taobh an chnoic. Agus is é an trioblóid leis seo ná go mbíonn claonadh ag baint le téip dhearg níos mó ná cuideachtaí móra a chur i bhfeidhm. Cé gur féidir le hiliomláin mhóra deis a thabhairt roinn dlí iomlán a fhostú chun leanúint ar bharr rudaí, tá go leor de ghnólachtaí beaga na Ríochta Aontaithe gan dóchas maidir le súil a choinneáil ar reachtaíocht. Agus leis an reachtaíocht sin ar tí dul trí na hathruithe is mó i nginiúint, ní chuireann sé go maith le haghaidh gnólachtaí beaga na Ríochta Aontaithe.<br/><br/>Ar an iomlán tá baol ann go dtiocfaidh an Ríocht Aontaithe i bhfad níos mó maorlathais agus níos lú chun bheith ina iar-bhráid cothrom ná an bealach eile timpeall.<br/><br/>Bhí roinnt conspiracies ann a scaiptear maidir leis an nimhiú Skripal. Cé go mbíodh súil agam go dosheachanta ag súil go dtitfidh na Rúise orthu seo, in éineacht lena n-aonar ar dheis ar an taobh clé, chuala mé cúpla ar an taobh clé arís iad (is dócha go bhfuil siad amhrasach faoi rud ar bith a deir an rialtas na laethanta seo), agus mar sin shíl mé gur fiú aghaidh a thabhairt ar an gceist.<br/><br/>D'fhéadfadh muid a dhéanamh anailís mhionsonraithe ar an gcás, ach nach mbeadh a bheith ag obair ach amháin má chreid tú i rudaí cosúil le "fianaise" nó "fít fíricí" agus tá seans ar bith a chreideann nach mbeidh na comhcheilg seo. Mar sin, beidh mé ag dul i muinín na modhanna a úsáidtear go minic ag na póilíní nuair a bhíonn coir á réiteach.Cé a bhfuil an spreagadh is láidre chun an choireacht a dhéanamh?Tosaíonn na póilíní ansin trí iad a imscrúdú, agus 9 n-uaire as 10 gheobhaidh tú amach go raibh siad é.<br/><br/>B'ghníomhaire dúbailte Briotanach é Sergei Skripal laistigh d'fhaisnéis na Rúise. Chun Putin (ex-KGB), tá sé sin náire go domhain agus cúis go leor.Agus tá sé ina fhoirm, tar éis spiairí iar-Speiseanaithe a fháil laistigh den Ríocht Aontaithe roimh, ag baint úsáide as ábhar radaighníomhach. Moladh chomh maith go raibh baint ag Skripal le cruthú an Dossier Cruach mar a thugtar air.Tabharfadh sé seo níos mó ná spreagadh chun é a thiúchan agus iad ag iarraidh na lúba idir an Kremlin agus an Trump a chur i gcliseadh.<br/><br/>Ach deir na teoiricí comhcheilg, cén fáth a úsáid Niviokoto mharú dó? Cén fáth nach reáchtáil ach air, nó shoot dó?, Bhuel mar sin ní bheidh sé soiléir go mba é na Rúiseach a maraíodh! Ba é an pointe iomlán a bhí ag iarraidh a mharú dó sampla de dó a dhéanamh le daoine eile, agus fós a choinneáil ar leibhéal éigin de inneability sochreidte.Cuir é ar an mbealach seo, a ligean ar is dócha go n-oibríonn tú i rialtas na Rúise. Tá tú ag disulised faoi shaol sa Rúis faoi Putin agus tá rochtain agat ar fhaisnéis náire ar Putin (m.sh. leis a phodole... agus níl mé ag caint faoin madra a choimeádann sé rúnda sa Kremlin). An bhfuil níos mó seans agat anois comhoibriú le hintleacht an iarthair nó níos lú?<br/><br/>Ach cén fáth ar tháinig siad air le haghaidh spiairí na Rúise má theastaigh uathu marbh é?Bhuel, tharla an spalp sin faoi Medved ag am nuair a bhí na Rúiseach ag iarraidh a gcaidreamh leis an Iarthar a fheabhsú. Spreagfadh gníomhaire an próiseas sin agus thug sé cuireadh dóibh a bheith ag obair ar ghníomhairí na Rúise thar lear. Cosúil le fir a rinneadh sa mafia, níl riail neamhscríofa san fhaisnéis nach bhfuil tú a mharú gníomhairí an fhear eile, gan cúis mhaith. Má dhéanann tú, ansin a théann sé go dtí an tochtanna am, mar go bhfuil an baol a bheidh an taobh eile a thógáil amach ar cheann de do chuid féin.<br/><br/>Agus, a bheith blunt, ag an am a bhí an US uachtarán (Obama) agus rúnaí stáit (Clinton) a bhí sásta a chur ar líne láidir leis an Rúis... cé go bhfuil siad fuair uachtarán a bhfuil a teanga ding daingean suas Putin's asal!<br/><br/>Anois, ní hé seo a mholadh dom go bhfuil MI6 a bunch na n-aingeal., I bhfad uaidh, níl fós an marc ceist mhór faoi cad a tharla do MI6 gníomhaire Gareth Williams., Mar sin féin, i gcás Skrill níl aon tirgiúil a mharú dó. Go deimhin tá an iarracht seo dúnmharú náire ar son na Ríochta Aontaithe, is é is ciall liom mura féidir leo a gcuid féin a chosaint ar ithir na Ríochta Aontaithe, a d'oibreodh i n-intinn ar dheis do MI6 thar lear.<br/><br/>Déanfaidh teoiricí comhcheilg na Rúise éileamh go raibh oibríocht bhratach bréagach air. Bhuel mar sin fíor, cén bratach bréagach a bhí ann? Má tharla, díreach ina dhiaidh sin, gur ghlac an Ríocht Aontaithe, nó SAM gníomh míleata i gcoinne na Rúise (nó Rúisis go léir) nó i dtíortha na Ríochta Aontaithe agus an Aontais a tharraing na tíortha go léir amach as an cupán domhanda, nó go raibh caidreamh taidhleoireachta acu, ansin ba mhaith liom a bheith beagán níos amhrasach. Ach ina ionad sin, bhí an Poutin go léir gafa rud éigin ionsaitheach ó Thaisteal na Seiceálaithe, Theresa "parto" Sa lá atá inniu ann. Más rud é, tharraing sé aird ar cé chomh lag atá an Ríocht Aontaithe go taidhleoireachta sa ré iar-bhreathnaithe. Más bratach bhréagach a bhí ann, is cosúil go raibh an sprioc chun bheith ag feidhmiú do thrúl na Rúise ar líne ag breathnú dúr.<br/><br/>Is cinnte go bhfuil mórán faoin eachtra seo buartha faoi. Gníomhairí na Rúise atá ag fánaíocht timpeall gníomhaire scriosach tuaithe Shasana gan cúram ar fud an domhain. M6 éalaigh ón albam sin, agus lena gcomhghuaillithe CIA, a bheidh anois le haghaidh díoltas (cad é an geall ar allies Putin anois tá beagán "teactaint".... ní haon ionadh Trump ar eagla sin ar staighre!). Níl a fhios agam a bhfuil níos mó buartha, ar an smaoineamh go ceadaithe Putin a ghníomhairí seo, nó go bhfuil sé caillte a rialú ar an bpointe sa chás ina ndeachaigh siad amach agus rinne sé seo gan dul i gcomhairle leis an Kremlin.<br/><br/>Ach ar bhealach ar bith, ag baint díot féin le teoiricí comhcheilg nach ndéanann neamhaird ar fhíricí an cháis, ná cuidiú leis an gcás. Is é an rud a léiríonn teoiricí den sórt sin conas, tar éis mac tíre a thriail an oiread sin uaireanta san am atá caite, ní chreideann daoine ach rialtais ar bith níos mó, fiú nuair is dócha go bhfuil siad ag insint na fírinne.<br/><br/>Ar ndóigh, de réir mar a tharla, cuireadh tús le seirbhísí poiblí ar bith, in ionad na gcostas a bhain go mór leis an méid cánach comhairle a fháil agus a bhuíochas sin do bhoilsciú. Agus ag an am céanna, tá an rialtas Thoraí ag gearradh an méid airgid a chaitheann sé ar rialtas áitiúil, mar chuid dá bheartas déine.Agus is ar éigean a chuidigh an t-ardú ar boilsciú a thugann an éifeacht Brexit leis. Mar sin fuair na comhairlí brúdh ó iliomad treoracha iad féin.<br/><br/>Níos measa fós, d’fhreagair roinnt comhairlí don ghéarchéim seo trí aon fhoinse ioncaim atá ar fáil dóibh a bhrú, go háirithe gnólachtaí beaga. Dá bhrí sin bhuail roinnt gnólachtaí beaga le mór-ruaigeanna ina rátaí nó ina gcíosanna. Cé go raibh cuid acu in ann déileáil leo siúd (go ginearálta iad siúd i gceantair níos saibhre) nár fillte go simplí (agus gurbh fhearr an titim i gcaiteachas tomhaltóirí i ngeall ar chíoradh gan aon rud acu a dhéanamh). Ar ndóigh, cruthaíonn sé seo dlí chun tuairisceáin a laghdú agus tá na comhairlí tar éis fiach a chur ar an ngéineach órga chun é a dhíothú, agus lár na mbailte a mhilleadh sa phróiseas.<br/><br/>Ar thaobh amháin, toisc go bhfuil go leor de na comhairlí seo Thoraí á reáchtáil (agus go minic i gceantair a vótáil thar a bheith chun Brexit) tá cathú ort a rá, diana a dhéanamh. Vótáil tú chuige seo, fuair tú é, má bhí tú balbh go leor chun vóta a chaitheamh (nach dtugtar an páirtí olc do rud ar bith) go bhfuil do luck féin amach., Mar sin féin, go minic na daoine atá ag mothú an níos measa de seo nach bhfuil na daoine a vótáil chun é. Tá a gcuid buntáistí faoi ghlas ag pinsinéirí triarach agus fálú fáinne.De ghnáth is iad na daoine óga nó iad siúd ar ioncam níos ísle atá ag mothú an ceann is measa de.<br/><br/>Ní gá a thuilleadh a bheith soiléir, d’fhéadfadh an rialtas céim isteach i gcomhair beartán a iarraidh ar chomhairlí poist. Mar sin féin, is é an baol ná go gcuirfeadh siad faoi bhannaí iad uile. Is í an fhírinne ná go bhfuil an chuid is mó de na rialtais áitiúla i Sasana suas sh*t creek agus díreach faoi bhainistiú agus a bheith ar an mbealach sin le tamall maith.<br/><br/>Dá mba rud é go mbíodh muid ag aistriú na bhfiacha sin go léir ar na comhairlí áitiúla chuig leabhair rialtas na Ríochta Aontaithe, is easnamh ollmhór, níos measa ná mar a bhí siad nuair a thosaigh an beartas sin. Agus dosheachanta, rud a chiallaíonn go dtiocfadh brú ar ais i réimsí eile.An bhfuil iompróirí aerárthaí, fomhuireáin, HS2 (nó aon chlaonadh déileáil), nuair nach féidir le comhairlí soilse na sráide a choinneáil ar a dtugtar go litriúil iad? Toisc go bhfuil na comhairlí i dtrioblóid sa deireadh toisc go bhfuil orthu na píosaí a thagann ó bheartas na déine Thoraí a phiocadh suas (m.sh. gearrtar sochar dá cuid, cad a tharlaíonn? Caithfidh an Chomhairle íoc as cóiríocht éigeandála) ach tá modhanna teoranta acu chun airgead a bhailiú chun na costais sin a chlúdach.<br/><br/>Ceist níos tábhachtaí ná conas an fhadhb a réiteach agus ba mhaith liom a mhaíomh go dtéann sé ar ais chuig an gcaoi a ndéantar maoiniú ar chomhairlí. Ní dhéanann córas cánach comhairle na Ríochta Aontaithe ciall ar bith, is ionann an cháin agus tiomáint ag lámhach. Agus níos measa mar gheall ar an méid praghsanna maoine a athraíodh ó rinneadh na rátaí a nuashonrú go deireanach nach raibh baint dáiríre ag ioncam níos mó.Mhínigh mé le fada go mbeadh cáin ioncaim áitiúil ina smaoineamh níos fearr.<br/><br/>Sea, is dócha go gciallódh sé seo iad siúd ar ioncam meánach (cosúil liomsa) a d’fhéadfadh go mbeadh ár gcánacha ag dul suas beagán, agus is cosúil go dtéann siad suas go leor ar ioncam níos airde. Ach ba mhaith liom beagán níos mó a íoc chun seirbhísí poiblí maithe a fháil ná mar a fháil ar chor ar bith. Agus, mar gheall ar an éascaíocht ar féidir cáin na comhairle a sheachaint (ach cuir aon litríocht ón gComhairle sa bhosca bruscair!), tá sé níos mó cánach ar macántacht. Táim amhrasach go mbainfeadh cáin ioncaim áitiúil leo siúd nach n-íocann cáin na comhairle agus b’fhéidir nach mbeadh sí chomh costasach agus a mheastar.<br/><br/>Chomh maith leis sin tá sé an-hipiciúil do pholaiteoirí Westminster a bheith ag cur Brexit chun cinn, chun ligean don Ríocht Aontaithe "rialú" a dhéanamh agus gan airgead a sheoladh chuig an mBruiséil. Ansin, déanann siad a chumhachtaí féin a chosaint go géar agus seasann siad ina n-aonar conas a leithdháiltear airgead, fiú amháin do na comhairlí a chaithfidh íoc as na hiarmhairtí a bhaineann le cinntí a rinneadh in Westminster. Go bunúsach is é an bhéigniú beagán níos mó ná grab cumhachta agus ruathar bainc ag an rialtas.<br/><br/>Má tá aon todhchaí ag an Ríocht Aontaithe i ndiaidh an Bhreatimeachta ní thiocfaidh sí chun cinn ach amháin má tá réabhlóid chuimsitheach ann maidir le cumhacht a bheith ag údaráis áitiúla agus ag tionóil áitiúla (m.sh. rialtas na hAlban). Seachas sin, beidh oidhreacht an rialtais reatha Thoraí mar dhíothú mí-ordúil ar fhormhór na hearnála poiblí agus an rialtais áitiúil sa Ríocht Aontaithe.<br/><br/>Agus parlaimint na Ríochta Aontaithe ag dul as oifig go bunúsach ar ábhar an bháin, chiallaigh sé an smaoineamh 2ú vóta agus tosing an cinneadh ar ais chuig na daoine, is deis anois é sin. Mar sin shíl mé go mbeadh sé fiú an t-ábhar a phlé.<br/><br/>Ciallaíonn Brexit...dac & chaos<br/><br/>Ar an gcéad dul síos, is í an tsaincheist do Theresa May ná nach bhfuil tromlach aici sa pharlaimint chun tacú lena cuid pleananna. Tá thart ar 14 nó mar sin, agus ní vótálfaidh an tAontas Eorpach do rud ar bith a bhfuil a fhios acu go mbeidh an t-aontas custaim i gceist. Ag an am céanna tá 80 MP ar an gcampas crua Brexit nach mbeidh vóta ar bith ann freisin i gcás aon rud a fhágann gur tháinig an Ríocht Aontaithe i gcomharbacht ar an Eoraip ar bhealach ar bith. Ní mór di an DUP chun tacú léi, ach a luaithe a thuigeann siad a neamhbhreithnithe do na bráithreoirí ciallóidh teorainn chrua i dTuaisceart Éireann, is dócha go mbeidh siad ag smeach.<br/><br/>Níl beagnach aon seans ann go bhfaighidh an rogha is fearr le Bealtaine trí na comóntachtaí...<br/><br/>De ghnáth, bheadh sé seo an deis iontach chun cur in aghaidh a chéim i, a chur síos a gcuid billí féin a fhios agam go mbeidh na reibiliúnaithe ar an taobh eile den oileán tacaíocht a thabhairt dó. Ach mar a bheadh saoir eile sna hoibreacha, beidh 4-5 oibrí mar bhreitse pro-chrua MP vóta i gcoinne a bpáirtí féin. Agus cé go bhfuil sé seo go nádúrtha pissing as an chéim pro-iarsmaí den chuid is mó agus baill pháirtí comhad, nach bhfuil Corbyn ag déanamh rud ar bith a reign iad i...go mór mar tá sé chomh maith le Brexit crua, a ní féidir a vóta a thabhairt leo mar gheall ar A) ba mhaith leis a bheith rebelling i gcoinne é féin agus B) bheadh a chuid léinte dearg féin i móiminteam craiceann beo.<br/><br/>... cé go bhfuil an corrlach i gcoinne déileáil stíl Ceanada nó an Iorua a lán níos dlúithe, go leor go mbeadh díreach 4 MP ar flipping an delock a bhriseadh<br/><br/>Má tá tú ag déanamh do shuimeanna agus má thuigimid go bhfuil muid thart ar 7 MP ar ghearr, d'fhéadfadh sé sin a bheith Sinn Fein. D'fhéadfadh siad an de Glas a bhriseadh go héasca, luascadh an Ríocht Aontaithe i dtreo brácacht bog agus teorainn chrua i dTuaisceart Éireann a sheachaint. Agus iad ag déanamh amhlaidh, tá siad ag diúltú freastal ar pharlaimint. Nuair a bhíonn siad ag déanamh amhlaidh, tá an oiread sin páirtithe sa tóir a bhíonn orthu, mar a bhíonn siad i ngéarchéim chomh húsáideach le miúileanna. Nuair a bhíonn an dul diana, a fháil ar na daoine tóir orthu ag dul.... mar atá i bhfeidhm siad sa treo eile!<br/><br/>Bealach amháin, ní féidir leis na búiséad a sheachadadh ar chíor bog nó deacair.Is cosúil anois gur rud maith é na bráititheoirí, mar ciallóidh sé dianchlaonadh de réir réamhshocraithe. Mar a luaigh mé cheana, áfach, is mó an seans go bhfaighfimid rogha an Aontais i ngleic leis an gcúlstop (cás is measa do na búiséadaithe) nó go gcríochnaíonn sé, mar a thugtar "creidmheas dall".<br/><br/>Tá go leor vótaí le bhfeidhm b'fhéidir trí rogha den sórt sin ag an 11ú uair an chloig, cé gur dócha go scoiltfeadh sé a páirtí agus a saothar agus é sin á dhéanamh. I gcás ar bith, bheadh sí ina saghas staide ina bhféadfadh sí aontú le ceann de na roghanna maidir le filleadh ar an bParlaimint (ní mór dóibh vótáil i gcomhaontú malartach a bhí inghlactha ag an Aontas, in éagmais sin, cibé foraithne an PM). Ar ndóigh, ag an bpointe seo, bheadh sé an corraitheoirí ag dul i ngleic le 2ú vóta. Cé go bhfuil an iliomad Brexit ar taifead mar tá reifreann 2nd ar iarraidh (bíodh sé ar théarmaí na Breatimeachta nó gan ach daoine a choinneáil ag vótáil go dtí go bhfuair siad an toradh a bhí orthu, cuimhnigh gur ghlac siad leis ar dtús gur mhaith leo a chailleadh).<br/><br/>É sin ráite, ba mhaith liom a bheith fainiciúil faoi na daoine sin a deir nach dtarlóidh aon chrit phlé.Ní éilíonn aon déileáil go deonach a bheith mar fhásta sa seomra, agus níl aon ráthaíocht ann a tharlóidh. Cé nach bhfuil i bhfad a rá riamh Jeremy Hunt go n-aontaíonn mé leis, is é a rabhadh ar aon déileáil le timpiste deis.<br/><br/>Agus cé go bhfuil muid ar an ábhar, a d'fhéadfadh duine éigin sa champa saoire mhíniú dom conas a d'fhéadfadh duine éigin i gcónaí (roimh an reifreann nó ó shin) labhairt faoi an baol chaos eacnamaíoch i gcás Brexit crua, a eagla tionscadail. Nuair a thosaíonn na teorannaithe ag pleanáil conas an chaos sin a láimhseáil, d'iarr sé ar a chíoradh ordúil a ullmhú. Nó conas a d'fhéadfadh a bheith iachall ar na daoine dul i ngleic le smacht na Fraince agus iad a impigh chun cabhair a fháil!?<br/><br/>Is é cineál ceistiúil an reifrinn roimhe seo an meascán seo a chur leis. I bhformhór na dtíortha Eorpacha mhaith leat a 50% den toghchán iomlán a dhéanamh rud éigin radacach (gan ach 37% den toghcháin, thart ar 27% den daonra fásta... sin an méid a chomhaireamh mar "uacht na ndaoine"). Ní hamháin gur chuir an coimisiún toghcháin deireadh leo, ach cuireadh faoi bhráid na bpóilíní iad chun tuilleadh imscrúdaithe a dhéanamh (rud a d’fhéadfaimis polaiteoirí a ionchúiseamh a fheiceáil).<br/><br/>Ach, táim díograiseach! Rud amháin le machnamh a dhéanamh ar 2ú vóta ná go bhféadfadh an Ríocht Aontaithe a bheith fágtha cheana féin ag an Aontas sula dtarlóidh sí. Mar gheall ar na torthaí a rinne sé, mhol an Coimisiún toghcháin moill 6 mhí ar a laghad orthu stop a chur leis an gcam a bhfuil an t-imeacht saoire i bhfeidhm arís chun vótáil cóir a chinntiú. Mar sin, fiú dá rith an pharlaimint a leithéid nuair a shuíonn sí arís i mí Mheán Fómhair, nach mbeadh dóthain ama ag teastáil, mura leathnaítear an t-alt 50, rud a d'fhéadfadh a bheith, mar stáit áirithe de chuid an Aontais (an Ungáir agus an Iodáil) ag iarraidh an Ríocht Aontaithe an AE a fhágáil (mar sin nuair a léimeann siad d'aidhm chun talamh ar chorp na Ríochta Aontaithe chun an titim a mhaolú).<br/><br/>An cheist<br/><br/>Breithniú tábhachtach a bhfuil ceist a chuirtear. Last am a bhí sé ar bhealach chun deireadh a oscailt., chiallaigh sé go bhféadfadh an campa saoire a dhíol go bhfuil siad císte agus a ithe Fantasy. D’fhéadfaidís gealltanas a thabhairt go litriúil deireadh a chur le saoirse gluaiseachta, stop gach íocaíocht chuig an mBruiséil, fós féin, sa mhargadh aonair (postaí nach bhfuil ag teacht leis) ar fad san abairt chéanna.<br/><br/>Agus ní hé seo amháin go raibh na búiséititheoirí dúr nó corraiceach.Mar atá siad tar éis ligean isteach ina stór iar-reifreann, ba ghníomhaí d’aon ghnó é. D’fhoilsigh SNP (b'fhéidir go bhfuil pleananna ann do Albain neamhspleách, nó do dhaoine eile tar éis dul i mbun staidéir ar neamhspleáchas na hAlban, chun an SPNF a chur chun cinn agus chun díospóireacht ionraic a dhéanamh. Bhí mé ag cuimhneamh ar thacaíocht a thabhairt do neamhspleáchas, ach roghnaíodh roinnt poill ina gcuid moltaí féin. Ach is cuimhin liom freisin a thabhairt chun suntais go fós, de réir mar a bhí pleananna SNanna, go raibh siad níos ciallmhar fós go mbeadh aon rud atá molta ag na bráithreachas na Cairbithe (sin an fáth go mbeadh vóta mór shábháilte ag Albain ó chrúitse crua).<br/><br/>Bhuel d’fhoghlaim an campa saoire ceacht an inyref, mar sin chuaigh siad d’aon ghnó as a mbealach chun an díospóireacht a mharú agus ag díriú ar shaincheisteanna beaga den chuid is mó, amhail iasc, bananaí beitn nó rialacháin AE. Agus gaibhn siad a gcuid talún le haghaidh bhainne agus geallúintí mil, agus a fhios acu go mbeadh an campa eile (agus na meáin) ag streachailt chun dul i ngleic leis an oiread sin bréaga a dúradh i dtréimhse ghearr.<br/><br/>Mar sin bheadh sé riachtanach go bhfágfadh an litir síos tríd an gceist ceart a iarraidh agus a bheith soiléir don phobal go mbeadh iarmhairtí ag a gcinneadh. An dara ceist (agus b’fhéidir an tríú ceist) a bhaineann leis an margadh aonair, saoirse gluaiseachta, custaim, etc.<br/><br/>Mar sin, mar shampla, bheadh sé soiléir (agus ba mhaith liom é a chur ar an bpáipéar ballóide) go bhfuil tú ag fágáil, ciallaíonn sé sin nach saoránach den Aontas tú a thuilleadh, caillfidh tú an ceart ar fad a thugann tú (rud a chiallaíonn níos mó páipéarachas an chéad uair eile a dhéanann tú iarracht taisteal go dtí an tAontas). Ní bheadh aon rochtain ar an margadh aonair i gceist le saorchead isteach a fháil ón gceanglas maidir le tionchar an tionscail agus chuirfeadh sé tús le vótaíocht teorann i dTuaisceart Éireann go huathoibríoch (cuireadh iallach air rogha a dhéanamh idir athaontú nó fanacht san aontas custaim) agus b’fhéidir in Albain freisin.<br/><br/>Molann Justin Greening go mbeadh trí rogha ann, fanacht, Brexit bog nó Brexit crua.Mhinn imní orm faoin vótáil atá fós fágtha a scoilteadh (nó an vóta saoire a chaitheamh go cothrom). Mar sin, ní oibreodh sé ach amháin dá mbeadh cineál éigin córais agat chun an rogha is lú éilimh a dhíchur, ansin na 2ú vóta tosaíochta a aistriú chuig an gceann eile.<br/><br/>Má tá an cheist á leagan ag na bteorantóirí féin, áfach, ní dhéanfaidh siad aon cheann díobh thuas ach níl uathu ach an pobal a shíniú agus ligean dóibh an bus a thiomáint as an aill. Is dócha go bhfágfaidh siad dhá cheist agus déanfaidh siad iad "Ar mhaith leat a bheith ina sclábhaí sa Bhruiséil le hardanna imirceach ag teacht os a chionn chun do phoist a ghoid agus coireacht scian a thabhairt ó Gypsies Rómáinis i gcolainn"? nó "a bhfuil tú ag iarraidh a fheiceáil ar an Impireacht glórmhar na Breataine 2.0 ardú agus a bheith salach ar an cruithneacht, sreabhadh aibhneacha le bainne agus mil, agus airgead bháisteach ón spéir".<br/><br/>An fachtóir Ponzi<br/><br/>Mar sin, ós rud é go bhfuil a fhios againn go raibh an campa fág le brath (tríd a n-iontráil féin), gur cheated siad agus bhris na rialacha, nach bhfuil siad soiléir leid cad atá á dhéanamh acu agus go bhfuil éifeachtaí an bhrúiseachta le feiceáil cheana féin (78% de dhaoine a cheapann go bhfuil an bhreabaíocht ag dul go dona), gur mhaith leat a bheith ina meisce slam don champa atá fágtha. An cás is measa, is beag nárbh fhéidir an vóta a chaitheamh ar chíoradh bog, ach is dóichí nach mbeidh vóta láidir ann fós.<br/><br/>Mar a dúirt Cionnionn Faobhrán, beidh go leor vótóirí saoire ag vótáil arís, mar go díreach trí vótáil, b'fhearr leo a bheith ag admháil dóibh féin agus dá bpiaraí go raibh siad mícheart an uair dheireanach agus ní féidir lena n-ego a ghlacadh ach an bhuail sin. A feiniméan Beidh mé ag glaoch "an fachtóir Ponzi".<br/><br/>Cuid lárnach d’aon mhona ná an marc a fháil chun tiomantas a thabhairt dó go mothúchánach (m.sh. trí ligean dóibh méid beag airgid a dhéanamh go héasca). Go deimhin, tá sé suimiúil a thabhairt faoi deara cé mhéad de theanga na ndrúiseoirí an-chosúil leis an méid a chuala tú ó ealaíontóir con ("creidimh sa Bhreatain" nó "sábháil do bhreabúchadh" etc). Is é an cuspóir anseo dhá fhilleadh. Ar dtús laghdaíonn sé an seans an marc a thacaíonn amach as an con nuair a thosaíonn na scoilteanna dosheachanta le feiceáil. Agus ar an dara dul síos, laghdaíonn sé an seans go mbeidh siad ag rith chun an cops tar éis a bheith teitheadh (toisc go gcreideann siad fós sa con, nó go bhfuil siad ró- náire a thuairisciú dó).<br/><br/>Bhí Charles Ponzi an máistir ar seo. Ag pointe amháin chuir sé cosc ar rith ar a chuideachta trí dhul taobh amuigh agus ag tabhairt amach ceapairí agus caife dóibh siúd atá ag fanacht lena gcuid airgid a tharraingt siar, rud a chuir ina luí ar go leor acu dul abhaile. Fiú nuair a bhí sé ag triail roinnt de na marcanna a chuirfeadh ar a chumas fianaise a thabhairt ina chosaint. Ceapadh go leor crochadh ar a theastas scaire nach raibh sé ach fiach cailleach ina choinne ag an bhunaíocht agus gur mhaith leis a fháil amach as an bpríosún, a dhéanamh ar ais a chuid airgid agus iad a íoc amach.<br/><br/>Mar sin ba mhaith liom a bheith ag súil go mbeidh ar a laghad 40% vóta a chaitheamh i gcruth éigin nó foirm arís.<br/><br/>An feachtas insurgent<br/><br/>Mar sin tá cuma gruama ar an gcuid eile den champa, ráthaítear bloc mór vótaí leis na búiséititheoirí beag is cuma agus éiríonn leo an cheist a chur agus an t-am a chaitear a roghnú (agus beidh am acu a phiocadh a bheidh chomh dóchúil go mbeidh líon íseal vótóirí fágtha ann, m.sh. i lár seachtainí scrúduithe an aonaid). Mar sin féin, tá cúpla fachtóir sna campaí fós i bhfabhar.Ar dtús báire, tá deireadh leis na céadta mílte de vótálaithe saoire, agus tá go leor vótálaithe nua cláraithe ann a d’fhan ar ais anois.<br/><br/>Chomh maith leis sin tá an méid ba mhaith liom glaoch ar an fachtóir insurgent.Rinne a lán daoine a vótáil é sin ar chúis amháin. fuath leo Cameron, an bunú polaitiúil agus a bheartas déine agus theastaigh uaidh an dá shábhail a thabhairt dó. Mar sin féin, agus na mórpháirtithe uile anois ag tacú le saoire (lena n-áirítear Corbyn) a casadh ar a cheann. D'fhéadfadh go mbeadh go leor den bhloc vótála seo ag aistriú go maith chun vótáil, gan ach an rialtas a phaisniú.<br/><br/>Is ar na cúiseanna sin a d’áitigh mé nach mór do fheachtas rathúil fanacht ina bhfeachtas frithbhulaíochta a reáchtáil. Is é an téama is mó ná nach bhfuil leid ag na polaiteoirí atá á dhéanamh acu, tá siad ag úsáid bréideachta mar pheil pholaitiúil chun a ngairmeacha beatha a chur chun cinn agus a bhrú trí chlár oibre nach mbaineann tairbhe ach amháin as an saibhreas. Idir an dá linn tá an tír ag dul i mbun pota, tá na ráillí i praiseach, tá an NHS ar thacaíocht saoil agus tá arántacht ag brú bailte iomlána i mbochtaineacht. Is é an t-aon réiteach ná an clár oibre polaitiúil a bhaint amach ionas go mbeadh iallach ar an bParlaimint díriú ar nithe níos tábhachtaí (a d’fhéadfadh go mbeadh vóta láidir fós ann chun ceist na giniúna a réiteach).<br/><br/>Dá bharr sin mholfainn neamhpholaitiúil a chur i bhfeidhm mar "aghaidh" an fheachtais. Nó b'fhéidir comen mar Marcus Brigstock nó Al Murray (aka an tiarna talún pub.) Cén fáth ar thriail muid a bhuaigh ar fhíricí agus ar fhigiúirí an uair dheireanach, ní raibh an obair sin. Mar sin, ba mhaith liom dul ar ghreann agus aoir a dhéanamh ar na teorannaithe breathnú dúr.<br/><br/>Agus ní bheidh mé i seilbh an Aontais Eorpaigh nuair a ionsaítear an feachtas é féin, ní institiúid foirfe í, ach tá a roghanna eile i bhfad níos measa. An uair dheireanach a leanfadh timpeall an champa saoire, bheadh pointe a thugann an Ghréig nó nádúr neamh-mheicriseach roinnt institiúidí AE ag iarraidh an spleáchas a chosaint. Cé go mbeadh mo fhreagra chomh uafásach sin, ach cé chomh díreach agus atá an tAontas ag imeacht chun é sin a athrú?<br/><br/>D'fhéadfadh go leor buntáistí a bheith ag feachtas rathúil.Ar an gcéad dul síos, ba léir go gciallódh sé go mbeadh an Ríocht Aontaithe fós san Aontas Eorpach. Mar sin féin, is dóigh liom nach mbeidh an iomarca corcra Champagne ag eitilt sa Bhruiséil (físint, ba mhaith leo a bheith in áit lámhaigh na Ríochta Aontaithe!) mar a bheadh siad ar an eolas faoi dul ar ais sa Ríocht Aontaithe níos misned. D’fhéadfadh sé sin a bheith ina réamhtheachtaí d’athrú dáiríre ar an gcaoi a bhfeidhmíonn an tAontas.<br/><br/>Bheadh na hiarmhairtí do pháirtí Thoraí go dona. Is é mo thuairim nach bhféadfadh siad maireachtáil i reifreann 2nd slán, fiú má tá siad bua. Is iad na corrs an páirtí roinnte, leis na lártheist agus leitlithe crua ag cruthú páirtithe ar leith (ar a dtabharfar an páirtí coimeádach fós).D'fhéadfadh an sciathán Breatimeachta crua teacht le chéile ansin le UKIP chun páirtí i bhfad i gceart a chruthú.<br/><br/>D’fhéadfadh an páirtí saothair an deireadh a chur le "breiters muesli" go háirithe, agus ar deireadh is dócha go gcuirfeadh siad i bhfeidhm roinnt páirtí crua ar chlé. Nó, i gcásanna áirithe, Kate Hoey, páirt a ghlacadh UKIP. Bheadh sé seo i ndáiríre chun tairbhe an pháirtí an lucht oibre, tá neart na n-iarrthóirí a d'fhéadfadh a bheith ina lámhaigh maith ag a bheith PM, lena n-áirítear roinnt ó na sciathán ar chlé an pháirtí. Agus leis na Tóraithe i ndúlra, tá an corra saothair a bhuaigh toghchán ina dhiaidh sin ard.<br/><br/>Ar an láimh eile, vótálaigh 2ú lá saoire, go háirithe gur chuir Corby leis an bpáirtí ar ais ina leath. Bheadh na daoine sin ar an ionad ar chlé nó ar na Briotaithe scor, b’fhéidir, ag teacht le chéile leis na hionaid ó pháirtí Thoraí chun ionad a bhunú de réir a chéile (rud a chiallaíonn go mbeadh sé dodhéanta go bunúsach ag rialtas saothair).<br/><br/>An reifreann dearg<br/><br/>Ar ndóigh, ba mhaith le gach ceann de na rudaí thuas a mhíniú cén fáth gur dócha go mbeidh reifreann 2nd, ní go dtí tar éis an bhreab agus an damáiste a bheith déanta. Bheadh sé a bheith ina cluiche bás do na ranganna polaitiúla, a Cluiche de Séasúr an Rí, le comhaireamh comhlacht a mheaitseáil., Ní go dtí go mbeidh capaill fiáin iad a tharraingt ar na vótaíochtaí a bheidh muid a fháil ar reifreann 2ú. Agus maidir le toil na ndaoine, sílim go bhfuil an teachtaireacht ó pholaiteoirí anois is dócha a bheith, chun Boris, fu*k na daoine a chur ar aghaidh., Tar éis an tsaoil, dá mba rud é go raibh an droim ar ais fíor, ní bheadh an reifreann 1ú reifreann ar siúl acu riamh!<br/><br/>Sin é an fáth má tá daoine ag iarraidh 2ú vóta, caithfidh tú do thacaíocht a tharraingt siar ó na príomhpháirtithe. Téigh chuig do MP in iúl dóibh go bhfuil sé ar intinn agat a vóta a chaitheamh riamh le haghaidh a / a páirtí arís, fiú má tá tú i suíomh imeallach, beidh tú ag tacú le páirtí pro 2nd vóta as seo amach (lib des, SNP, Green's). Ach amháin nuair a bheidh go leor MP ar tús a eagla as a gcuid post a bhfuil aon seans ann eile vóta a chaitheamh. |
|  148<br/>8<br/>Making ESA Decisions in the Face of Uncertainty<br/>The previous chapter described our ability to estimate the risk of extinction for populations of organisms.<br/>This is a major part of estimating the degree of endangerment of a population or species. In this chapter, we build<br/>on that information to discuss how important understanding and assessing risk is in ESA decision making and<br/>the question of whether different levels of risk should apply to different decisions. Finally, because decisions<br/>regarding endangered species must always be made in the face of uncertainty regarding estimates of extinction<br/>risk and future events, we suggest ways of improving agency decisions involving risk and uncertainty.<br/>DECISIONS REQUIRED UNDER THE ESA<br/>The objectives of the ESA are to conserve the ecosystems upon which endangered and threatened species<br/>depend, provide a program for the conservation of endangered and threatened species, and achieve the purposes<br/>of several international conservation agreements. While these objectives are not intrinsically or philosophically<br/>conflicting, they can conflict when agencies faced with limited budgets must decide how to apportion funds.<br/>More serious conflicts arise when the objectives of the act conflict with other human objectives, such as private-<br/>property rights and private and public developments. The act specifies the extent to which such conflicting<br/>objectives should be considered when making the different decisions required under the act. Consideration of<br/>human objectives other than those specified in the act is specifically prohibited when making decisions regarding<br/>list 149<br/>ing, take, and jeopardy, but is required when making decisions regarding critical habitat. Initial recovery<br/>planning is to be based solely on scientific considerations, but economic effects of the plan are to be considered<br/>before implementation.<br/>The terminology of the act implies that many decisions regarding conservation of species should consider<br/>estimates of extinction risk. Specific examples of such terminology include the definitions of endangered and<br/>threatened species, the provisions for removing species from the list, and the definitions of jeopardy on public<br/>lands and taking on private lands (see Box 8-1).<br/>THE NEED FOR NEW APPROACHES TO DECISION MAKING<br/>Agency decisions that have been taken under the direction of the act have been criticized by the general<br/>public (Mann and Plummer, 1992) and<br/>BOX 8-1 EXAMPLES OF TERMINOLOGY IN THE ESA (ITALICIZED) THAT IMPLIES AN<br/>ASSESSMENT OF THE DEGREE OF RISK TO A SPECIES<br/>ENDANGERED SPECIES: any species which is in danger of extinction throughout all or a significant<br/>portion of its range.<br/>THREATENED SPECIES: any species which is likely to become an endangered species within the<br/>foreseeable future throughout all or a significant portion of its range.<br/>CRITICAL HABITAT: the specific areas within the geographical area occupied by the species . . . on<br/>which are found those physical or biological features essential to the conservation of the species and which<br/>may require special management considerations or protection.<br/>REMOVAL FROM LIST (RECOVERY): [each recovery plan shall include] objective, measurable<br/>criteria which, when met, would result in a determination, in accordance with the provisions of this section,<br/>that the species be removed from the list.<br/>JEOPARDY ON PUBLIC LANDS: [each federal agency shall ensure that any action authorized,<br/>funded or carried out by such agency] is not likely to jeopardize the continued existence of any endangered<br/>species or threatened species or result in the destruction or adverse modification of habitat of such species<br/>which is determined to be critical.<br/>TAKE ON PRIVATE LANDS: . . . taking will not appreciably reduce the likelihood of the survival and<br/>recovery of the species in the wild 150<br/>the scientific community (Brownell et al., 1989; Goodman, 1993; Wilcove et al., 1993, Tear et al., 1993) for<br/>being arbitrary. For example, Brownell et al. (1989) pointed out that, at least for cetaceans, the list of threatened<br/>and endangered species is not scientifically defensible. Several large whales in little danger of extinction were on<br/>the list in 1989, although many small dolphins and porpoises with very small population sizes were not.<br/>Population size alone is not necessarily a sensitive indicator of extinction risk, but many species had declined to<br/>alarmingly low numbers by the time they were listed. Wilcove et al. (1993) found that the median population<br/>sizes of species when listed were about 1,000 individuals for animals and 120 individuals for plants.<br/>The backlog of candidate species awaiting listing and the number of legal actions taken related to listing<br/>decisions indicate that the current process for making listing decisions needs review and revision. The Fish and<br/>Wildlife Service (FWS) uses three categories to help make such decisions (see Box 8-2). Category 1 now<br/>contains about 400 species for which the agencies have substantial information to support the proposal to list but<br/>do not have sufficient resources to complete the process. Category 2 now contains about 3,500 species for which<br/>a petition to list might be justified but is deemed to be lacking in critical information. The large number of<br/>species in Category 2 indicates that the agencies have not developed a workable process for making listing<br/>decisions when faced with limited data, uncertainty, and limited financial and human resources. Indeed, lack of<br/>scientific information and uncertainty plague many public and private policy decisions.<br/>Recovery plans often fail to provide appropriate guidance on biologically reasonable levels of risk. A recent<br/>survey of recovery plans concluded that goals for species recovery were often unrealistically low and that these<br/>plans frequently manage for extinction rather than survival (Tear et al., 1993). For example, of the 54 threatened<br/>and endangered species for which population size data were available, 15 (28%) had recovery goals set at or<br/>below the existing population size at the time the plan was written (Tear et al., 1993). If the population was<br/>endangered at the time of listing—and the review of Wilcove et al. (1993) suggests that most listed populations<br/>are at risk—then such recovery goals are probably too low.<br/>The committee also notes that some recovered species have not been delisted or upgraded (i.e., from<br/>endangered to threatened) in a timely fashion. For example, eastern Pacific gray whales (Eschrictius robustus )<br/>were not delisted until 1994 (Fed. Reg. 59:31094), although population estimates were approaching the likely<br/>pre-exploitation sizes of 12,000-15,000 by the late 1970s and have continued to increase (Reilly, 1992). The<br/>comments about decisions to list also apply to decisions about delisting 151<br/>BOX 8-2 U.S. FISH AND WILDLIFE SERVICE DEFINITIONS FOR CATEGORIES 1, 2, AND 3,<br/>UNDER THE ENDANGERED SPECIES ACT (FED. REG. 58:51145, SEPT. 30, 1993).<br/>Category 1.—Taxa for which the Service has on file sufficient information on biological vulnerability<br/>and threat(s) to support proposals to list them as endangered or threatened species. Proposed rules have<br/>not yet been issued because this action is precluded at present by other listing activity. Development and<br/>publication of proposed rules on Category 1 taxa are anticipated, however, and the Service encourages<br/>other Federal agencies to give consideration to such taxa in environmental planning.<br/>Category 2.—Taxa for which information now in the possession of the Service indicates that proposing<br/>to list as endangered or threatened is possibly appropriate, but for which sufficient data on biological<br/>vulnerability and threat are not currently available to support proposed rules. The Service emphasizes that<br/>these taxa are not being proposed for listing by this notice, and that there are not current plans for such<br/>proposals unless additional supporting information becomes available. Further biological research and field<br/>study usually will be necessary to ascertain the status of taxa in this category. It is likely that many will be<br/>found not to warrant listing, either because they are not threatened or endangered or because they do not<br/>qualify as species under the definitions in the Act, while others will be found to be in greater danger of<br/>extinction than some taxa in Category 1.<br/>Category 3.—Taxa in Category 3 are not current candidates for listing. Such taxa are further divided<br/>into three subcategories to indicate the reason(s) for their removal from consideration:<br/>Category 3A.—Taxa for which the Service has persuasive evidence of extinction. If rediscovered,<br/>such taxa might acquire high priority for listing. At this time, however, the best available information<br/>indicates that the taxa in this subcategory, or the habitats from which they were known, have been lost.<br/>Category 3B.—Names that, on the basis of current taxonomic understanding (usually as represented<br/>in published revisions and monographs), do not represent distinct taxa meeting the Act's definition of<br/>"species." Such supposed taxa could be reevaluated in the future on the basis of new information.<br/>Category 3C.—Taxa that have proven to be more abundant or widespread than previously believed<br/>and/or those that are not subject to any identifiable threat. If further research or changes in habitat indicate<br/>a significant decline in any of these taxa, they may be reevaluated for possible inclusion in categories 1 or 2 152<br/>PROVIDING OBJECTIVE RISK STANDARDS<br/>The qualitative definitions in the act provide a framework for decision-making, that is, they provide a list of<br/>administrative and management actions and a general rationale for selecting each action. However, qualitative<br/>definitions alone can be interpreted in different ways by different people, and agencies have provided no<br/>guidance on the appropriate degrees of extinction risk for making the different decisions required by the act, such<br/>as listing a species as either threatened or endangered or declaring a species recovered. To ensure that ESA<br/>decisions protect endangered species as they are intended to and do so in a scientifically defensible way requires<br/>objective methods for assessing risk of extinction (see Chapter 7) and for assigning species to categories of<br/>protection according to their risk of extinction. Standards for assigning species to categories should be<br/>quantitative wherever possible, and, when this is not possible, qualitative procedures should at least be<br/>systematic and clearly defined.<br/>Developing Quantitative Risk Standards<br/>Agencies could achieve greater consistency in decisions if they provided quantitative risk standards for such<br/>terms as "endangered" and "threatened" to the many agency personnel involved in implementing the act. Risk<br/>levels should be defined as the probability of extinction within a specific time. As an example of quantitative<br/>guidance, Shaffer (1981) defined a minimum viable population size as that which would have at least a 99%<br/>chance of surviving for 1,000 years. The committee is not endorsing Shaffer's definition but noting that by<br/>providing quantitative guidance, Shaffer has made it possible to discuss or disagree with his definition<br/>objectively and apply the definition in a standard manner. As another example of a quantitative definition, Mace<br/>and Lande (1991) suggested that endangered could be defined as a 20% chance of extinction in 20 years.<br/>Time Frame for Estimating Risk of Extinction<br/>When providing a quantitative standard for assigning risk categories, risk of extinction must be defined with<br/>a specific time frame in mind, i.e., x probability of extinction in y years. Critical levels of extinction risk, which<br/>will trigger ESA decisions, such as listing or delisting, must be associated with a particular number of years or<br/>generations. As the time frame increases, the probability of extinction also increases, approaching 100% for all<br/>species if the period is long enough (see Chapter 2). The choice of a time frame for evaluating risk of extinction<br/>for purposes of the ESA reflects scientific and societal concerns. From a scientific standpoint, time should 153<br/>be long enough to guard against making management choices that might have favorable effects over the short<br/>term (e.g., the next 5 to 10 years), but consequent adverse effects over the longer term (e.g., the next 100 years or<br/>more). An example of such a choice might be management that extends the lifespan of adults currently in a<br/>population (enhancing short-term survival), but jeopardizes successful reproduction (leading to population<br/>decline as the current adult population ages and dies). Species' life-history characteristics help determine an<br/>appropriate time frame, with longer times being more appropriate for longer-lived species.<br/>Another scientific consideration is the time scale for natural cycles of disturbance and regeneration in the<br/>species' habitat. Evaluating the success of endangered species management over only a portion of the natural<br/>habitat cycle runs the risk of confusing natural fluctuations in population size with adverse reactions to<br/>management.<br/>Societal considerations regarding time frame include the desire to preserve species and their habitats over<br/>time scales meaningful to humans and their offspring, political cycles of 2-6 years, economic cycles, and many<br/>others. Because of the large variety of societal factors, this committee cannot specify all the appropriate time<br/>scales that should be considered in decision making or even the range of time scales for which extinction<br/>probabilities should be calculated. However, it is clear that the time scales implicit in the various calculations<br/>should be made explicit for informed decision making. In some cases, where there is an immediate and<br/>potentially reversible threat to species survival (such as a proposed development), it could make sense to analyze<br/>the probability of survival over a short period, perhaps 5 to 10 years or less, when comparing options for species<br/>recovery over the short term. Such an analysis should be followed, however, by another assessment of extinction<br/>risk over a longer period to ensure that short-term gains do not become long-term losses.<br/>Listing Systems Based on Objective Criteria and Rules<br/>A system for making listing decisions requires a set of objective criteria for assigning species to risk<br/>categories, such as endangered or threatened. The objective criteria most suitable for making listing decisions<br/>would be different degrees of extinction risk. However, we rarely have sufficient data to allow good estimates of<br/>extinction risk, so we need a system allowing the use of other criteria as well. Such a system could be based on<br/>objective criteria, such as some combination of population size and number, believed to represent a specific level<br/>of extinction risk.<br/>The need to develop a listing system based on objective criteria has been recognized by the principal<br/>international organization concerned with the listing and conservation of endangered species, the International<br/>Union 154<br/>for the Conservation of Nature (IUCN). In June 1992, the CITES (Convention on the International Trade in<br/>Endangered Species of Flora and Fauna) Standing Committee requested that IUCN help to develop new criteria<br/>for listing species in the appendices to the CITES treaty, which regulates trade in wildlife and wildlife products.<br/>The resulting proposed IUCN system for assessing degree of threat (Mace et al., 1992) was produced after an<br/>international effort by groups of scientists in a series of workshops. The proposed system is now being evaluated<br/>by members of the IUCN/SSC taxon specialist groups, various CITES committees, and interested scientists<br/>(Gnam, 1993) and was considered at the CITES 1994 meeting. At that meeting, a proposal was made to adopt<br/>the IUCN criteria; a counterproposal was made by the United States and a working group developed a<br/>compromise, which was recently accepted by CITES (Rosemarie Gnam, FWS, pers. commun., March 1, 1995).<br/>Much of the substance of the IUCN criteria remains in Annex 5 of the report as definitions, rather than the<br/>criteria for listing.<br/>In the proposed IUCN system, a species could be listed as endangered based on any of several criteria, each<br/>of which was intended to represent approximately the same risk of extinction. Decisions to list a species could be<br/>based on any of the following criteria: probability of extinction, trends in abundance, population size, number of<br/>populations, and geographical extent (Mace et al., 1992). The IUCN system was based on a combination of two<br/>previous approaches for assessing degree of threat: population-based criteria developed by those working with<br/>higher vertebrates and area-habitat criteria developed by those working with plants and invertebrates. Population-<br/>based criteria, known as the ''Mace-Lande criteria," were proposed in 1991 (Mace and Lande, 1991). They<br/>suggested three categories:<br/>Critical: 50% probability of extinction within 5 years or two generations, whichever is longer.<br/>Endangered: 20% probability of extinction within 20 years or 10 generations, whichever is longer.<br/>Vulnerable: 10% probability of extinction within 100 years.<br/>Unfortunately, due to the limited data on most taxa of conservation concern, a formal population viability<br/>analysis to estimate the probability of extinction in a particular case is often impossible. Moreover, population<br/>viability models for plants are poorly developed (Menges, 1990). To address this problem, Mace and Lande<br/>offered several surrogate criteria based on other types of information. For example, they considered that a<br/>population would qualify as endangered if the current population estimate was fewer than 250 mature<br/>individuals, even if insufficient demographic data were available to calculate an estimated probability of<br/>extinction. The current IUCN criteria rely heavily on population viability analysis, popula 155<br/>tion size, and range area. Concern has been expressed that these criteria omit historical data on a species'<br/>abundance and distribution, omit data on reproductive fitness, and ignore life cycles (Gnam, 1993). Furthermore,<br/>the IUCN system is still incomplete, because it lacks a set of rules to allow decisions to be made when there is<br/>uncertainty regarding the criteria used to categorize species according to relative risk of extinction. Nevertheless,<br/>those criteria represent the most important scientific effort to date to reach consensus on standard criteria for<br/>assigning taxa to threat categories in a uniform, objective manner.<br/>The adoption of a similar system in the U.S. would make listing decisions more consistent. Unfortunately,<br/>there is as yet no evidence that the criteria used in the IUCN system do represent comparable degrees of risk.<br/>Any system of criteria developed for use with the ESA should be thoroughly tested against a variety of<br/>population and metapopulation models before implementation. Designing and testing an appropriate system for<br/>listing species is a formidable scientific task best accomplished by an independent scientific committee. For<br/>example, for a much simpler problem, management of the commercial harvest of whales, the Scientific<br/>Committee, of the International Whaling Commission took 6 years to evaluate five alternative proposed<br/>management procedures (Donovan, 1989; R. Brownell, pers. commun., NMFS, 1994).<br/>Limitations on Estimates of Risk<br/>Our ability to estimate risk of extinction for use in assigning species to protection categories is limited by<br/>our understanding of the factors influencing extinction. Two areas where we are acutely aware of limitations are<br/>in models used to estimate risk of extinction and in our understanding of the role of critical thresholds of risk and<br/>of cumulative effects of risk factors.<br/>Limits of Models<br/>As described in Chapter 7, models for estimating risk of extinction are limited in their ability to incorporate<br/>the full complexity of species population dynamics. Estimates of risk derived from these models may reflect only<br/>a subset of the factors actually influencing a species' risk of extinction. As discussed in Chapter 7, it seems likely<br/>that the simplifications and omissions of current models can underestimate risk of extinction.<br/>Poor Understanding of Cumulative Effects and Thresholds<br/>Decisions under the ESA regarding take or jeopardy require making decisions regarding incremental risks<br/>of extinction. Assessing the added 156<br/>risk from specific human actions is usually an even more difficult task than estimating the overall extinction risk<br/>to a species. Individual human actions, such as developing a few acres of habitat, pose small incremental risks of<br/>extinction. At some point, however, the small incremental risks from numerous human actions, if not stemmed,<br/>will accumulate so as to produce a major effect. Not enough is known about cumulative effects and threshold<br/>points to make accurate risk predictions possible (e.g., Beanlands et al., 1986), although there has been some<br/>theoretical work on critical thresholds of habitat patch size or fragmentation (e.g., Lande, 1988 for spotted owl<br/>habitat fragmentation). When considering the probable effects of incremental human activities, it is reasonable to<br/>assume that additional activity means additional risk, but we rarely know whether the relationship between<br/>additional activity and additional risk is linear or whether there might be critical levels of activity above which<br/>the risk of extinction increases dramatically.<br/>Should Different Risk Standards Apply to Different ESA Decisions?<br/>Endangered, Threatened, Recovered<br/>In judging whether different levels of risk should apply to different types of decisions under the act, the<br/>committee considered carefully the terminology of the act shown in Box 8-1. The definition of an endangered<br/>species as one that is already in danger of extinction and a threatened species as one that is likely to become an<br/>endangered species implies that a species listed as endangered is at greater risk of extinction than a threatened<br/>one. The determination that a species should be removed from the list implies that its risk of extinction has<br/>decreased to the point where it is no longer considered threatened. Thus, it is clear that determinations of a single<br/>species as successively endangered, threatened, and recovered should represent a series of decreasing levels of<br/>risk of extinction faced by the species.<br/>Different Taxa<br/>Although cross-species comparisons are complicated by many factors specific to the biology of individual<br/>species, it is appropriate to set the same degree of risk as a standard for listing any species, whether plant or<br/>animal, as endangered and another, somewhat lower degree of risk, for listing any species as threatened. Thus it<br/>is reasonable to expect that species determined to be endangered should, on the average, face a greater risk of<br/>extinction than those determined to be threatened. This seemingly obvious 157<br/>ordering of risk has not always been followed in practice (Wilcove et al., 1993).<br/>Public Versus Private Land<br/>Controversy has arisen over whether the inclusion of habitat destruction or modification as a form of taking<br/>under Section 9 sets a different standard of responsibility for protection of endangered species by private versus<br/>public entities. (We note again that this interpretation of taking is under court review at this writing.) In<br/>particular, it has appeared to some that the standard of responsibility might be interpreted to be more stringent<br/>for private than for public entities. This seems, if anything, the reverse of what was intended by the ESA. From a<br/>scientific perspective, actions resulting in a given degree of risk of extinction are equally hazardous to species<br/>whether they are carried out by public or private entities on public or on private land. The committee sees no<br/>scientific reason for setting different standards for categorizing risk of extinction under different sections of the<br/>act. However, because public and private entities behave differently, achieving the same degree of biological<br/>protection on public versus private lands does not necessarily imply identical regulatory requirements on behalf<br/>of species experiencing the same risk of extinction on public and on private lands.<br/>USING STRUCTURED APPROACHES TO DECISION MAKING<br/>Why use structured approaches to ESA decision making? Because the issues are complex and relevant<br/>scientific data are often fragmentary, it can be difficult to make decisions regarding endangered or threatened<br/>species. Decisions regarding endangered species are often characterized by insufficient data, probabilistic<br/>predictions regarding future events, considerable uncertainty regarding the accuracy of these predictions,<br/>conflicting management objectives, disagreement over the best course of action, and the need to justify whatever<br/>decision is made.<br/>Given these problems, it is important that we try to make our decision-making process as explicit as<br/>possible, especially because research on the psychology of human decision making (Hogarth, 1980; Kahneman<br/>et al., 1982; von Winterfeldt and Edwards, 1986) has shown that intuitive decisions exhibit many inconsistencies<br/>and biases, particularly when probabilistic information and multiple objectives are involved. The use of an<br/>explicit framework to guide decisions can help us make choices that are consistent with our goals, data, and<br/>beliefs and facilitate compromise among those with differing views. Several techniques developed in the fields<br/>of operations research and management science—the academic disciplines dealing with scientific approaches to<br/>decision making—provide helpful frameworks 158<br/>for making these difficult decisions. These techniques help decision makers think about the decision in a<br/>systematic way; break down difficult decisions into a series of smaller, easier decisions; and document the<br/>process used to reach the decision, which makes the decision easier to justify and defend.<br/>Some applications of these structured approaches, particularly the qualitative ones, might appear to be<br/>simple pleas for clear thinking, and they are. But clear thinking does not come easily or naturally in the face of<br/>scientific complexity and uncertainty, competing objectives within recovery programs and beyond, and political<br/>pressures from multiple constituencies. We propose decision analysis as an example of a structured problem-<br/>solving method, although other methods (such as the Analytic Hierarchy Process (Saaty, 1990)) or approaches<br/>based on goal programming and multiattribute ranking (Ralls and Starfield, 1995) can be useful as well. We<br/>stress the merits of using these tools as conceptual frameworks, not just as number-crunching devices. And we<br/>emphasize that using these approaches is not necessarily a call for more information, but rather for more<br/>coherent use of existing information.<br/>Using Subjective Probability and Expert Opinion<br/>In some cases, there is very little "hard" information that seems relevant to estimating the risks affecting<br/>endangered species, but some experts have accumulated experience that allows them to make informed<br/>judgments about these risks. Such expert judgment is so often available for endangered species decisions that it<br/>is of great benefit to have orderly methods of eliciting and using it for decision making. One of the strengths of<br/>decision analysis is its ability to estimate "subjective probabilities" and then use them for analysis in the same<br/>way that long-term frequency estimates would be used (Behn and Vaupel, 1982; von Winterfeldt and Edwards,<br/>1986; Maguire, 1987). The methods allow a concrete expression of expert opinion, facilitating scrutiny by the<br/>public and comparison with other views.<br/>Another place where expert opinion can be essential is in cases when some background information is<br/>available, but unique aspects of a situation differentiate it from similar situations that have occurred. An example<br/>might be assessing the likelihood of successful reproduction in a captive population where experience has been<br/>dismal but new reproductive technologies have been developed that might prove helpful, as for example with the<br/>endangered Hawaiian crow (NRC, 1992a). In this case, it is relevant to amend or update the historical<br/>information in light of the new techniques. There are formal ways to combine old data with new opinion (and<br/>new data with old opinion) using Bayesian estimation (Raiffa, 1968; Clemen, 1991 159<br/>Linking Science and Values<br/>Even though estimates of risk are grounded in scientific information, those implementing the act often make<br/>value judgments when making decisions about listing, jeopardy, etc. They are deciding which species to list<br/>quickly and which to relegate to delayed listing, which areas of habitat are worth the socioeconomic cost or<br/>political effort to designate as critical and which are not, what degree of jeopardy is worth altering a federally<br/>funded development project for and what is not. Many citizens are willing to allow public officials to make such<br/>judgments on their behalf, but those involved might be more comfortable if the values informing those<br/>judgments and their effects on ESA decisions were articulated more clearly.<br/>A hallmark of formal decision analysis (Behn and Vaupel, 1982; Clemen, 1991) and other structured<br/>problem-solving methods (Ralls and Starfield, 1995) is an emphasis on articulating clearly the objectives for a<br/>decision and criteria for evaluating how well alternative proposals might meet those objectives. Use of such<br/>methods can improve ESA decisions by making the connection between values, objectives, and decisions more<br/>transparent, helping to disarm criticisms that the government is capricious or partisan in implementing the act<br/>(Mann and Plummer, 1992; Tear et al., 1993).<br/>Making good use of science, as instructed in the ESA, requires making appropriate connections between the<br/>values and objectives being pursued in a decision and the scientific evidence and reasoning used to evaluate<br/>alternative ways of meeting those objectives. Science by itself is not sufficient input to policy decisions, apart<br/>from the objectives and values it serves. Articulating an explicit framework can help link science and values and<br/>lead to better and more defensible decisions.<br/>Scientific Uncertainty in ESA Decisions<br/>For even the best-studied endangered species, essential pieces of information might be lacking, yet<br/>decisions must be made. Sometimes, it is possible to delay action while gathering better information, although<br/>that strategy carries its own risks. Sometimes, important factors affecting how management actions turn out, such<br/>as catastrophic weather conditions or pollution accidents, are inherently uncertain, and no amount of further<br/>study could do more than improve the accuracy and precision of estimates of their likelihoods. In any case,<br/>weighing the best choice under uncertainty about outcomes is a necessity. This kind of probabilistic reasoning<br/>does not come naturally and many managers are uncomfortable with it, resorting to shortcut heuristics to<br/>simplify information and justify their choices (von Winterfeldt and Edwards, 1986). The framework of decision<br/>analysis offers a structure 160<br/>for considering probabilistic information in a coherent and consistent way, providing better use of whatever<br/>information is available to guide decisions.<br/>Estimating Uncertain Quantities<br/>Several types of information bearing on ESA decisions can be uncertain, including factors influencing how<br/>management actions turn out (such as weather events or sociopolitical events) and measures of outcome (such as<br/>the likelihood of population persistence under a particular management strategy). Such probabilistic quantities<br/>enter the analysis in different ways, but in both cases, methods of estimating those quantities are needed.<br/>Sometimes, relevant long-term data on the frequency of an uncertain event can be used to estimate its<br/>probability; as an example, there are weather records for severe storms in particular coastal areas. These could be<br/>used to estimate the likelihood of a tropical storm striking the Gulf Coast of Texas near the wintering ground for<br/>the migratory whooping crane population or the probability that a hurricane would destroy the habitat of some<br/>red-cockaded woodpeckers. In one case, population models were used to help decisions concerning conservation<br/>of endangered sea turtles by identifying the most sensitive life stages (NRC, 1992b).<br/>In other cases, no useful long-term data are available, but there might be models incorporating our best<br/>understanding of the factors affecting the likelihood of an uncertain event. Examples are stochastic population<br/>models, such as are discussed in Chapter 7. Those use information about demographic parameters and<br/>environmental factors to predict probability of extinction for a population with particular characteristics.<br/>Reducing Uncertainty by Gathering Information<br/>Listing actions, recovery plans, or other ESA decisions often are delayed due to inadequate information.<br/>Those implementing the act almost always believe that with additional information, they could make a better<br/>decision. Nevertheless, decisions to delay action pending further information and directives to gather additional<br/>information should be viewed critically. What kind of information and of what quality could be gathered within<br/>the time and resources available? What are the possible answers that such investigation might reveal? What<br/>decisions would be triggered by different answers? How are those decisions different from those that would be<br/>made using existing information? What effect will continuing the status quo have on species status and on<br/>options for future action? Considering these questions in a structured framework can make it more likely that a<br/>reasonable decision will be made.<br/>An example of such an analysis comes from Bart and Robson (in press 161<br/>They analyzed the variability in raptor population data to find out how many years of data would be<br/>required to tell whether the northern spotted owl population was stable or increasing, a criterion for delisting<br/>specified in the spotted owl recovery plan. Their analysis showed that it would not be possible to render a sound<br/>judgment about delisting based on fewer than 8 years of population monitoring data. (See Box 8-3 on statistical<br/>power and types of errors.) Such an analysis promotes realistic expectations about the time and effort required to<br/>obtain a satisfactory answer and forestalls charges that the FWS is delaying delisting for nonscientific reasons.<br/>Sometimes a qualitative but orderly consideration of questions is sufficient to guide action, giving managers<br/>the confidence either to pursue additional information or proceed on the basis of the information they already<br/>have. At other times, a more formal analysis of the value of information (Raiffa, 1968; Clemen, 1991) might be<br/>needed. In either case, the scientific uncertainty must be examined within the context set by the objectives for a<br/>particular situation. The question of how many years of monitoring data would be required to make a decision<br/>about delisting the northern spotted owl can be answered only with reference to the level of confidence required,<br/>which can be determined only with reference to the objectives of spotted owl management (Taylor and<br/>Gerrodette, 1993).<br/>More information almost always seems better to those trained as cautious natural resource scientists. Yet,<br/>too much risk aversion, or fear of making the wrong decision based on limited information, can be crippling. The<br/>California condor and the black-footed ferret are good examples. In these cases, and in many similar ones<br/>worldwide, a management decision to remove individuals from the wild to begin or to augment a captive-<br/>breeding effort would have an indisputably negative effect on the survival of the population in the wild<br/>(Maguire, 1986; 1989). Such a decision must be justified in terms of the likelihood of extinction of the<br/>population even if no removals were made, and the long-term benefit to species survival from a captive breeding<br/>program, if successful. In the condor and ferret programs, only when it became clear that a continuation of status-<br/>quo management of the wild population would lead to disaster did the negative consequences of removals<br/>become acceptable. But by that point, it was very nearly too late to make a success of the captive-breeding<br/>programs (Ralls and Ballou, 1992). Although both programs were ultimately successful in producing animals for<br/>reintroduction, neither case can be cited as a model of informed decision making under uncertainty. In both<br/>cases, too much attention was given to the possible negative consequences of the novel strategy (capturing<br/>animals from the wild) and too little to the possible negative consequences of continuing the conservative<br/>approach (trying to protect the dwindling wild populations 162<br/>BOX 8-3 STATISTICAL POWER AND TYPES OF ERRORS<br/>Statistical tests are used to control the likelihood of drawing erroneous conclusions about scientific<br/>hypotheses from data. The null hypothesis usually states that there is no difference between two or more)<br/>populations with regard to some characteristic in which we are interested. In contrast, the alternative<br/>hypothesis states that there is a difference in that particular characteristic.<br/>The alternative hypothesis is the more important one from the practical point of view because we<br/>would be likely to take some management action if it were true. However, the rules of statistics require that<br/>we test the null hypothesis rather than the alternative hypothesis. in other words, we make an "argument by<br/>contradiction:" we assume that the null hypothesis is true and then see if the data enable us to conclude<br/>that it is actually highly likely to be false. If so, we conclude that the alternative hypothesis is likely to be<br/>true. On the other hand, if we cannot show that the null hypothesis is likely to be false, we conclude that the<br/>alternative hypothesis is unlikely to be true.<br/>Because statistical tests merely allow us to estimate the probability that a hypothesis is true given a<br/>particular set of observations, two types of mistakes are possible. The first possible mistake is that we<br/>might conclude that the null hypothesis is false when in fact it is true. This is called rejecting a true null<br/>hypothesis or making a Type 1 error. The second possible mistake is that we might conclude that the null<br/>hypothesis is true when in fact it is false. This is called accepting a false null hypothesis or making a Type 2<br/>error.<br/>A specific example may be helpful. Suppose we would like to know whether or not the population of an<br/>endangered species is smaller in 1990 than it was in 1980. There would be no need for a statistical<br/>analysis if we could count every individual: if the number counted in 1990 was smaller than the number<br/>counted in 1980 we could conclude with certainty that the population had decreased over this time period.<br/>The need for statistics arises because we cannot count every individual; we can only conduct surveys and<br/>make estimates of population size. These estimates might be very precise, that is we might have a low<br/>degree of uncertainty about them. Usually, however, we are only able to make somewhat imprecise<br/>estimates about which we have a considerable degree of uncertainty. Regardless of the degree of precision<br/>of our estimates, we must consider a probability distribution of possible population sizes in 1990 and<br/>another probability distribution of population sizes in 1980 (Figure 8-1a for a more precise estimate and<br/>Figure 8-1b for a less precise estimate).<br/>The null hypothesis is that there is no difference between the population size in 1980 and that in 1990.<br/>The alternative hypothesis is that the population size in 1990 is smaller than the population size in 1980.<br/>We test the null hypothesis that there is no difference between the population sizes in 1980 and in 1990.<br/>Three things determine our ability to say whether the 1990 population is smaller than the 1980<br/>population: the magnitude of the difference between our two population estimates; the precision of our<br/>population estimates; and the risk we are willing to take that we falsely conclude the 1990 population is<br/>smaller. The situation with very precise population estimates is shown in 163<br/>Figure 8-1a. Looking at the distribution of population estimates for 1980, we see that 1,000 is the most<br/>likely population size but that there is some probability of many other population sizes, some of which<br/>overlap with the range of possible population sizes for 1990. The scientist must decide what estimate for<br/>1990 is sufficiently unlikely to be part of the 1980 distribution to warrant the conclusion that the two are<br/>really different.<br/>Scientists are usually cautious about such decisions. If the efficacy of a drug were being tested, it<br/>would not contribute to scientific progress if scientists often said a drug did have an effect when it did not.<br/>For this reason, the standard risk taken of falsely rejecting the null hypothesis is 1 in 20 (usually phrased<br/>). In our population example, that means we would reject the null hypothesis for any 1990<br/>population estimate less than the value shown by the dark vertical bar in Figure 8-1a. The area of the 1980<br/>distribution smaller than that value is 5% of the distribution (1/20th).<br/>Now consider our 1990 estimate. The most likely population size is 800. We want to know if the 1990<br/>population is smaller: has our endangered species continued to decline?
